16	/-!
17	# The sheafification functor for presheaves of modules
18	
19	In this file, we construct a functor
20	`PresheafOfModules.sheafification α : PresheafOfModules R₀ ⥤ SheafOfModules R`
21	for a locally bijective morphism `α : R₀ ⟶ R.val` where `R₀` is a presheaf of rings
22	and `R` a sheaf of rings.
23	In particular, if `α` is the identity of `R.val`, we obtain the
24	sheafification functor `PresheafOfModules R.val ⥤ SheafOfModules R`.
25	
26	-/
27	
28	@[expose] public section
29	
30	universe v v' u u'
31	
32	open CategoryTheory Category Limits
33	
34	variable {C : Type u'} [Category.{v'} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
35	  {R₀ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {R : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}} (α : R₀ ⟶ R.obj)
36	  [Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J α] [Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J α]
37	  [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{v}]
38	
39	namespace PresheafOfModules
40	
41	instance : (SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{v} R).ReflectsIsomorphisms :=
42	  have : (SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{v} R ⋙ sheafToPresheaf _ _).ReflectsIsomorphisms :=
43	    inferInstanceAs (SheafOfModules.forget.{v} R ⋙ toPresheaf _).ReflectsIsomorphisms
44	  reflectsIsomorphisms_of_comp _ (sheafToPresheaf _ _)
45	
46	section
47	
48	variable [HasWeakSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{v}]
49	
50	/-- Given a locally bijective morphism `α : R₀ ⟶ R.val` where `R₀` is a presheaf of rings
51	and `R` a sheaf of rings (i.e. `R` identifies to the sheafification of `R₀`), this is
52	the associated sheaf of modules functor `PresheafOfModules.{v} R₀ ⥤ SheafOfModules.{v} R`. -/
53	@[simps! -isSimp map]
54	noncomputable def sheafification : PresheafOfModules.{v} R₀ ⥤ SheafOfModules.{v} R where
55	  obj M₀ := sheafify α (CategoryTheory.toSheafify J M₀.presheaf)
56	  map f := sheafifyMap _ _ _ f
57	    ((toPresheaf R₀ ⋙ presheafToSheaf J AddCommGrpCat).map f)
58	      (by apply toSheafify_naturality)
59	  map_id M₀ := by
60	    ext1
61	    apply (toPresheaf _).map_injective
62	    simp
63	    rfl
64	  map_comp _ _ := by
65	    ext1
66	    apply (toPresheaf _).map_injective
67	    simp
68	    rfl
69	
70	/-- The sheafification of presheaves of modules commutes with the functor which
71	forgets the module structures. -/
72	noncomputable def sheafificationCompToSheaf :
73	    sheafification.{v} α ⋙ SheafOfModules.toSheaf _ ≅
74	      toPresheaf _ ⋙ presheafToSheaf J AddCommGrpCat :=
75	  Iso.refl _
76	
77	/-- The sheafification of presheaves of modules commutes with the functor which
78	forgets the module structures. -/
79	noncomputable def sheafificationCompForgetCompToPresheaf :
80	    sheafification.{v} α ⋙ SheafOfModules.forget _ ⋙ toPresheaf _ ≅
81	      toPresheaf _ ⋙ presheafToSheaf J AddCommGrpCat ⋙ sheafToPresheaf J AddCommGrpCat :=
82	  Iso.refl _
83	
84	/-- The bijection between types of morphisms which is part of the adjunction
85	`sheafificationAdjunction`. -/
86	noncomputable def sheafificationHomEquiv
87	    {P : PresheafOfModules.{v} R₀} {F : SheafOfModules.{v} R} :
88	    ((sheafification α).obj P ⟶ F) ≃
89	      (P ⟶ (restrictScalars α).obj ((SheafOfModules.forget _).obj F)) := by
90	  apply sheafifyHomEquiv
91	
92	lemma toPresheaf_map_sheafificationHomEquiv_def
93	    {P : PresheafOfModules.{v} R₀} {F : SheafOfModules.{v} R}
94	    (f : (sheafification α).obj P ⟶ F) :
95	    (toPresheaf R₀).map (sheafificationHomEquiv α f) =
96	      CategoryTheory.toSheafify J P.presheaf ≫ (toPresheaf R.obj).map f.val := rfl
97	
98	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
99	lemma toPresheaf_map_sheafificationHomEquiv
100	    {P : PresheafOfModules.{v} R₀} {F : SheafOfModules.{v} R}
101	    (f : (sheafification α).obj P ⟶ F) :
102	    (toPresheaf R₀).map (sheafificationHomEquiv α f) =
103	      (sheafificationAdjunction J AddCommGrpCat).homEquiv P.presheaf
104	        ((SheafOfModules.toSheaf _).obj F) ((SheafOfModules.toSheaf _).map f) := by
105	  rw [toPresheaf_map_sheafificationHomEquiv_def, Adjunction.homEquiv_unit]
106	  dsimp
107	
108	lemma toSheaf_map_sheafificationHomEquiv_symm
109	    {P : PresheafOfModules.{v} R₀} {F : SheafOfModules.{v} R}
110	    (g : P ⟶ (restrictScalars α).obj ((SheafOfModules.forget _).obj F)) :
111	    (SheafOfModules.toSheaf _).map ((sheafificationHomEquiv α).symm g) =
112	      (((sheafificationAdjunction J AddCommGrpCat).homEquiv
113	        P.presheaf ((SheafOfModules.toSheaf R).obj F)).symm ((toPresheaf R₀).map g)) := by
114	  obtain ⟨f, rfl⟩ := (sheafificationHomEquiv α).surjective g
115	  apply ((sheafificationAdjunction J AddCommGrpCat).homEquiv _ _).injective
116	  rw [Equiv.apply_symm_apply, Adjunction.homEquiv_unit, Equiv.symm_apply_apply]
117	  rfl
118	
119	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
120	/-- Given a locally bijective morphism `α : R₀ ⟶ R.val` where `R₀` is a presheaf of rings
121	and `R` a sheaf of rings, this is the adjunction
122	`sheafification.{v} α ⊣ SheafOfModules.forget R ⋙ restrictScalars α`. -/
123	noncomputable def sheafificationAdjunction :
124	    sheafification.{v} α ⊣ SheafOfModules.forget R ⋙ restrictScalars α :=
125	  Adjunction.mkOfHomEquiv
126	    { homEquiv := fun _ _ ↦ sheafificationHomEquiv α
127	      homEquiv_naturality_left_symm := fun {P₀ Q₀ N} f g ↦ by
128	        apply (SheafOfModules.toSheaf _).map_injective
129	        rw [Functor.map_comp]
130	        erw [toSheaf_map_sheafificationHomEquiv_symm,
131	          toSheaf_map_sheafificationHomEquiv_symm α g]
132	        rw [Functor.map_comp]
133	        apply (CategoryTheory.sheafificationAdjunction J
134	          AddCommGrpCat.{v}).homEquiv_naturality_left_symm
135	      homEquiv_naturality_right := fun {P₀ M N} f g ↦ by
136	        apply (toPresheaf _).map_injective
137	        erw [toPresheaf_map_sheafificationHomEquiv] }
138	
139	lemma sheafificationAdjunction_homEquiv_apply {P : PresheafOfModules.{v} R₀}
140	    {F : SheafOfModules.{v} R} (f : (sheafification α).obj P ⟶ F) :
141	    (sheafificationAdjunction α).homEquiv P F f = sheafificationHomEquiv α f := rfl
142	
143	@[simp]
144	lemma toPresheaf_map_sheafificationAdjunction_unit_app (M₀ : PresheafOfModules.{v} R₀) :
145	    (toPresheaf _).map ((sheafificationAdjunction α).unit.app M₀) =
146	      CategoryTheory.toSheafify J M₀.presheaf := rfl
147	
148	@[simp]
149	lemma toSheaf_map_sheafificationAdjunction_counit_app (M : SheafOfModules.{v} R) :
150	    (SheafOfModules.toSheaf R).map ((sheafificationAdjunction α).counit.app M) =
151	      (CategoryTheory.sheafificationAdjunction J
152	          AddCommGrpCat.{v}).counit.app ((SheafOfModules.toSheaf R).obj M) :=
153	  (toSheaf_map_sheafificationHomEquiv_symm _ _).trans
154	    (by rw [← Adjunction.homEquiv_symm_id]; rfl)
155	
156	instance : (sheafification.{v} α).IsLeftAdjoint :=
157	  (sheafificationAdjunction α).isLeftAdjoint
158	
159	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
160	instance : IsIso (sheafificationAdjunction α).counit := by
161	  rw [NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app]
162	  intro F
163	  rw [← isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso _ (SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{v} R)]
164	  simp only [Functor.comp_obj, SheafOfModules.forget_obj, restrictScalars_obj, Functor.id_obj,
165	    toSheaf_map_sheafificationAdjunction_counit_app]
166	  infer_instance
167	
168	instance : (SheafOfModules.forget.{v} R ⋙ restrictScalars α).Full :=
169	  (sheafificationAdjunction.{v} α).fullyFaithfulROfIsIsoCounit.full
170	
171	instance : (SheafOfModules.forget.{v} R ⋙ restrictScalars α).Faithful :=
172	  (sheafificationAdjunction.{v} α).fullyFaithfulROfIsIsoCounit.faithful
173	
174	end
175	
176	section
177	
178	variable [HasSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{v}]
179	
180	noncomputable instance :
181	    PreservesFiniteLimits (sheafification.{v} α ⋙ SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{v} R) :=
182	  comp_preservesFiniteLimits (toPresheaf.{v} R₀) (presheafToSheaf J AddCommGrpCat)
183	
184	instance : (SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{v} R).ReflectsIsomorphisms :=
185	  reflectsIsomorphisms_of_comp (SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{v} R) (sheafToPresheaf J _)
186	
187	noncomputable instance : ReflectsFiniteLimits (SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{v} R) where
188	  reflects _ _ _ := inferInstance
189	
190	noncomputable instance : PreservesFiniteLimits (sheafification.{v} α) :=
191	  preservesFiniteLimits_of_reflects_of_preserves
192	    (sheafification.{v} α) (SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{v} R)
193	
194	end
195	
196	end PresheafOfModules

4	Authors: Dagur Asgeirsson, Jack McKoen, Joël Riou

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Colimits
9	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Monoidal.Closed
10	
11	/-!
12	# The monoidal category structure on presheaves of modules
13	
14	Given a presheaf of commutative rings `R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat`, we construct
15	the monoidal category structure on the category of presheaves of modules
16	`PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)`. The tensor product `M₁ ⊗ M₂` is defined
17	as the presheaf of modules which sends `X : Cᵒᵖ` to `M₁.obj X ⊗ M₂.obj X`.
18	
19	## Notes
20	
21	This contribution was created as part of the AIM workshop
22	"Formalizing algebraic geometry" in June 2024.
23	
24	-/
25	
26	@[expose] public section
27	
28	open CategoryTheory MonoidalCategory BraidedCategory Category Limits
29	
30	universe v u v₁ u₁
31	
32	variable {C : Type*} [Category* C] {R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
33	
34	instance (X : Cᵒᵖ) : CommRing ((R ⋙ forget₂ _ RingCat).obj X) :=
35	  inferInstanceAs (CommRing (R.obj X))
36	
37	namespace PresheafOfModules
38	
39	namespace Monoidal
40	
41	variable (M₁ M₂ M₃ M₄ : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
42	
43	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
44	/-- Auxiliary definition for `tensorObj`. -/
45	noncomputable def tensorObjMap {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y) : M₁.obj X ⊗ M₂.obj X ⟶
46	    (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (R.map f).hom).obj (M₁.obj Y ⊗ M₂.obj Y) :=
47	  ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensorLift (fun m₁ m₂ ↦ M₁.map f m₁ ⊗ₜ M₂.map f m₂)
48	    (by
49	      intro m₁ m₁' m₂
50	      dsimp +instances
51	      rw [map_add, TensorProduct.add_tmul])
52	    (by intro a m₁ m₂; dsimp; erw [M₁.map_smul]; rfl)
53	    (by
54	      intro m₁ m₂ m₂'
55	      dsimp +instances
56	      rw [map_add, TensorProduct.tmul_add])
57	    (by intro a m₁ m₂; dsimp; erw [M₂.map_smul, TensorProduct.tmul_smul (r := R.map f a)]; rfl)
58	
59	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
60	/-- The tensor product of two presheaves of modules. -/
61	@[simps obj]
62	noncomputable def tensorObj : PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _) where
63	  obj X := M₁.obj X ⊗ M₂.obj X
64	  map f := tensorObjMap M₁ M₂ f
65	  map_id X := ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (by
66	    intro m₁ m₂
67	    dsimp [tensorObjMap]
68	    simp
69	    rfl) -- `ModuleCat.restrictScalarsId'App_inv_apply` doesn't get picked up due to type mismatch
70	  map_comp f g := ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (by
71	    intro m₁ m₂
72	    dsimp [tensorObjMap]
73	    simp +instances)
74	
75	variable {M₁ M₂ M₃ M₄}
76	
77	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
78	@[simp]
79	lemma tensorObj_map_tmul {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y) (m₁ : M₁.obj X) (m₂ : M₂.obj X) :
80	    DFunLike.coe (α := (M₁.obj X ⊗ M₂.obj X :))
81	      (β := fun _ ↦ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (R.map f).hom).obj (M₁.obj Y ⊗ M₂.obj Y))
82	      (ModuleCat.Hom.hom (R := ↑(R.obj X)) ((tensorObj M₁ M₂).map f)) (m₁ ⊗ₜ[R.obj X] m₂) =
83	    M₁.map f m₁ ⊗ₜ[R.obj Y] M₂.map f m₂ := rfl
84	
85	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
86	/-- The tensor product of two morphisms of presheaves of modules. -/
87	@[simps]
88	noncomputable def tensorHom (f : M₁ ⟶ M₂) (g : M₃ ⟶ M₄) : tensorObj M₁ M₃ ⟶ tensorObj M₂ M₄ where
89	  app X := f.app X ⊗ₘ g.app X
90	  naturality {X Y} φ := ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (fun m₁ m₃ ↦ by
91	    dsimp
92	    rw [tensorObj_map_tmul]
93	    -- Need `erw` because of the type mismatch in `map` and the tensor product.
94	    erw [ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensorHom_tmul, tensorObj_map_tmul]
95	    rw [naturality_apply, naturality_apply]
96	    simp)
97	
98	end Monoidal
99	
100	open Monoidal
101	
102	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
103	open ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory in
104	noncomputable instance monoidalCategoryStruct :
105	    MonoidalCategoryStruct (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)) where
106	  tensorObj := tensorObj
107	  whiskerLeft _ _ _ g := tensorHom (𝟙 _) g
108	  whiskerRight f _ := tensorHom f (𝟙 _)
109	  tensorHom := tensorHom
110	  tensorUnit := unit _
111	  associator M₁ M₂ M₃ := isoMk (fun _ ↦ α_ _ _ _)
112	    (fun _ _ _ ↦ ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext₃' (by intros; rfl))
113	  leftUnitor M := Iso.symm (isoMk (fun _ ↦ (λ_ _).symm) (fun X Y f ↦ by
114	    ext m
115	    dsimp [CommRingCat.forgetToRingCat_obj]
116	    erw [leftUnitor_inv_apply, leftUnitor_inv_apply, tensorObj_map_tmul, (R.map f).hom.map_one]
117	    rfl))
118	  rightUnitor M := Iso.symm (isoMk (fun _ ↦ (ρ_ _).symm) (fun X Y f ↦ by
119	    ext m
120	    dsimp [CommRingCat.forgetToRingCat_obj]
121	    erw [rightUnitor_inv_apply, rightUnitor_inv_apply, tensorObj_map_tmul, (R.map f).hom.map_one]
122	    rfl))
123	
124	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
125	noncomputable instance monoidalCategory :
126	    MonoidalCategory (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)) where
127	  tensorHom_def _ _ := by ext1; apply tensorHom_def
128	  id_tensorHom_id _ _ := by ext1; apply id_tensorHom_id
129	  tensorHom_comp_tensorHom _ _ _ _ := by ext1; apply tensorHom_comp_tensorHom
130	  whiskerLeft_id M₁ M₂ := by
131	    ext1 X
132	    apply MonoidalCategory.whiskerLeft_id (C := ModuleCat (R.obj X))
133	  id_whiskerRight _ _ := by
134	    ext1 X
135	    apply MonoidalCategory.id_whiskerRight (C := ModuleCat (R.obj X))
136	  associator_naturality _ _ _ := by ext1; apply associator_naturality
137	  leftUnitor_naturality _ := by ext1; apply leftUnitor_naturality
138	  rightUnitor_naturality _ := by ext1; apply rightUnitor_naturality
139	  pentagon _ _ _ _ := by ext1; apply pentagon
140	  triangle _ _ := by ext1; apply triangle
141	
142	open BraidedCategory
143	
144	instance symmetricCategory :
145	    SymmetricCategory (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)) where
146	  braiding M₁ M₂ :=
147	    isoMk (fun X ↦ braiding (C := ModuleCat (R.obj X)) (M₁.obj X) (M₂.obj X))
148	      (fun _ _ f ↦ ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (fun _ _ ↦ rfl))
149	  braiding_naturality_right _ _ _ _ := by
150	    ext : 1
151	    exact ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (fun _ _ ↦ rfl)
152	  braiding_naturality_left _ _ := by
153	    ext : 1
154	    exact ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (fun _ _ ↦ rfl)
155	  hexagon_forward _ _ _ := by
156	    ext : 1
157	    apply hexagon_forward (C := ModuleCat (R.obj _))
158	  hexagon_reverse _ _ _ := by
159	    ext : 1
160	    apply hexagon_reverse (C := ModuleCat (R.obj _))
161	  symmetry _ _ := by
162	    ext : 1
163	    apply SymmetricCategory.symmetry (C := ModuleCat (R.obj _))
164	
165	section
166	
167	variable (M₁ M₂ M₃ M₄ : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
168	
169	lemma tensorObj_obj (X : Cᵒᵖ) :
170	    (M₁ ⊗ M₂).obj X =
171	      MonoidalCategory.tensorObj (C := ModuleCat (R.obj X)) (M₁.obj X) (M₂.obj X) := rfl
172	
173	attribute [local simp] tensorObj_obj
174	
175	variable {M₂ M₃} in
176	@[simp]
177	lemma whiskerLeft_app (f : M₂ ⟶ M₃) (X : Cᵒᵖ) :
178	    dsimp% (M₁ ◁ f).app X = whiskerLeft (C := ModuleCat (R.obj X)) (M₁.obj X) (f.app X) :=
179	  rfl
180	
181	variable {M₁ M₂} in
182	@[simp]
183	lemma whiskerRight_app (f : M₁ ⟶ M₂) (M₃ : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)) (X : Cᵒᵖ) :
184	    dsimp% (f ▷ M₃).app X = whiskerRight (C := ModuleCat (R.obj X)) (f.app X) (M₃.obj X) := rfl
185	
186	variable {M₁ M₂ M₃ M₄} in
187	@[simp]
188	lemma tensorHom_app (f : M₁ ⟶ M₂) (g : M₃ ⟶ M₄) (X : Cᵒᵖ) :
189	    dsimp% (f ⊗ₘ g).app X =
190	      MonoidalCategory.tensorHom (C := ModuleCat (R.obj X)) (f.app X) (g.app X) := rfl
191	
192	@[simp]
193	lemma leftUnitor_hom_app (X : Cᵒᵖ) :
194	    dsimp% (λ_ M₁).hom.app X = (leftUnitor (C := ModuleCat (R.obj X)) (M₁.obj X)).hom :=
195	  rfl
196	
197	@[simp]
198	lemma leftUnitor_inv_app (X : Cᵒᵖ) :
199	    dsimp% (λ_ M₁).inv.app X = (leftUnitor (C := ModuleCat (R.obj X)) (M₁.obj X)).inv := by

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Localization.Bousfield
9	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Sheafification
10	
11	/-!
12	# The sheaf category as a localized category
13	
14	In this file, it is shown that the category of sheaves `Sheaf J A` is a localization
15	of the category `Presheaf J A` with respect to the class `J.W` of morphisms
16	of presheaves which become isomorphisms after applying the sheafification functor.
17	
18	-/
19	
20	universe w
21	
22	@[expose] public section
23	
24	namespace CategoryTheory
25	
26	open Localization
27	
28	variable {C : Type*} [Category* C] (J : GrothendieckTopology C) {A : Type*} [Category* A]
29	
30	namespace GrothendieckTopology
31	
32	/-- The class of morphisms of presheaves which become isomorphisms after sheafification.
33	(See `GrothendieckTopology.W_iff`.) -/
34	abbrev W : MorphismProperty (Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) := ObjectProperty.isLocal (Presheaf.IsSheaf J)
35	
36	variable (A) in
37	lemma W_eq_isLocal_range_sheafToPresheaf_obj :
38	    J.W = ObjectProperty.isLocal (· ∈ Set.range (sheafToPresheaf J A).obj) := by
39	  apply congr_arg
40	  ext P
41	  constructor
42	  · intro hP
43	    exact ⟨⟨P, hP⟩, rfl⟩
44	  · rintro ⟨F, rfl⟩
45	    exact F.property
46	
47	@[deprecated (since := "2025-11-20")] alias W_eq_W_range_sheafToPresheaf_obj :=
48	  W_eq_isLocal_range_sheafToPresheaf_obj
49	
50	lemma W_sheafToPresheaf_map_iff_isIso {F₁ F₂ : Sheaf J A} (φ : F₁ ⟶ F₂) :
51	    J.W ((sheafToPresheaf J A).map φ) ↔ IsIso φ := by
52	  rw [W_eq_isLocal_range_sheafToPresheaf_obj,
53	    ObjectProperty.isLocal_iff_isIso _ _ ⟨_, rfl⟩ ⟨_, rfl⟩, isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso]
54	
55	section Adjunction
56	
57	variable {G : (Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) ⥤ Sheaf J A}
58	
59	lemma W_adj_unit_app (adj : G ⊣ sheafToPresheaf J A) (P : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) : J.W (adj.unit.app P) := by
60	  rw [W_eq_isLocal_range_sheafToPresheaf_obj]
61	  exact ObjectProperty.isLocal_adj_unit_app adj P
62	
63	lemma W_iff_isIso_map_of_adjunction (adj : G ⊣ sheafToPresheaf J A)
64	    {P₁ P₂ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f : P₁ ⟶ P₂) :
65	    J.W f ↔ IsIso (G.map f) := by
66	  rw [W_eq_isLocal_range_sheafToPresheaf_obj]
67	  exact ObjectProperty.isLocal_iff_isIso_map adj f
68	
69	lemma W_eq_inverseImage_isomorphisms_of_adjunction (adj : G ⊣ sheafToPresheaf J A) :
70	    J.W = (MorphismProperty.isomorphisms _).inverseImage G := by
71	  rw [W_eq_isLocal_range_sheafToPresheaf_obj,
72	    ObjectProperty.isLocal_eq_inverseImage_isomorphisms adj]
73	
74	end Adjunction
75	
76	section HasWeakSheafify
77	
78	variable [HasWeakSheafify J A]
79	
80	lemma W_toSheafify (P : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) : J.W (toSheafify J P) :=
81	  J.W_adj_unit_app (sheafificationAdjunction J A) P
82	
83	lemma W_iff {P₁ P₂ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f : P₁ ⟶ P₂) :
84	    J.W f ↔ IsIso ((presheafToSheaf J A).map f) :=
85	  J.W_iff_isIso_map_of_adjunction (sheafificationAdjunction J A) f
86	
87	variable (A) in
88	lemma W_eq_inverseImage_isomorphisms :
89	    J.W = (MorphismProperty.isomorphisms _).inverseImage (presheafToSheaf J A) :=
90	  J.W_eq_inverseImage_isomorphisms_of_adjunction (sheafificationAdjunction J A)
91	
92	instance : (presheafToSheaf J A).IsLocalization J.W := by
93	  rw [W_eq_inverseImage_isomorphisms]
94	  exact (sheafificationAdjunction J A).isLocalization
95	
96	end HasWeakSheafify
97	
98	end GrothendieckTopology
99	
100	lemma Sieve.W_shrinkFunctor_ι_of_mem [LocallySmall.{w} C] {X : C} (S : Sieve X) (hS : S ∈ J X) :
101	    J.W (Sieve.shrinkFunctor.{w} S).ι := by
102	  intro Z hZ
103	  rw [isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_of_type] at hZ
104	  rw [← Presieve.isSheafFor_iff_bijective_shrinkFunctor_ι_comp]
105	  exact hZ _ hS
106	
107	variable {D : Type*} [Category* D] {K : GrothendieckTopology D}
108	
109	/-- SGA 4 III 1.2 (ii) => (i) -/
110	lemma Presieve.IsSheaf.comp_of_W_map_of_adjunction
111	    [LocallySmall.{w} C] {F : C ⥤ D} {H : (Cᵒᵖ ⥤ Type w) ⥤ (Dᵒᵖ ⥤ Type w)}
112	    (adj : H ⊣ (Functor.whiskeringLeft _ _ _).obj F.op)
113	    (h : ∀ ⦃X : C⦄ ⦃S : Sieve X⦄, S ∈ J X → K.W (H.map <| (Sieve.shrinkFunctor.{w} S).ι))
114	    (G : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ Type w) (hG : Presieve.IsSheaf K G) :
115	    Presieve.IsSheaf J (F.op ⋙ G) := by
116	  intro X S hS
117	  rw [Presieve.isSheafFor_iff_bijective_shrinkFunctor_ι_comp, ← Functor.whiskeringLeft_obj_obj,
118	    ← adj.map_comp_bijective_iff]
119	  refine h hS _ ?_
120	  rwa [isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_of_type]
121	
122	end CategoryTheory
123

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.ChangeOfRings
9	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.Ring.Basic
10	
11	/-!
12	# Presheaves of modules over a presheaf of rings.
13	
14	Given a presheaf of rings `R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat`, we define the category `PresheafOfModules R`.
15	An object `M : PresheafOfModules R` consists of a family of modules
16	`M.obj X : ModuleCat (R.obj X)` for all `X : Cᵒᵖ`, together with the data, for all `f : X ⟶ Y`,
17	of a functorial linear map `M.map f` from `M.obj X` to the restriction
18	of scalars of `M.obj Y` via `R.map f`.
19	
20	
21	## Future work
22	
23	* Compare this to the definition as a presheaf of pairs `(R, M)` with specified first part.
24	* Compare this to the definition as a module object of the presheaf of rings
25	  thought of as a monoid object.
26	* Presheaves of modules over a presheaf of commutative rings form a monoidal category.
27	* Pushforward and pullback.
28	-/
29	
30	@[expose] public section
31	
32	universe v v₁ u₁ u
33	
34	open CategoryTheory LinearMap Opposite
35	
36	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}}
37	
38	variable (R) in
39	/-- A presheaf of modules over `R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat` consists of family of
40	objects `obj X : ModuleCat (R.obj X)` for all `X : Cᵒᵖ` together with
41	functorial maps `obj X ⟶ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (R.map f)).obj (obj Y)`
42	for all `f : X ⟶ Y` in `Cᵒᵖ`. -/
43	structure PresheafOfModules where
44	  /-- a family of modules over `R.obj X` for all `X` -/
45	  obj (X : Cᵒᵖ) : ModuleCat.{v} (R.obj X)
46	  /-- the restriction maps of a presheaf of modules -/
47	  map {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y) : obj X ⟶ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (R.map f).hom).obj (obj Y)
48	  map_id (X : Cᵒᵖ) :
49	    map (𝟙 X) = (ModuleCat.restrictScalarsId' (R.map (𝟙 X)).hom
50	      (congrArg RingCat.Hom.hom (R.map_id X))).inv.app _ := by
51	        cat_disch
52	  map_comp {X Y Z : Cᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) :
53	    map (f ≫ g) = map f ≫ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars _).map (map g) ≫
54	      (ModuleCat.restrictScalarsComp' (R.map f).hom (R.map g).hom (R.map (f ≫ g)).hom
55	        (congrArg RingCat.Hom.hom <| R.map_comp f g)).inv.app _ := by cat_disch
56	
57	namespace PresheafOfModules
58	
59	attribute [simp] map_id map_comp
60	attribute [reassoc] map_comp
61	
62	#adaptation_note /-- lean-pr-testing-12564
63	This is required for `Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Differentials.Presheaf` -/
64	instance {R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}} (X : Cᵒᵖ) (M : PresheafOfModules.{v} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)) :
65	    Module (R.obj X) (M.obj X) := (M.obj X).isModule
66	
67	variable (M M₁ M₂ : PresheafOfModules.{v} R)
68	
69	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
70	protected lemma map_smul {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y) (r : R.obj X) (m : M.obj X) :
71	    M.map f (r • m) = R.map f r • M.map f m := by simp
72	
73	lemma congr_map_apply {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} {f g : X ⟶ Y} (h : f = g) (m : M.obj X) :
74	    M.map f m = M.map g m := by rw [h]
75	
76	lemma map_comp_apply {U V W : Cᵒᵖ} (i : U ⟶ V) (j : V ⟶ W) (x) :
77	    M.map (i ≫ j) x = M.map j (M.map i x) := by
78	  rw [M.map_comp]; rfl
79	
80	/-- A morphism of presheaves of modules consists of a family of linear maps which
81	satisfy the naturality condition. -/
82	@[ext]
83	structure Hom where
84	  /-- a family of linear maps `M₁.obj X ⟶ M₂.obj X` for all `X`. -/
85	  app (X : Cᵒᵖ) : M₁.obj X ⟶ M₂.obj X
86	  naturality {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y) :
87	      M₁.map f ≫ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (R.map f).hom).map (app Y) =
88	        app X ≫ M₂.map f := by cat_disch
89	
90	attribute [reassoc (attr := simp)] Hom.naturality
91	
92	instance : Category (PresheafOfModules.{v} R) where
93	  Hom := Hom
94	  id _ := { app := fun _ ↦ 𝟙 _ }
95	  comp f g := { app := fun _ ↦ f.app _ ≫ g.app _ }
96	
97	variable {M₁ M₂}
98	
99	@[ext]
100	lemma hom_ext {f g : M₁ ⟶ M₂} (h : ∀ (X : Cᵒᵖ), f.app X = g.app X) :

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.LeftExact
9	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.PreservesSheafification
10	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Subsheaf
11	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Whiskering

13	/-!
14	# Locally injective morphisms of (pre)sheaves
15	
16	Let `C` be a category equipped with a Grothendieck topology `J`,
17	and let `D` be a concrete category. In this file, we introduce the typeclass
18	`Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J φ` for a morphism `φ : F₁ ⟶ F₂` in the category
19	`Cᵒᵖ ⥤ D`. This means that `φ` is locally injective. More precisely,
20	if `x` and `y` are two elements of some `F₁.obj U` such
21	the images of `x` and `y` in `F₂.obj U` coincide, then
22	the equality `x = y` must hold locally, i.e. after restriction
23	by the maps of a covering sieve.
24	
25	-/
26	
27	@[expose] public section
28	
29	universe w v' v u' u
30	
31	namespace CategoryTheory
32	
33	open Opposite Limits
34	
35	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C]
36	  {D : Type u'} [Category.{v'} D] {FD : D → D → Type*} {CD : D → Type w}
37	  [∀ X Y, FunLike (FD X Y) (CD X) (CD Y)] [ConcreteCategory.{w} D FD]
38	  (J : GrothendieckTopology C)
39	
40	namespace Presheaf
41	
42	/-- If `F : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ D` is a presheaf with values in a concrete category, if `x` and `y` are
43	elements in `F.obj X`, this is the sieve of `X.unop` consisting of morphisms `f`
44	such that `F.map f.op x = F.map f.op y`. -/
45	@[simps]
46	def equalizerSieve {F : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ D} {X : Cᵒᵖ} (x y : ToType (F.obj X)) : Sieve X.unop where
47	  arrows _ f := F.map f.op x = F.map f.op y
48	  downward_closed {X Y} f hf g := by
49	    dsimp at hf ⊢
50	    simp [hf]
51	
52	@[simp]
53	lemma equalizerSieve_self_eq_top {F : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ D} {X : Cᵒᵖ} (x : ToType (F.obj X)) :
54	    equalizerSieve x x = ⊤ := by aesop
55	
56	@[simp]
57	lemma equalizerSieve_eq_top_iff {F : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ D} {X : Cᵒᵖ} (x y : ToType (F.obj X)) :
58	    equalizerSieve x y = ⊤ ↔ x = y := by
59	  constructor
60	  · intro h
61	    simpa using (show equalizerSieve x y (𝟙 _) by simp [h])
62	  · rintro rfl
63	    apply equalizerSieve_self_eq_top
64	
65	variable {F₁ F₂ F₃ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ D} (φ : F₁ ⟶ F₂) (ψ : F₂ ⟶ F₃)
66	
67	/-- A morphism `φ : F₁ ⟶ F₂` of presheaves `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ D` (with `D` a concrete category)
68	is locally injective for a Grothendieck topology `J` on `C` if
69	whenever two sections of `F₁` are sent to the same section of `F₂`, then these two
70	sections coincide locally. -/
71	class IsLocallyInjective : Prop where
72	  equalizerSieve_mem {X : Cᵒᵖ} (x y : ToType (F₁.obj X)) (h : φ.app X x = φ.app X y) :
73	    equalizerSieve x y ∈ J X.unop
74	
75	lemma equalizerSieve_mem [IsLocallyInjective J φ]
76	    {X : Cᵒᵖ} (x y : ToType (F₁.obj X)) (h : φ.app X x = φ.app X y) :
77	    equalizerSieve x y ∈ J X.unop :=
78	  IsLocallyInjective.equalizerSieve_mem x y h
79	
80	lemma isLocallyInjective_of_injective (hφ : ∀ (X : Cᵒᵖ), Function.Injective (φ.app X)) :
81	    IsLocallyInjective J φ where
82	  equalizerSieve_mem {X} x y h := by
83	    convert J.top_mem X.unop
84	    ext Y f
85	    simp only [equalizerSieve_apply, op_unop, Sieve.top_apply, iff_true]
86	    apply hφ
87	    simp [h]
88	
89	instance [IsIso φ] : IsLocallyInjective J φ :=
90	  isLocallyInjective_of_injective J φ (fun X => Function.Bijective.injective (by
91	    rw [bijective_iff_isIso_ofHom]
92	    infer_instance))
93	
94	instance isLocallyInjective_forget [IsLocallyInjective J φ] :
95	    IsLocallyInjective J (Functor.whiskerRight φ (forget D)) where
96	  equalizerSieve_mem x y h := equalizerSieve_mem J φ x y h
97	
98	lemma isLocallyInjective_forget_iff :
99	    IsLocallyInjective J (Functor.whiskerRight φ (forget D)) ↔ IsLocallyInjective J φ := by
100	  constructor
101	  · intro
102	    exact ⟨fun x y h => equalizerSieve_mem J (Functor.whiskerRight φ (forget D)) x y h⟩
103	  · intro
104	    infer_instance
105	
106	lemma isLocallyInjective_iff_equalizerSieve_mem_imp :
107	    IsLocallyInjective J φ ↔ ∀ ⦃X : Cᵒᵖ⦄ (x y : ToType (F₁.obj X)),
108	      equalizerSieve (φ.app _ x) (φ.app _ y) ∈ J X.unop → equalizerSieve x y ∈ J X.unop := by
109	  constructor
110	  · intro _ X x y h
111	    let S := equalizerSieve (φ.app _ x) (φ.app _ y)
112	    let T : ∀ ⦃Y : C⦄ ⦃f : Y ⟶ X.unop⦄ (_ : S f), Sieve Y := fun Y f _ =>
113	      equalizerSieve (F₁.map f.op x) ((F₁.map f.op y))
114	    refine J.superset_covering ?_ (J.transitive h (Sieve.bind S.1 T) ?_)
115	    · rintro Y f ⟨Z, a, g, hg, ha, rfl⟩
116	      simpa using ha
117	    · intro Y f hf
118	      refine J.superset_covering (Sieve.le_pullback_bind S.1 T _ hf)
119	        (equalizerSieve_mem J φ _ _ ?_)
120	      rw [NatTrans.naturality_apply, NatTrans.naturality_apply]
121	      exact hf
122	  · intro hφ
123	    exact ⟨fun {X} x y h => hφ x y (by simp [h])⟩
124	
125	lemma equalizerSieve_mem_of_equalizerSieve_app_mem
126	    {X : Cᵒᵖ} (x y : ToType (F₁.obj X)) (h : equalizerSieve (φ.app _ x) (φ.app _ y) ∈ J X.unop)
127	    [IsLocallyInjective J φ] :
128	    equalizerSieve x y ∈ J X.unop :=
129	  (isLocallyInjective_iff_equalizerSieve_mem_imp J φ).1 inferInstance x y h
130	
131	instance isLocallyInjective_comp [IsLocallyInjective J φ] [IsLocallyInjective J ψ] :
132	    IsLocallyInjective J (φ ≫ ψ) where
133	  equalizerSieve_mem {X} x y h := by
134	    apply equalizerSieve_mem_of_equalizerSieve_app_mem J φ
135	    exact equalizerSieve_mem J ψ _ _ (by simpa using h)
136	
137	lemma isLocallyInjective_of_isLocallyInjective [IsLocallyInjective J (φ ≫ ψ)] :
138	    IsLocallyInjective J φ where
139	  equalizerSieve_mem {X} x y h := equalizerSieve_mem J (φ ≫ ψ) x y (by simp [h])
140	
141	variable {φ ψ}
142	
143	lemma isLocallyInjective_of_isLocallyInjective_fac {φψ : F₁ ⟶ F₃} (fac : φ ≫ ψ = φψ)
144	    [IsLocallyInjective J φψ] : IsLocallyInjective J φ := by
145	  subst fac
146	  exact isLocallyInjective_of_isLocallyInjective J φ ψ
147	
148	lemma isLocallyInjective_iff_of_fac {φψ : F₁ ⟶ F₃} (fac : φ ≫ ψ = φψ) [IsLocallyInjective J ψ] :
149	    IsLocallyInjective J φψ ↔ IsLocallyInjective J φ := by
150	  constructor

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Subsheaf
9	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.CompatibleSheafification
10	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.LocallyInjective
11	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.ShrinkYoneda
12	/-!
13	
14	# Locally surjective morphisms
15	
16	## Main definitions
17	
18	- `IsLocallySurjective` : A morphism of presheaves valued in a concrete category is locally
19	  surjective with respect to a Grothendieck topology if every section in the target is locally
20	  in the set-theoretic image, i.e. the image sheaf coincides with the target.
21	
22	## Main results
23	
24	- `Presheaf.isLocallySurjective_toSheafify`: `toSheafify` is locally surjective.
25	- `Sheaf.isLocallySurjective_iff_epi`: a morphism of sheaves of types is locally
26	  surjective iff it is epi.
27	
28	-/
29	
30	@[expose] public section
31	
32	
33	universe w v u v' u' w'
34	
35	open Opposite CategoryTheory CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology CategoryTheory.Functor Limits
36	
37	namespace CategoryTheory
38	
39	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] (J : GrothendieckTopology C)
40	
41	variable {A : Type u'} [Category.{v'} A] {FA : A → A → Type*} {CA : A → Type w'}
42	variable [∀ X Y, FunLike (FA X Y) (CA X) (CA Y)] [ConcreteCategory.{w'} A FA]
43	
44	namespace Presheaf
45	
46	/-- Given `f : F ⟶ G`, a morphism between presieves, and `s : G.obj (op U)`, this is the sieve
47	of `U` consisting of the `i : V ⟶ U` such that `s` restricted along `i` is in the image of `f`. -/
48	@[simps -isSimp]
49	def imageSieve {F G : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f : F ⟶ G) {U : C} (s : ToType (G.obj (op U))) : Sieve U where
50	  arrows V i := ∃ t : ToType (F.obj (op V)), f.app _ t = G.map i.op s
51	  downward_closed := by
52	    rintro V W i ⟨t, ht⟩ j
53	    refine ⟨F.map j.op t, ?_⟩
54	    rw [op_comp, G.map_comp, ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← ht, NatTrans.naturality_apply f]
55	
56	lemma pullback_imageSieve
57	    {F G : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f : F ⟶ G) {U : C} (s : ToType (G.obj (op U)))
58	    {V : C} (g : V ⟶ U) :
59	    (imageSieve f s).pullback g = imageSieve f (G.map g.op s) := by
60	  ext W g
61	  simp [imageSieve]
62	
63	theorem imageSieve_eq_sieveOfSection {F G : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f : F ⟶ G) {U : C}
64	    (s : ToType (G.obj (op U))) :
65	    imageSieve f s = (Subfunctor.range (whiskerRight f (forget A))).sieveOfSection s :=
66	  rfl
67	
68	theorem imageSieve_whisker_forget {F G : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f : F ⟶ G) {U : C} (s : ToType (G.obj (op U))) :
69	    imageSieve (whiskerRight f (forget A)) s = imageSieve f s :=
70	  rfl
71	
72	theorem imageSieve_app {F G : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f : F ⟶ G) {U : C} (s : ToType (F.obj (op U))) :
73	    imageSieve f (f.app _ s) = ⊤ := by
74	  ext V i
75	  simp only [Sieve.top_apply, iff_true, imageSieve_apply]
76	  exact ⟨F.map i.op s, NatTrans.naturality_apply f i.op s⟩
77	
78	/-- If a morphism `g : V ⟶ U.unop` belongs to the sieve `imageSieve f s g`, then
79	this is choice of a preimage of `G.map g.op s` in `F.obj (op V)`, see
80	`app_localPreimage`. -/
81	noncomputable def localPreimage {F G : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f : F ⟶ G) {U : Cᵒᵖ} (s : ToType (G.obj U))
82	    {V : C} (g : V ⟶ U.unop) (hg : imageSieve f s g) :
83	    ToType (F.obj (op V)) :=
84	  hg.choose
85	
86	@[simp]
87	lemma app_localPreimage {F G : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f : F ⟶ G) {U : Cᵒᵖ} (s : ToType (G.obj U))
88	    {V : C} (g : V ⟶ U.unop) (hg : imageSieve f s g) :
89	    f.app _ (localPreimage f s g hg) = G.map g.op s :=
90	  hg.choose_spec
91	
92	/-- A morphism of presheaves `f : F ⟶ G` is locally surjective with respect to a Grothendieck
93	topology if every section of `G` is locally in the image of `f`. -/
94	class IsLocallySurjective {F G : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f : F ⟶ G) : Prop where
95	  imageSieve_mem {U : C} (s : ToType (G.obj (op U))) : imageSieve f s ∈ J U
96	
97	lemma imageSieve_mem {F G : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f : F ⟶ G) [IsLocallySurjective J f] {U : Cᵒᵖ}
98	    (s : ToType (G.obj U)) : imageSieve f s ∈ J U.unop :=
99	  IsLocallySurjective.imageSieve_mem _
100	
101	instance {F G : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f : F ⟶ G) [IsLocallySurjective J f] :
102	    IsLocallySurjective J (whiskerRight f (forget A)) where
103	  imageSieve_mem s := imageSieve_mem J f s
104	
105	theorem isLocallySurjective_iff_range_sheafify_eq_top {F G : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f : F ⟶ G) :
106	    IsLocallySurjective J f ↔ (Subfunctor.range (whiskerRight f (forget A))).sheafify J = ⊤ := by
107	  simp only [Subfunctor.ext_iff, funext_iff, Set.ext_iff, Subfunctor.top_obj,
108	    Set.top_eq_univ, Set.mem_univ, iff_true]
109	  exact ⟨fun H _ => H.imageSieve_mem, fun H => ⟨H _⟩⟩
110	
111	theorem isLocallySurjective_iff_range_sheafify_eq_top' {F G : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ Type w} (f : F ⟶ G) :
112	    IsLocallySurjective J f ↔ (Subfunctor.range f).sheafify J = ⊤ := by
113	  apply isLocallySurjective_iff_range_sheafify_eq_top
114	
115	theorem isLocallySurjective_iff_whisker_forget {F G : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f : F ⟶ G) :
116	    IsLocallySurjective J f ↔ IsLocallySurjective J (whiskerRight f (forget A)) := by
117	  simp only [isLocallySurjective_iff_range_sheafify_eq_top]
118	  rfl
119	
120	theorem isLocallySurjective_of_surjective {F G : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f : F ⟶ G)
121	    (H : ∀ U, Function.Surjective (f.app U)) : IsLocallySurjective J f where
122	  imageSieve_mem {U} s := by
123	    obtain ⟨t, rfl⟩ := H _ s
124	    rw [imageSieve_app]
125	    exact J.top_mem _
126	
127	instance isLocallySurjective_of_iso {F G : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f : F ⟶ G) [IsIso f] :
128	    IsLocallySurjective J f := by
129	  apply isLocallySurjective_of_surjective
130	  intro U
131	  apply Function.Bijective.surjective
132	  rw [bijective_iff_isIso_ofHom]
133	  infer_instance
134	
135	instance isLocallySurjective_comp {F₁ F₂ F₃ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f₁ : F₁ ⟶ F₂) (f₂ : F₂ ⟶ F₃)
136	    [IsLocallySurjective J f₁] [IsLocallySurjective J f₂] :
137	    IsLocallySurjective J (f₁ ≫ f₂) where
138	  imageSieve_mem s := by
139	    have : (Sieve.bind (imageSieve f₂ s) fun _ _ h => imageSieve f₁ h.choose) ≤
140	        imageSieve (f₁ ≫ f₂) s := by
141	      rintro V i ⟨W, i, j, H, ⟨t', ht'⟩, rfl⟩
142	      refine ⟨t', ?_⟩
143	      rw [op_comp, F₃.map_comp, NatTrans.comp_app, ConcreteCategory.comp_apply,
144	        ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ht', NatTrans.naturality_apply, H.choose_spec]
145	    apply J.superset_covering this
146	    apply J.bind_covering
147	    · apply imageSieve_mem
148	    · intros; apply imageSieve_mem

80:lemma isLocallyInjective_of_injective (hφ : ∀ (X : Cᵒᵖ), Function.Injective (φ.app X)) :

149	    IsLocallyInjective J φψ ↔ IsLocallyInjective J φ := by

163	lemma isLocallyInjective_iff_injective_of_separated
164	    (hsep : Presieve.IsSeparated J (F₁ ⋙ forget D)) :
165	    IsLocallyInjective J φ ↔ ∀ (X : Cᵒᵖ), Function.Injective (φ.app X) := by
166	  constructor

186	    exact J.superset_covering (fun Y f hf => congr_fun (congr_arg Subtype.val eq) ⟨Y, f, hf⟩) W.2

195	instance isLocallyInjective_toSheafify' {CD : D → Type (max u v)}
196	    [∀ X Y, FunLike (FD X Y) (CD X) (CD Y)] [ConcreteCategory.{max u v} D FD]
197	    (P : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ D) [HasWeakSheafify J D] [J.HasSheafCompose (forget D)]

215	is equivalent to the injectivity of all maps `φ.val.app X`,
216	see `isLocallyInjective_iff_injective`. -/

226	lemma mono_of_injective
227	    (hφ : ∀ (X : Cᵒᵖ), Function.Injective (φ.hom.app X)) : Mono φ :=
228	  have : ∀ X, Mono (φ.hom.app X) := fun X ↦ ConcreteCategory.mono_of_injective _ (hφ X)

238	    IsLocallyInjective φ ↔ ∀ (X : Cᵒᵖ), Function.Injective (φ.hom.app X) :=
239	  Presheaf.isLocallyInjective_iff_injective_of_separated _ _ (by
240	    apply Presieve.IsSheaf.isSeparated

242	    exact ((sheafCompose J (forget D)).obj F₁).2)
243	
244	lemma mono_of_isLocallyInjective [IsLocallyInjective φ] : Mono φ := by
245	  apply mono_of_injective
246	  rw [← isLocallyInjective_iff_injective]
247	  infer_instance

189	    {F₁ F₂ F₃ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f₁ : F₁ ⟶ F₂) (f₂ : F₂ ⟶ F₃)
190	    [IsLocallyInjective J (f₁ ≫ f₂)] [IsLocallySurjective J f₁] :

193	    let S := imageSieve f₁ x₁ ⊓ imageSieve f₁ x₂

195	      apply J.intersection_covering

197	    let T : ∀ ⦃Y : C⦄ (f : Y ⟶ X.unop) (_ : S f), Sieve Y := fun Y f hf =>
198	      equalizerSieve (localPreimage f₁ x₁ f hf.1) (localPreimage f₁ x₂ f hf.2)

270	noncomputable def sheafificationIsoImagePresheaf (F : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ Type (max u v)) :
271	    J.sheafify F ≅ ((Subfunctor.range (J.toSheafify F)).sheafify J).toFunctor where
272	  hom :=
273	    J.sheafifyLift (Subfunctor.toRangeSheafify J _)
274	      ((isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_of_type J _).mpr <|
275	        Subfunctor.sheafify_isSheaf _ <|
276	          (isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_of_type J _).mp <| GrothendieckTopology.sheafify_isSheaf J _)
277	  inv := Subfunctor.ι _
278	  hom_inv_id :=
279	    J.sheafify_hom_ext _ _ (J.sheafify_isSheaf _) (by simp [Subfunctor.toRangeSheafify])
280	  inv_hom_id := by
281	    rw [← cancel_mono (Subfunctor.ι _), Category.id_comp, Category.assoc]
282	    refine Eq.trans ?_ (Category.comp_id _)
283	    congr 1
284	    exact J.sheafify_hom_ext _ _ (J.sheafify_isSheaf _) (by simp [Subfunctor.toRangeSheafify])

39	/-- A presheaf of modules over `R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat` consists of family of
40	objects `obj X : ModuleCat (R.obj X)` for all `X : Cᵒᵖ` together with
41	functorial maps `obj X ⟶ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (R.map f)).obj (obj Y)`
42	for all `f : X ⟶ Y` in `Cᵒᵖ`. -/
43	structure PresheafOfModules where
44	  /-- a family of modules over `R.obj X` for all `X` -/
45	  obj (X : Cᵒᵖ) : ModuleCat.{v} (R.obj X)
46	  /-- the restriction maps of a presheaf of modules -/
47	  map {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y) : obj X ⟶ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (R.map f).hom).obj (obj Y)

49	    map (𝟙 X) = (ModuleCat.restrictScalarsId' (R.map (𝟙 X)).hom
50	      (congrArg RingCat.Hom.hom (R.map_id X))).inv.app _ := by
51	        cat_disch

53	    map (f ≫ g) = map f ≫ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars _).map (map g) ≫
54	      (ModuleCat.restrictScalarsComp' (R.map f).hom (R.map g).hom (R.map (f ≫ g)).hom
55	        (congrArg RingCat.Hom.hom <| R.map_comp f g)).inv.app _ := by cat_disch

9	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.LocallySurjective

11	/-!
12	# The associated sheaf of a presheaf of modules
13	
14	In this file, given a presheaf of modules `M₀` over a presheaf of rings `R₀`,
15	we construct the associated sheaf of `M₀`. More precisely, if `R` is a sheaf of
16	rings and `α : R₀ ⟶ R.val` is locally bijective, and `A` is the sheafification
17	of the underlying presheaf of abelian groups of `M₀`, i.e. we have a locally bijective
18	map `φ : M₀.presheaf ⟶ A.val`, then we endow `A` with the structure of a
19	sheaf of modules over `R`: this is `PresheafOfModules.sheafify α φ`.
20	
21	In many applications, the morphism `α` shall be the identity, but this more
22	general construction allows the sheafification of both the presheaf of rings
23	and the presheaf of modules.
24	
25	-/
26	
27	@[expose] public section
28	
29	universe w v v₁ u₁ u
30	
31	open CategoryTheory Functor
32	
33	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
34	
35	namespace CategoryTheory
36	
37	namespace Presieve.FamilyOfElements
38	
39	section smul
40	
41	variable {R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {M : PresheafOfModules.{v} R} {X : C} {P : Presieve X}
42	  (r : FamilyOfElements (R ⋙ forget _) P) (m : FamilyOfElements (M.presheaf ⋙ forget _) P)
43	
44	/-- The scalar multiplication of family of elements of a presheaf of modules `M` over `R`
45	by a family of elements of `R`. -/
46	def smul : FamilyOfElements (M.presheaf ⋙ forget _) P := fun Y f hf =>
47	  HSMul.hSMul (α := R.obj (Opposite.op Y)) (β := M.obj (Opposite.op Y)) (r f hf) (m f hf)
48	
49	end smul

53	variable {R₀ R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} (α : R₀ ⟶ R) [Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J α]
54	  {M₀ : PresheafOfModules.{v} R₀} {A : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat.{v}} (φ : M₀.presheaf ⟶ A)
55	  [Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J φ] (hA : Presheaf.IsSeparated J A)
56	  {X : C} (r : R.obj (Opposite.op X)) (m : A.obj (Opposite.op X)) {P : Presieve X}
57	  (r₀ : FamilyOfElements (R₀ ⋙ forget _) P) (m₀ : FamilyOfElements (M₀.presheaf ⋙ forget _) P)
58	include hA

60	lemma _root_.PresheafOfModules.Sheafify.app_eq_of_isLocallyInjective
61	    {Y : C} (r₀ r₀' : R₀.obj (Opposite.op Y))
62	    (m₀ m₀' : M₀.obj (Opposite.op Y))
63	    (hr₀ : α.app _ r₀ = α.app _ r₀')
64	    (hm₀ : φ.app _ m₀ = φ.app _ m₀') :
65	    φ.app _ (r₀ • m₀) = φ.app _ (r₀' • m₀') := by
66	  apply hA _ (Presheaf.equalizerSieve r₀ r₀' ⊓
67	      Presheaf.equalizerSieve (F := M₀.presheaf) m₀ m₀')
68	  · apply J.intersection_covering
69	    · exact Presheaf.equalizerSieve_mem J α _ _ hr₀
70	    · exact Presheaf.equalizerSieve_mem J φ _ _ hm₀
71	  · intro Z g hg
72	    rw [← NatTrans.naturality_apply (D := Ab), ← NatTrans.naturality_apply (D := Ab)]
73	    erw [M₀.map_smul, M₀.map_smul, hg.1, hg.2]
74	    rfl

77	lemma isCompatible_map_smul_aux {Y Z : C} (f : Y ⟶ X) (g : Z ⟶ Y)
78	    (r₀ : R₀.obj (Opposite.op Y)) (r₀' : R₀.obj (Opposite.op Z))
79	    (m₀ : M₀.obj (Opposite.op Y)) (m₀' : M₀.obj (Opposite.op Z))
80	    (hr₀ : α.app _ r₀ = R.map f.op r) (hr₀' : α.app _ r₀' = R.map (f.op ≫ g.op) r)
81	    (hm₀ : φ.app _ m₀ = A.map f.op m) (hm₀' : φ.app _ m₀' = A.map (f.op ≫ g.op) m) :
82	    φ.app _ (M₀.map g.op (r₀ • m₀)) = φ.app _ (r₀' • m₀') := by
83	  rw [← PresheafOfModules.Sheafify.app_eq_of_isLocallyInjective α φ hA (R₀.map g.op r₀) r₀'
84	    (M₀.map g.op m₀) m₀', M₀.map_smul]
85	  · rw [hr₀', R.map_comp, RingCat.comp_apply, ← hr₀, ← RingCat.comp_apply, NatTrans.naturality,
86	      RingCat.comp_apply]
87	  · rw [hm₀', A.map_comp, AddCommGrpCat.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, ← hm₀]
88	    erw [NatTrans.naturality_apply φ]

90	variable (hr₀ : (r₀.map (whiskerRight α (forget _))).IsAmalgamation r)
91	  (hm₀ : (m₀.map (whiskerRight φ (forget _))).IsAmalgamation m)
92	
93	include hr₀ hm₀ in
94	lemma isCompatible_map_smul : ((r₀.smul m₀).map (whiskerRight φ (forget _))).Compatible := by
95	  intro Y₁ Y₂ Z g₁ g₂ f₁ f₂ h₁ h₂ fac
96	  let a₁ := r₀ f₁ h₁
97	  let b₁ := m₀ f₁ h₁
98	  let a₂ := r₀ f₂ h₂
99	  let b₂ := m₀ f₂ h₂
100	  let a₀ := R₀.map g₁.op a₁
101	  let b₀ := M₀.map g₁.op b₁
102	  have ha₁ : (α.app (Opposite.op Y₁)) a₁ = (R.map f₁.op) r := (hr₀ f₁ h₁).symm
103	  have ha₂ : (α.app (Opposite.op Y₂)) a₂ = (R.map f₂.op) r := (hr₀ f₂ h₂).symm
104	  have hb₁ : (φ.app (Opposite.op Y₁)) b₁ = (A.map f₁.op) m := (hm₀ f₁ h₁).symm
105	  have hb₂ : (φ.app (Opposite.op Y₂)) b₂ = (A.map f₂.op) m := (hm₀ f₂ h₂).symm
106	  have ha₀ : (α.app (Opposite.op Z)) a₀ = (R.map (f₁.op ≫ g₁.op)) r := by
107	    rw [← RingCat.comp_apply, NatTrans.naturality, RingCat.comp_apply, ha₁, Functor.map_comp,
108	      RingCat.comp_apply]
109	  have hb₀ : (φ.app (Opposite.op Z)) b₀ = (A.map (f₁.op ≫ g₁.op)) m := by
110	    dsimp [b₀]
111	    erw [NatTrans.naturality_apply φ, hb₁, Functor.map_comp, ConcreteCategory.comp_apply]
112	  have ha₀' : (α.app (Opposite.op Z)) a₀ = (R.map (f₂.op ≫ g₂.op)) r := by
113	    rw [ha₀, ← op_comp, fac, op_comp]
114	  have hb₀' : (φ.app (Opposite.op Z)) b₀ = (A.map (f₂.op ≫ g₂.op)) m := by
115	    rw [hb₀, ← op_comp, fac, op_comp]
116	  dsimp
117	  erw [← NatTrans.naturality_apply φ, ← NatTrans.naturality_apply φ]
118	  exact (isCompatible_map_smul_aux α φ hA r m f₁ g₁ a₁ a₀ b₁ b₀ ha₁ ha₀ hb₁ hb₀).trans
119	    (isCompatible_map_smul_aux α φ hA r m f₂ g₂ a₂ a₀ b₂ b₀ ha₂ ha₀' hb₂ hb₀').symm
120	
121	end

132	variable {M₀ : PresheafOfModules.{v} R₀} {A : Sheaf J AddCommGrpCat.{v}}
133	  (φ : M₀.presheaf ⟶ A.obj)
134	  [Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J φ] [Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J φ]

137	
138	variable {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (π : X ⟶ Y) (r r' : R.obj.obj X) (m m' : A.obj.obj X)
139	
140	/-- Assuming `α : R₀ ⟶ R.val` is the sheafification map of a presheaf of rings `R₀`
141	and `φ : M₀.presheaf ⟶ A.val` is the sheafification map of the underlying
142	sheaf of abelian groups of a presheaf of modules `M₀` over `R₀`, then given
143	`r : R.val.obj X` and `m : A.val.obj X`, this structure contains the data
144	of `x : A.val.obj X` along with the property which makes `x` a good candidate
145	for the definition of the scalar multiplication `r • m`. -/
146	structure SMulCandidate where
147	  /-- The candidate for the scalar product `r • m`. -/
148	  x : A.obj.obj X
149	  h ⦃Y : Cᵒᵖ⦄ (f : X ⟶ Y) (r₀ : R₀.obj Y) (hr₀ : α.app Y r₀ = R.obj.map f r)
150	    (m₀ : M₀.obj Y) (hm₀ : φ.app Y m₀ = A.obj.map f m) : A.obj.map f x = φ.app Y (r₀ • m₀)
151	
152	/-- Constructor for `SMulCandidate`. -/
153	def SMulCandidate.mk' (S : Sieve X.unop) (hS : S ∈ J X.unop)
154	    (r₀ : Presieve.FamilyOfElements (R₀ ⋙ forget _) S.arrows)
155	    (m₀ : Presieve.FamilyOfElements (M₀.presheaf ⋙ forget _) S.arrows)
156	    (hr₀ : (r₀.map (whiskerRight α (forget _))).IsAmalgamation r)
157	    (hm₀ : (m₀.map (whiskerRight φ (forget _))).IsAmalgamation m)
158	    (a : A.obj.obj X)
159	    (ha : ((r₀.smul m₀).map (whiskerRight φ (forget _))).IsAmalgamation a) :
160	    SMulCandidate α φ r m where
161	  x := a
162	  h Y f a₀ ha₀ b₀ hb₀ := by
163	    apply A.isSeparated _ _ (J.pullback_stable f.unop hS)
164	    rintro Z g hg
165	    dsimp at hg
166	    rw [← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← A.obj.map_comp, ← NatTrans.naturality_apply (D := Ab)]
167	    erw [M₀.map_smul] -- Mismatch between `M₀.map` and `M₀.presheaf.map`
168	    refine (ha _ hg).trans (app_eq_of_isLocallyInjective α φ A.isSeparated _ _ _ _ ?_ ?_)
169	    · rw [← RingCat.comp_apply, NatTrans.naturality, RingCat.comp_apply, ha₀]
170	      apply (hr₀ _ hg).symm.trans
171	      simp
172	      rfl
173	    · erw [NatTrans.naturality_apply φ, hb₀]
174	      apply (hm₀ _ hg).symm.trans
175	      dsimp
176	      rw [Functor.map_comp]
177	      rfl
178	
179	instance : Nonempty (SMulCandidate α φ r m) := ⟨by
180	  let S := (Presheaf.imageSieve α r ⊓ Presheaf.imageSieve φ m)
181	  have hS : S ∈ J _ := by
182	    apply J.intersection_covering
183	    all_goals apply Presheaf.imageSieve_mem
184	  have h₁ : S ≤ Presheaf.imageSieve α r := fun _ _ h => h.1
185	  have h₂ : S ≤ Presheaf.imageSieve φ m := fun _ _ h => h.2
186	  let r₀ := (Presieve.FamilyOfElements.localPreimage (whiskerRight α (forget _)) r).restrict h₁
187	  let m₀ := (Presieve.FamilyOfElements.localPreimage (whiskerRight φ (forget _)) m).restrict h₂
188	  have hr₀ : (r₀.map (whiskerRight α (forget _))).IsAmalgamation r := by
189	    rw [Presieve.FamilyOfElements.restrict_map]
190	    apply Presieve.isAmalgamation_restrict
191	    apply Presieve.FamilyOfElements.isAmalgamation_map_localPreimage
192	  have hm₀ : (m₀.map (whiskerRight φ (forget _))).IsAmalgamation m := by
193	    rw [Presieve.FamilyOfElements.restrict_map]
194	    apply Presieve.isAmalgamation_restrict
195	    apply Presieve.FamilyOfElements.isAmalgamation_map_localPreimage
196	  exact SMulCandidate.mk' α φ r m S hS r₀ m₀ hr₀ hm₀ _ (Presieve.IsSheafFor.isAmalgamation
197	    (((sheafCompose J (forget _)).obj A).2.isSheafFor S hS)
198	    (Presieve.FamilyOfElements.isCompatible_map_smul α φ A.isSeparated r m r₀ m₀ hr₀ hm₀))⟩
199	
200	instance : Subsingleton (SMulCandidate α φ r m) where

17	/-!
18	# Flat modules
19	
20	A module `M` over a commutative semiring `R` is *mono-flat* if for all monomorphisms of modules
21	(i.e., injective linear maps) `N →ₗ[R] P`, the canonical map `N ⊗ M → P ⊗ M` is injective
22	(cf. [Katsov2004], [KatsovNam2011]).
23	To show a module is mono-flat, it suffices to check inclusions of finitely generated
24	submodules `N` into finitely generated modules `P`, and `P` can be further assumed to lie in
25	the same universe as `R`.
26	
27	`M` is flat if `· ⊗ M` preserves finite limits (equivalently, pullbacks, or equalizers).
28	If `R` is a ring, an `R`-module `M` is flat if and only if it is mono-flat, and to show
29	a module is flat, it suffices to check inclusions of finitely generated ideals into `R`.
30	See <https://stacks.math.columbia.edu/tag/00HD>.
31	
32	Currently, `Module.Flat` is defined to be equivalent to mono-flatness over a semiring.
33	It is left as a TODO item to introduce the genuine flatness over semirings and rename
34	the current `Module.Flat` to `Module.MonoFlat`.
35	
36	## Main declaration
37	
38	* `Module.Flat`: the predicate asserting that an `R`-module `M` is flat.
39	
40	## Main theorems
41	
42	* `Module.Flat.of_retract`: retracts of flat modules are flat
43	* `Module.Flat.of_linearEquiv`: modules linearly equivalent to a flat module are flat
44	* `Module.Flat.directSum`: arbitrary direct sums of flat modules are flat
45	* `Module.Flat.of_free`: free modules are flat
46	* `Module.Flat.of_projective`: projective modules are flat
47	* `Module.Flat.preserves_injective_linearMap`: If `M` is a flat module then tensoring with `M`
48	  preserves injectivity of linear maps. This lemma is fully universally polymorphic in all
49	  arguments, i.e. `R`, `M` and linear maps `N → N'` can all have different universe levels.
50	* `Module.Flat.iff_rTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap`: a module is flat iff tensoring modules
51	  in the higher universe preserves injectivity.
52	* `Module.Flat.lTensor_exact`: If `M` is a flat module then tensoring with `M` is an exact
53	  functor. This lemma is fully universally polymorphic in all arguments, i.e.
54	  `R`, `M` and linear maps `N → N' → N''` can all have different universe levels.
55	* `Module.Flat.iff_lTensor_exact`: a module is flat iff tensoring modules
56	  in the higher universe is an exact functor.
57	
58	## TODO
59	
60	* Generalize flatness to noncommutative semirings.
61	
62	-/
63

80	/-! ### Flatness over a semiring -/
81	
82	variable {R : Type u} {M : Type v} {N P Q : Type*} [CommSemiring R] [AddCommMonoid M] [Module R M]
83	  [AddCommMonoid N] [Module R N] [AddCommMonoid P] [Module R P] [AddCommMonoid Q] [Module R Q]
84	
85	theorem _root_.LinearMap.rTensor_injective_of_fg {f : N →ₗ[R] P}
86	    (h : ∀ (N' : Submodule R N) (P' : Submodule R P),
87	      N'.FG → P'.FG → ∀ h : N' ≤ P'.comap f, Function.Injective ((f.restrict h).rTensor M)) :
88	    Function.Injective (f.rTensor M) := fun x y eq ↦ by
89	  have ⟨N', Nfg, sub⟩ := Submodule.exists_fg_le_subset_range_rTensor_subtype {x, y} (by simp)
90	  obtain ⟨x, rfl⟩ := sub (.inl rfl)
91	  obtain ⟨y, rfl⟩ := sub (.inr rfl)
92	  simp_rw [← rTensor_comp_apply, show f ∘ₗ N'.subtype = (N'.map f).subtype ∘ₗ f.submoduleMap N'
93	    from rfl, rTensor_comp_apply] at eq
94	  have ⟨P', Pfg, le, eq⟩ := (Nfg.map _).exists_rTensor_fg_inclusion_eq eq
95	  simp_rw [← rTensor_comp_apply] at eq
96	  rw [h _ _ Nfg Pfg (map_le_iff_le_comap.mp le) eq]
97	
98	lemma _root_.LinearMap.rTensor_injective_iff_subtype {f : N →ₗ[R] P} (hf : Function.Injective f)
99	    (e : P ≃ₗ[R] Q) : Function.Injective (f.rTensor M) ↔
100	      Function.Injective ((range <| e.toLinearMap ∘ₗ f).subtype.rTensor M) := by
101	  simp_rw [← EquivLike.injective_comp <| (LinearEquiv.ofInjective (e.toLinearMap ∘ₗ f)
102	    (e.injective.comp hf)).rTensor M, ← EquivLike.comp_injective _ (e.rTensor M),
103	    ← LinearEquiv.coe_coe, ← coe_comp, LinearEquiv.coe_rTensor, ← rTensor_comp]
104	  rfl
105	
106	variable (R M) in
107	/-- An `R`-module `M` is flat if for every finitely generated submodule `N` of every
108	finitely generated `R`-module `P` in the same universe as `R`,
109	the canonical map `N ⊗ M → P ⊗ M` is injective. This implies the same is true for
110	arbitrary `R`-modules `N` and `P` and injective linear maps `N →ₗ[R] P`, see
111	`Flat.rTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap`. To show a module over a ring `R` is flat, it
112	suffices to consider the case `P = R`, see `Flat.iff_rTensor_injective`. -/
113	@[mk_iff] class Flat : Prop where
114	  out ⦃P : Type u⦄ [AddCommMonoid P] [Module R P] [Module.Finite R P] (N : Submodule R P) : N.FG →
115	    Function.Injective (N.subtype.rTensor M)
116	
117	namespace Flat
118	
119	/-- If `M` is a flat module, then `f ⊗ 𝟙 M` is injective for all injective linear maps `f`. -/
120	theorem rTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap [Flat R M] (f : N →ₗ[R] P)
121	    (hf : Function.Injective f) : Function.Injective (f.rTensor M) := by
122	  refine rTensor_injective_of_fg fun N P Nfg Pfg le ↦ ?_
123	  rw [← Finite.iff_fg] at Nfg Pfg
124	  have := Finite.small R P
125	  let se := (Shrink.linearEquiv R P).symm
126	  have := Module.Finite.equiv se
127	  rw [rTensor_injective_iff_subtype (fun _ _ ↦ (Subtype.ext <| hf <| Subtype.ext_iff.mp ·)) se]
128	  exact (flat_iff R M).mp ‹_› _ (Finite.iff_fg.mp inferInstance)
129	
130	/-- If `M` is a flat module, then `𝟙 M ⊗ f` is injective for all injective linear maps `f`. -/
131	theorem lTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap [Flat R M] (f : N →ₗ[R] P)
132	    (hf : Function.Injective f) : Function.Injective (f.lTensor M) :=
133	  (f.lTensor_inj_iff_rTensor_inj M).2 (rTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap f hf)
134	
135	/-- `M` is flat if and only if `f ⊗ 𝟙 M` is injective whenever `f` is an injective linear map
136	in a universe that `R` fits in. -/
137	lemma iff_rTensor_preserves_injective_linearMapₛ [Small.{v'} R] : Flat R M ↔
138	    ∀ ⦃N N' : Type v'⦄ [AddCommMonoid N] [AddCommMonoid N'] [Module R N] [Module R N']
139	      (f : N →ₗ[R] N'), Function.Injective f → Function.Injective (f.rTensor M) :=
140	  ⟨by introv _; apply rTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap, fun h ↦ ⟨fun P _ _ _ _ _ ↦ by
141	    have := Finite.small.{v'} R P
142	    rw [rTensor_injective_iff_subtype Subtype.val_injective (Shrink.linearEquiv R P).symm]
143	    exact h _ Subtype.val_injective⟩⟩
144	
145	/-- `M` is flat if and only if `𝟙 M ⊗ f` is injective whenever `f` is an injective linear map
146	in a universe that `R` fits in. -/
147	lemma iff_lTensor_preserves_injective_linearMapₛ [Small.{v'} R] : Flat R M ↔
148	    ∀ ⦃N N' : Type v'⦄ [AddCommMonoid N] [AddCommMonoid N'] [Module R N] [Module R N']
149	      (f : N →ₗ[R] N'), Function.Injective f → Function.Injective (f.lTensor M) := by
150	  simp_rw [iff_rTensor_preserves_injective_linearMapₛ, LinearMap.lTensor_inj_iff_rTensor_inj]
151	
152	/-- An easier-to-use version of `Module.flat_iff`, with finiteness conditions removed. -/
153	lemma iff_rTensor_injectiveₛ : Flat R M ↔ ∀ ⦃P : Type u⦄ [AddCommMonoid P] [Module R P]
154	    (N : Submodule R P), Function.Injective (N.subtype.rTensor M) :=
155	  ⟨fun _ _ _ _ _ ↦ rTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap _ Subtype.val_injective,
156	    fun h ↦ ⟨fun _ _ _ _ _ _ ↦ h _⟩⟩
157	
158	lemma iff_lTensor_injectiveₛ : Flat R M ↔ ∀ ⦃P : Type u⦄ [AddCommMonoid P] [Module R P]
159	    (N : Submodule R P), Function.Injective (N.subtype.lTensor M) := by
160	  simp_rw [iff_rTensor_injectiveₛ, LinearMap.lTensor_inj_iff_rTensor_inj]
161	
162	instance instSubalgebraToSubmodule {S : Type v} [Semiring S] [Algebra R S]
163	    (A : Subalgebra R S) [Flat R A] : Flat R A.toSubmodule := ‹Flat R A›
164	
165	instance self : Flat R R where
166	  out _ _ _ _ I _ := by
167	    rw [← (TensorProduct.rid R I).symm.injective_comp, ← (TensorProduct.rid R _).comp_injective]
168	    convert Subtype.coe_injective using 1
169	    ext; simp
170	
171	/-- A retract of a flat `R`-module is flat. -/
172	lemma of_retract [f : Flat R M] (i : N →ₗ[R] M) (r : M →ₗ[R] N) (h : r.comp i = LinearMap.id) :
173	    Flat R N := by
174	  rw [iff_rTensor_injectiveₛ] at *
175	  refine fun P _ _ Q ↦ .of_comp (f := lTensor P i) ?_
176	  rw [← coe_comp, lTensor_comp_rTensor, ← rTensor_comp_lTensor, coe_comp]
177	  refine (f Q).comp (Function.RightInverse.injective (g := lTensor Q r) fun x ↦ ?_)
178	  simp [← comp_apply, ← lTensor_comp, h]
179	
180	/-- An `R`-module linearly equivalent to a flat `R`-module is flat. -/
181	lemma of_linearEquiv [Flat R M] (e : N ≃ₗ[R] M) : Flat R N :=
182	  of_retract e.toLinearMap e.symm (by simp)
183	
184	/-- If an `R`-module `M` is linearly equivalent to another `R`-module `N`, then `M` is flat
185	  if and only if `N` is flat. -/
186	lemma equiv_iff (e : M ≃ₗ[R] N) : Flat R M ↔ Flat R N :=
187	  ⟨fun _ ↦ of_linearEquiv e.symm, fun _ ↦ of_linearEquiv e⟩
188	
189	instance ulift [Flat R M] : Flat R (ULift.{v'} M) :=
190	  of_linearEquiv ULift.moduleEquiv
191	
192	-- Making this an instance causes an infinite sequence `M → ULift M → ULift (ULift M) → ...`.
193	lemma of_ulift [Flat R (ULift.{v'} M)] : Flat R M :=
194	  of_linearEquiv ULift.moduleEquiv.symm
195	
196	instance shrink [Small.{v'} M] [Flat R M] : Flat R (Shrink.{v'} M) :=
197	  of_linearEquiv (Shrink.linearEquiv R M)
198	
199	-- Making this an instance causes an infinite sequence `M → Shrink M → Shrink (Shrink M) → ...`.
200	lemma of_shrink [Small.{v'} M] [Flat R (Shrink.{v'} M)] : Flat R M :=

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Exact
9	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.Maps
10	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.Quotient.Defs
11	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.TensorProduct.Maps
12	
13	/-! # Right-exactness properties of tensor product
14	
15	## Modules
16	
17	* `LinearMap.rTensor_surjective` asserts that when one tensors
18	  a surjective map on the right, one still gets a surjective linear map.
19	  More generally, `LinearMap.rTensor_range`  computes the range of
20	  `LinearMap.rTensor`
21	
22	* `LinearMap.lTensor_surjective` asserts that when one tensors
23	  a surjective map on the left, one still gets a surjective linear map.
24	  More generally, `LinearMap.lTensor_range`  computes the range of
25	  `LinearMap.lTensor`
26	
27	* `TensorProduct.rTensor_exact` says that when one tensors a short exact
28	  sequence on the right, one still gets a short exact sequence
29	  (right-exactness of `TensorProduct.rTensor`),
30	  and `rTensor.equiv` gives the LinearEquiv that follows from this
31	  combined with `LinearMap.rTensor_surjective`.
32	
33	* `TensorProduct.lTensor_exact` says that when one tensors a short exact
34	  sequence on the left, one still gets a short exact sequence
35	  (right-exactness of `TensorProduct.rTensor`)
36	  and `lTensor.equiv` gives the LinearEquiv that follows from this
37	  combined with `LinearMap.lTensor_surjective`.
38	
39	* For `N : Submodule R M`, `LinearMap.exact_subtype_mkQ N` says that
40	  the inclusion of the submodule and the quotient map form an exact pair,
41	  and `lTensor_mkQ` compute `ker (lTensor Q (N.mkQ))` and similarly for `rTensor_mkQ`
42	
43	* `TensorProduct.map_ker` computes the kernel of `TensorProduct.map f g'`
44	  in the presence of two short exact sequences.
45	
46	The proofs are those of [bourbaki1989] (chap. 2, §3, n°6)
47	
48	## Algebras
49	
50	In the case of a tensor product of algebras, these results can be particularized
51	to compute some kernels.
52	
53	* `Algebra.TensorProduct.ker_map` computes the kernel of `Algebra.TensorProduct.map f g`
54	
55	* `Algebra.TensorProduct.lTensor_ker` and `Algebra.TensorProduct.rTensor_ker`
56	  compute the kernels of `Algebra.TensorProduct.map f id` and `Algebra.TensorProduct.map id g`
57	
58	## Note on implementation
59	
60	* All kernels are computed by applying the first isomorphism theorem and
61	  establishing some isomorphisms.
62	
63	* The proofs are essentially done twice,
64	  once for `lTensor` and then for `rTensor`.
65	  It is possible to apply `TensorProduct.flip` to deduce one of them
66	  from the other.
67	  However, this approach will lead to different isomorphisms,
68	  and it is not quicker.
69	
70	* The proofs of `Ideal.map_includeLeft_eq` and `Ideal.map_includeRight_eq`
71	  could be easier if `I ⊗[R] B` was naturally an `A ⊗[R] B` module,
72	  and the map to `A ⊗[R] B` was known to be linear.
73	  This depends on the B-module structure on a tensor product
74	  whose use rapidly conflicts with everything…
75	
76	## TODO
77	
78	* Treat the noncommutative case
79	
80	* Treat the case of modules over semirings
81	  (For a possible definition of an exact sequence of commutative semigroups, see
82	  [Grillet-1969b], Pierre-Antoine Grillet,
83	  *The tensor product of commutative semigroups*,
84	  Trans. Amer. Math. Soc. 138 (1969), 281-293, doi:10.1090/S0002-9947-1969-0237688-1 .)
85	
86	-/
87	
88	@[expose] public section
89	
90	assert_not_exists Cardinal
91	
92	section Modules
93	
94	open TensorProduct LinearMap
95	
96	section Semiring
97	
98	variable {R : Type*} [CommSemiring R] {M N P Q : Type*}
99	    [AddCommMonoid M] [AddCommMonoid N] [AddCommMonoid P] [AddCommMonoid Q]
100	    [Module R M] [Module R N] [Module R P] [Module R Q]
101	    {f : M →ₗ[R] N} (g : N →ₗ[R] P)
102	
103	lemma le_comap_range_lTensor (q : Q) :
104	    LinearMap.range g ≤ (LinearMap.range (lTensor Q g)).comap (TensorProduct.mk R Q P q) := by
105	  rintro x ⟨n, rfl⟩
106	  exact ⟨q ⊗ₜ[R] n, rfl⟩
107	
108	lemma le_comap_range_rTensor (q : Q) :
109	    LinearMap.range g ≤ (LinearMap.range (rTensor Q g)).comap
110	      ((TensorProduct.mk R P Q).flip q) := by
111	  rintro x ⟨n, rfl⟩
112	  exact ⟨n ⊗ₜ[R] q, rfl⟩
113	
114	variable (Q) {g}
115	
116	/-- If `g` is surjective, then `lTensor Q g` is surjective -/
117	theorem LinearMap.lTensor_surjective (hg : Function.Surjective g) :
118	    Function.Surjective (lTensor Q g) := by
119	  intro z
120	  induction z with
121	  | zero => exact ⟨0, map_zero _⟩
122	  | tmul q p =>
123	    obtain ⟨n, rfl⟩ := hg p
124	    exact ⟨q ⊗ₜ[R] n, rfl⟩
125	  | add x y hx hy =>
126	    obtain ⟨x, rfl⟩ := hx
127	    obtain ⟨y, rfl⟩ := hy
128	    exact ⟨x + y, map_add _ _ _⟩
129	
130	theorem LinearMap.lTensor_range :
131	    range (lTensor Q g) =
132	      range (lTensor Q (Submodule.subtype (range g))) := by
133	  have : g = (Submodule.subtype _).comp g.rangeRestrict := rfl
134	  nth_rewrite 1 [this]
135	  rw [lTensor_comp]
136	  apply range_comp_of_range_eq_top
137	  rw [range_eq_top]
138	  apply lTensor_surjective
139	  rw [← range_eq_top, range_rangeRestrict]
140	
141	/-- If `g` is surjective, then `rTensor Q g` is surjective -/
142	theorem LinearMap.rTensor_surjective (hg : Function.Surjective g) :
143	    Function.Surjective (rTensor Q g) := by
144	  intro z
145	  induction z with
146	  | zero => exact ⟨0, map_zero _⟩
147	  | tmul p q =>
148	    obtain ⟨n, rfl⟩ := hg p
149	    exact ⟨n ⊗ₜ[R] q, rfl⟩
150	  | add x y hx hy =>

100	namespace GrothendieckTopology
101	
102	/-- Given a category `C` equipped with a Grothendieck topology `J` and a concrete category `A`,
103	this property holds if a morphism in `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A` satisfies `J.W` (i.e. becomes an iso after
104	sheafification) iff it is both locally injective and locally surjective. -/
105	class WEqualsLocallyBijective : Prop where
106	  iff {X Y : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f : X ⟶ Y) :
107	    J.W f ↔ Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J f ∧ Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J f
108	
109	section
110	
111	variable {A}
112	variable [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective A] {X Y : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f : X ⟶ Y)
113	
114	lemma W_iff_isLocallyBijective :
115	    J.W f ↔ Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J f ∧ Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J f := by
116	  apply WEqualsLocallyBijective.iff
117	
118	lemma W_of_isLocallyBijective [Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J f]
119	    [Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J f] : J.W f := by
120	  rw [W_iff_isLocallyBijective]
121	  constructor <;> infer_instance
122	
123	variable {J f}
124	
125	lemma W.isLocallyInjective (hf : J.W f) : Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J f :=
126	  ((J.W_iff_isLocallyBijective f).1 hf).1
127	
128	lemma W.isLocallySurjective (hf : J.W f) : Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J f :=
129	  ((J.W_iff_isLocallyBijective f).1 hf).2
130	
131	variable [HasWeakSheafify J A] (P : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A)
132	
133	instance : Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J (CategoryTheory.toSheafify J P) :=
134	  (J.W_toSheafify P).isLocallyInjective
135	
136	instance : Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J (CategoryTheory.toSheafify J P) :=
137	  (J.W_toSheafify P).isLocallySurjective
138	
139	end
140	
141	lemma WEqualsLocallyBijective.mk' [HasWeakSheafify J A] [(forget A).ReflectsIsomorphisms]
142	    [J.HasSheafCompose (forget A)]
143	    [∀ (P : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A), Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J (CategoryTheory.toSheafify J P)]
144	    [∀ (P : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A), Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J (CategoryTheory.toSheafify J P)] :
145	    J.WEqualsLocallyBijective A where
146	  iff {P Q} f := by
147	    rw [W_iff, ← Sheaf.isLocallyBijective_iff_isIso (A := A),
148	      ← Presheaf.isLocallyInjective_comp_iff J f (CategoryTheory.toSheafify J Q),
149	      ← Presheaf.isLocallySurjective_comp_iff J f (CategoryTheory.toSheafify J Q),

8	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Colimits
9	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Monoidal.Closed
10	
11	/-!
12	# The monoidal category structure on presheaves of modules
13	
14	Given a presheaf of commutative rings `R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat`, we construct
15	the monoidal category structure on the category of presheaves of modules
16	`PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)`. The tensor product `M₁ ⊗ M₂` is defined
17	as the presheaf of modules which sends `X : Cᵒᵖ` to `M₁.obj X ⊗ M₂.obj X`.
18	
19	## Notes
20	
21	This contribution was created as part of the AIM workshop
22	"Formalizing algebraic geometry" in June 2024.
23	
24	-/
25	
26	@[expose] public section
27	
28	open CategoryTheory MonoidalCategory BraidedCategory Category Limits
29	
30	universe v u v₁ u₁
31	
32	variable {C : Type*} [Category* C] {R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
33	
34	instance (X : Cᵒᵖ) : CommRing ((R ⋙ forget₂ _ RingCat).obj X) :=
35	  inferInstanceAs (CommRing (R.obj X))
36	
37	namespace PresheafOfModules
38	
39	namespace Monoidal
40	
41	variable (M₁ M₂ M₃ M₄ : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
42	
43	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
44	/-- Auxiliary definition for `tensorObj`. -/
45	noncomputable def tensorObjMap {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y) : M₁.obj X ⊗ M₂.obj X ⟶
46	    (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (R.map f).hom).obj (M₁.obj Y ⊗ M₂.obj Y) :=
47	  ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensorLift (fun m₁ m₂ ↦ M₁.map f m₁ ⊗ₜ M₂.map f m₂)
48	    (by
49	      intro m₁ m₁' m₂
50	      dsimp +instances
51	      rw [map_add, TensorProduct.add_tmul])
52	    (by intro a m₁ m₂; dsimp; erw [M₁.map_smul]; rfl)
53	    (by
54	      intro m₁ m₂ m₂'
55	      dsimp +instances
56	      rw [map_add, TensorProduct.tmul_add])
57	    (by intro a m₁ m₂; dsimp; erw [M₂.map_smul, TensorProduct.tmul_smul (r := R.map f a)]; rfl)
58	
59	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
60	/-- The tensor product of two presheaves of modules. -/
61	@[simps obj]
62	noncomputable def tensorObj : PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _) where
63	  obj X := M₁.obj X ⊗ M₂.obj X
64	  map f := tensorObjMap M₁ M₂ f
65	  map_id X := ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (by
66	    intro m₁ m₂
67	    dsimp [tensorObjMap]
68	    simp
69	    rfl) -- `ModuleCat.restrictScalarsId'App_inv_apply` doesn't get picked up due to type mismatch
70	  map_comp f g := ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (by
71	    intro m₁ m₂
72	    dsimp [tensorObjMap]
73	    simp +instances)
74	
75	variable {M₁ M₂ M₃ M₄}
76	
77	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
78	@[simp]
79	lemma tensorObj_map_tmul {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y) (m₁ : M₁.obj X) (m₂ : M₂.obj X) :
80	    DFunLike.coe (α := (M₁.obj X ⊗ M₂.obj X :))
81	      (β := fun _ ↦ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (R.map f).hom).obj (M₁.obj Y ⊗ M₂.obj Y))
82	      (ModuleCat.Hom.hom (R := ↑(R.obj X)) ((tensorObj M₁ M₂).map f)) (m₁ ⊗ₜ[R.obj X] m₂) =
83	    M₁.map f m₁ ⊗ₜ[R.obj Y] M₂.map f m₂ := rfl
84	
85	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
86	/-- The tensor product of two morphisms of presheaves of modules. -/
87	@[simps]
88	noncomputable def tensorHom (f : M₁ ⟶ M₂) (g : M₃ ⟶ M₄) : tensorObj M₁ M₃ ⟶ tensorObj M₂ M₄ where
89	  app X := f.app X ⊗ₘ g.app X
90	  naturality {X Y} φ := ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (fun m₁ m₃ ↦ by
91	    dsimp
92	    rw [tensorObj_map_tmul]
93	    -- Need `erw` because of the type mismatch in `map` and the tensor product.
94	    erw [ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensorHom_tmul, tensorObj_map_tmul]
95	    rw [naturality_apply, naturality_apply]
96	    simp)
97	
98	end Monoidal
99	
100	open Monoidal
101	
102	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
103	open ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory in
104	noncomputable instance monoidalCategoryStruct :
105	    MonoidalCategoryStruct (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)) where
106	  tensorObj := tensorObj
107	  whiskerLeft _ _ _ g := tensorHom (𝟙 _) g
108	  whiskerRight f _ := tensorHom f (𝟙 _)
109	  tensorHom := tensorHom
110	  tensorUnit := unit _
111	  associator M₁ M₂ M₃ := isoMk (fun _ ↦ α_ _ _ _)
112	    (fun _ _ _ ↦ ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext₃' (by intros; rfl))
113	  leftUnitor M := Iso.symm (isoMk (fun _ ↦ (λ_ _).symm) (fun X Y f ↦ by
114	    ext m
115	    dsimp [CommRingCat.forgetToRingCat_obj]
116	    erw [leftUnitor_inv_apply, leftUnitor_inv_apply, tensorObj_map_tmul, (R.map f).hom.map_one]
117	    rfl))
118	  rightUnitor M := Iso.symm (isoMk (fun _ ↦ (ρ_ _).symm) (fun X Y f ↦ by
119	    ext m
120	    dsimp [CommRingCat.forgetToRingCat_obj]
121	    erw [rightUnitor_inv_apply, rightUnitor_inv_apply, tensorObj_map_tmul, (R.map f).hom.map_one]
122	    rfl))
123	
124	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
125	noncomputable instance monoidalCategory :
126	    MonoidalCategory (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)) where
127	  tensorHom_def _ _ := by ext1; apply tensorHom_def
128	  id_tensorHom_id _ _ := by ext1; apply id_tensorHom_id
129	  tensorHom_comp_tensorHom _ _ _ _ := by ext1; apply tensorHom_comp_tensorHom
130	  whiskerLeft_id M₁ M₂ := by
131	    ext1 X
132	    apply MonoidalCategory.whiskerLeft_id (C := ModuleCat (R.obj X))
133	  id_whiskerRight _ _ := by
134	    ext1 X
135	    apply MonoidalCategory.id_whiskerRight (C := ModuleCat (R.obj X))
136	  associator_naturality _ _ _ := by ext1; apply associator_naturality
137	  leftUnitor_naturality _ := by ext1; apply leftUnitor_naturality
138	  rightUnitor_naturality _ := by ext1; apply rightUnitor_naturality
139	  pentagon _ _ _ _ := by ext1; apply pentagon
140	  triangle _ _ := by ext1; apply triangle
141	
142	open BraidedCategory
143	
144	instance symmetricCategory :
145	    SymmetricCategory (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)) where
146	  braiding M₁ M₂ :=
147	    isoMk (fun X ↦ braiding (C := ModuleCat (R.obj X)) (M₁.obj X) (M₂.obj X))
148	      (fun _ _ f ↦ ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (fun _ _ ↦ rfl))
149	  braiding_naturality_right _ _ _ _ := by
150	    ext : 1
151	    exact ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (fun _ _ ↦ rfl)
152	  braiding_naturality_left _ _ := by
153	    ext : 1
154	    exact ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (fun _ _ ↦ rfl)
155	  hexagon_forward _ _ _ := by
156	    ext : 1
157	    apply hexagon_forward (C := ModuleCat (R.obj _))
158	  hexagon_reverse _ _ _ := by
159	    ext : 1
160	    apply hexagon_reverse (C := ModuleCat (R.obj _))
161	  symmetry _ _ := by
162	    ext : 1
163	    apply SymmetricCategory.symmetry (C := ModuleCat (R.obj _))
164	
165	section
166	
167	variable (M₁ M₂ M₃ M₄ : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
168	
169	lemma tensorObj_obj (X : Cᵒᵖ) :
170	    (M₁ ⊗ M₂).obj X =
171	      MonoidalCategory.tensorObj (C := ModuleCat (R.obj X)) (M₁.obj X) (M₂.obj X) := rfl
172	
173	attribute [local simp] tensorObj_obj
174	
175	variable {M₂ M₃} in
176	@[simp]
177	lemma whiskerLeft_app (f : M₂ ⟶ M₃) (X : Cᵒᵖ) :
178	    dsimp% (M₁ ◁ f).app X = whiskerLeft (C := ModuleCat (R.obj X)) (M₁.obj X) (f.app X) :=
179	  rfl
180	
181	variable {M₁ M₂} in
182	@[simp]
183	lemma whiskerRight_app (f : M₁ ⟶ M₂) (M₃ : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)) (X : Cᵒᵖ) :
184	    dsimp% (f ▷ M₃).app X = whiskerRight (C := ModuleCat (R.obj X)) (f.app X) (M₃.obj X) := rfl
185	
186	variable {M₁ M₂ M₃ M₄} in
187	@[simp]
188	lemma tensorHom_app (f : M₁ ⟶ M₂) (g : M₃ ⟶ M₄) (X : Cᵒᵖ) :
189	    dsimp% (f ⊗ₘ g).app X =
190	      MonoidalCategory.tensorHom (C := ModuleCat (R.obj X)) (f.app X) (g.app X) := rfl
191	
192	@[simp]
193	lemma leftUnitor_hom_app (X : Cᵒᵖ) :
194	    dsimp% (λ_ M₁).hom.app X = (leftUnitor (C := ModuleCat (R.obj X)) (M₁.obj X)).hom :=
195	  rfl
196	
197	@[simp]
198	lemma leftUnitor_inv_app (X : Cᵒᵖ) :
199	    dsimp% (λ_ M₁).inv.app X = (leftUnitor (C := ModuleCat (R.obj X)) (M₁.obj X)).inv := by
200	  rfl
201	
202	@[simp]
203	lemma rightUnitor_hom_app (X : Cᵒᵖ) :
204	    dsimp% (ρ_ M₁).hom.app X = (rightUnitor (C := ModuleCat (R.obj X)) (M₁.obj X)).hom :=
205	  rfl
206	
207	@[simp]
208	lemma rightUnitor_inv_app (X : Cᵒᵖ) :
209	    dsimp% (ρ_ M₁).inv.app X = (rightUnitor (C := ModuleCat (R.obj X)) (M₁.obj X)).inv :=
210	  rfl
211	
212	@[simp]
213	lemma associator_hom_app (X : Cᵒᵖ) :
214	    (α_ M₁ M₂ M₃).hom.app X =
215	      (associator (C := ModuleCat (R.obj X)) (M₁.obj X) (M₂.obj X) (M₃.obj X)).hom :=
216	  rfl
217	
218	@[simp]
219	lemma associator_inv_app (X : Cᵒᵖ) :
220	    (α_ M₁ M₂ M₃).inv.app X =
221	      (associator (C := ModuleCat (R.obj X)) (M₁.obj X) (M₂.obj X) (M₃.obj X)).inv :=
222	  rfl
223	
224	@[simp]
225	lemma braiding_hom_app (X : Cᵒᵖ) :
226	    dsimp% (braiding M₁ M₂).hom.app X =
227	      (braiding (C := ModuleCat (R.obj X)) (M₁.obj X) (M₂.obj X)).hom := by
228	  rfl
229	
230	@[simp]
231	lemma braiding_inv_app (X : Cᵒᵖ) :
232	    dsimp% (braiding M₁ M₂).inv.app X =
233	      (braiding (C := ModuleCat (R.obj X)) (M₁.obj X) (M₂.obj X)).inv := rfl
234	
235	end
236	
237	instance (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)) :
238	    PreservesColimitsOfSize.{u, u} (tensorLeft F) where
239	  preservesColimitsOfShape := ⟨⟨fun hc ↦ ⟨evaluationJointlyReflectsColimits _ _
240	      (fun X ↦ isColimitOfPreserves (tensorLeft (show ModuleCat (R.obj X) from F.obj X))
241	        (isColimitOfPreserves (evaluation _ X) hc))⟩⟩⟩
242	
243	instance (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)) :
244	    PreservesColimitsOfSize.{u, u} (tensorRight F) :=
245	  preservesColimits_of_natIso (tensorLeftIsoTensorRight F)
246	
247	end PresheafOfModules
