4	Authors: Kevin Buzzard, Kim Morrison, Jakob von Raumer
5	-/
6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Basic
9	public import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.TensorProduct.Associator
10	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Linear
11	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Transport
12	
13	/-!
14	# The monoidal category structure on R-modules
15	
16	Mostly this uses existing machinery in `LinearAlgebra.TensorProduct`.
17	We just need to provide a few small missing pieces to build the
18	`MonoidalCategory` instance.
19	The `SymmetricCategory` instance is in `Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Monoidal.Symmetric`
20	to reduce imports.
21	
22	Note the universe level of the modules must be at least the universe level of the ring,
23	so that we have a monoidal unit.
24	For now, we simplify by insisting both universe levels are the same.
25	
26	We construct the monoidal closed structure on `ModuleCat R` in
27	`Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Monoidal.Closed`.
28	
29	If you're happy using the bundled `ModuleCat R`, it may be possible to mostly
30	use this as an interface and not need to interact much with the implementation details.
31	-/
32	
33	@[expose] public section
34	
35	universe v w x u
36	
37	open CategoryTheory
38	
39	namespace SemimoduleCat
40	
41	variable {R : Type u} [CommSemiring R]
42	
43	namespace MonoidalCategory
44	
45	-- The definitions inside this namespace are essentially private.
46	-- After we build the `MonoidalCategory (Module R)` instance,
47	-- you should use that API.
48	open TensorProduct
49	
50	attribute [local ext] TensorProduct.ext
51	
52	/-- (implementation) tensor product of R-modules -/
53	def tensorObj (M N : SemimoduleCat R) : SemimoduleCat R :=
54	  SemimoduleCat.of R (M ⊗[R] N)
55	
56	/-- (implementation) tensor product of morphisms R-modules -/
57	def tensorHom {M N M' N' : SemimoduleCat R} (f : M ⟶ N) (g : M' ⟶ N') :
58	    tensorObj M M' ⟶ tensorObj N N' :=
59	  ofHom <| TensorProduct.map f.hom g.hom
60	
61	/-- (implementation) left whiskering for R-modules -/
62	def whiskerLeft (M : SemimoduleCat R) {N₁ N₂ : SemimoduleCat R} (f : N₁ ⟶ N₂) :
63	    tensorObj M N₁ ⟶ tensorObj M N₂ :=
64	  ofHom <| f.hom.lTensor M
65	
66	/-- (implementation) right whiskering for R-modules -/
67	def whiskerRight {M₁ M₂ : SemimoduleCat R} (f : M₁ ⟶ M₂) (N : SemimoduleCat R) :
68	    tensorObj M₁ N ⟶ tensorObj M₂ N :=
69	  ofHom <| f.hom.rTensor N
70	
71	theorem id_tensorHom_id (M N : SemimoduleCat R) :
72	    tensorHom (𝟙 M) (𝟙 N) = 𝟙 (SemimoduleCat.of R (M ⊗ N)) := by
73	  ext : 1
74	  -- Porting note (https://github.com/leanprover-community/mathlib4/issues/11041): even with high priority `ext` fails to find this.
75	  apply TensorProduct.ext
76	  rfl
77	
78	theorem tensorHom_comp_tensorHom {X₁ Y₁ Z₁ X₂ Y₂ Z₂ : SemimoduleCat R} (f₁ : X₁ ⟶ Y₁) (f₂ : X₂ ⟶ Y₂)
79	    (g₁ : Y₁ ⟶ Z₁) (g₂ : Y₂ ⟶ Z₂) :
80	    tensorHom f₁ f₂ ≫ tensorHom g₁ g₂ = tensorHom (f₁ ≫ g₁) (f₂ ≫ g₂) := by
81	  ext : 1
82	  -- Porting note (https://github.com/leanprover-community/mathlib4/issues/11041): even with high priority `ext` fails to find this.
83	  apply TensorProduct.ext
84	  rfl
85	
86	/-- (implementation) the associator for R-modules -/
87	def associator (M : SemimoduleCat.{v} R) (N : SemimoduleCat.{w} R) (K : SemimoduleCat.{x} R) :
88	    tensorObj (tensorObj M N) K ≅ tensorObj M (tensorObj N K) :=
89	  (TensorProduct.assoc R M N K).toModuleIsoₛ
90	
91	/-- (implementation) the left unitor for R-modules -/
92	def leftUnitor (M : SemimoduleCat.{u} R) : SemimoduleCat.of R (R ⊗[R] M) ≅ M :=
93	  (TensorProduct.lid R M).toModuleIsoₛ
94	
95	/-- (implementation) the right unitor for R-modules -/
96	def rightUnitor (M : SemimoduleCat.{u} R) : SemimoduleCat.of R (M ⊗[R] R) ≅ M :=
97	  (TensorProduct.rid R M).toModuleIsoₛ
98	
99	@[simps -isSimp]
100	instance instMonoidalCategoryStruct : MonoidalCategoryStruct (SemimoduleCat.{u} R) where
101	  tensorObj := tensorObj
102	  whiskerLeft := whiskerLeft
103	  whiskerRight := whiskerRight
104	  tensorHom := tensorHom
105	  tensorUnit := SemimoduleCat.of R R
106	  associator := associator
107	  leftUnitor := leftUnitor
108	  rightUnitor := rightUnitor
109	
110	theorem associator_naturality {X₁ X₂ X₃ Y₁ Y₂ Y₃ : SemimoduleCat R} (f₁ : X₁ ⟶ Y₁) (f₂ : X₂ ⟶ Y₂)
111	    (f₃ : X₃ ⟶ Y₃) :
112	    tensorHom (tensorHom f₁ f₂) f₃ ≫ (associator Y₁ Y₂ Y₃).hom =
113	      (associator X₁ X₂ X₃).hom ≫ tensorHom f₁ (tensorHom f₂ f₃) := by
114	  ext : 1
115	  apply TensorProduct.ext_threefold
116	  intro x y z
117	  rfl
118	
119	theorem pentagon (W X Y Z : SemimoduleCat R) :
120	    whiskerRight (associator W X Y).hom Z ≫
121	        (associator W (tensorObj X Y) Z).hom ≫ whiskerLeft W (associator X Y Z).hom =
122	      (associator (tensorObj W X) Y Z).hom ≫ (associator W X (tensorObj Y Z)).hom := by
123	  ext : 1
124	  apply TensorProduct.ext_fourfold
125	  intro w x y z
126	  rfl
127	
128	theorem leftUnitor_naturality {M N : SemimoduleCat R} (f : M ⟶ N) :
129	    tensorHom (𝟙 (SemimoduleCat.of R R)) f ≫ (leftUnitor N).hom = (leftUnitor M).hom ≫ f := by
130	  ext : 1
131	  -- Porting note (https://github.com/leanprover-community/mathlib4/issues/11041): broken ext
132	  apply TensorProduct.ext
133	  ext
134	  simp [tensorHom, tensorObj, leftUnitor]
135	
136	theorem rightUnitor_naturality {M N : SemimoduleCat R} (f : M ⟶ N) :
137	    tensorHom f (𝟙 (SemimoduleCat.of R R)) ≫ (rightUnitor N).hom = (rightUnitor M).hom ≫ f := by
138	  ext : 1
139	  -- Porting note (https://github.com/leanprover-community/mathlib4/issues/11041): broken ext
140	  apply TensorProduct.ext
141	  ext
142	  simp [tensorHom, tensorObj, rightUnitor]
143	
144	theorem triangle (M N : SemimoduleCat.{u} R) :
145	    (associator M (SemimoduleCat.of R R) N).hom ≫ tensorHom (𝟙 M) (leftUnitor N).hom =
146	      tensorHom (rightUnitor M).hom (𝟙 N) := by
147	  ext : 1
148	  apply TensorProduct.ext_threefold
149	  intro x y
150	  exact TensorProduct.tmul_smul _ _
151	
152	end MonoidalCategory
153	
154	open MonoidalCategory
155	
156	instance monoidalCategory : MonoidalCategory (SemimoduleCat.{u} R) := MonoidalCategory.ofTensorHom
157	  (id_tensorHom_id := fun M N ↦ id_tensorHom_id M N)
158	  (tensorHom_comp_tensorHom := fun f g h ↦ MonoidalCategory.tensorHom_comp_tensorHom f g h)
159	  (associator_naturality := fun f g h ↦ MonoidalCategory.associator_naturality f g h)
160	  (leftUnitor_naturality := fun f ↦ MonoidalCategory.leftUnitor_naturality f)
161	  (rightUnitor_naturality := fun f ↦ rightUnitor_naturality f)
162	  (pentagon := fun M N K L ↦ pentagon M N K L)
163	  (triangle := fun M N ↦ triangle M N)
164	
165	/-- Remind ourselves that the monoidal unit, being just `R`, is still a commutative semiring. -/
166	instance : CommSemiring ((𝟙_ (SemimoduleCat.{u} R) : SemimoduleCat.{u} R) : Type u) :=
167	  inferInstanceAs <| CommSemiring R
168	
169	theorem hom_tensorHom {K L M N : SemimoduleCat.{u} R} (f : K ⟶ L) (g : M ⟶ N) :
170	    (f ⊗ₘ g).hom = TensorProduct.map f.hom g.hom :=
171	  rfl
172	
173	theorem hom_whiskerLeft (L : SemimoduleCat.{u} R) {M N : SemimoduleCat.{u} R} (f : M ⟶ N) :

258	variable (f : M₁ → M₂ → M₃) (h₁ : ∀ m₁ m₂ n, f (m₁ + m₂) n = f m₁ n + f m₂ n)
259	  (h₂ : ∀ (a : R) m n, f (a • m) n = a • f m n)
260	  (h₃ : ∀ m n₁ n₂, f m (n₁ + n₂) = f m n₁ + f m n₂)
261	  (h₄ : ∀ (a : R) m n, f m (a • n) = a • f m n)

263	/-- Construct for morphisms from the tensor product of two objects in `SemimoduleCat`. -/
264	def tensorLift : M₁ ⊗ M₂ ⟶ M₃ :=
265	  ofHom <| TensorProduct.lift (LinearMap.mk₂ R f h₁ h₂ h₃ h₄)
266

269	    tensorLift f h₁ h₂ h₃ h₄ (m ⊗ₜ n) = f m n := rfl

273	lemma tensor_ext {f g : M₁ ⊗ M₂ ⟶ M₃} (h : ∀ m n, f.hom (m ⊗ₜ n) = g.hom (m ⊗ₜ n)) :
274	    f = g :=
275	  hom_ext <| TensorProduct.ext (by ext; apply h)

277	/-- Extensionality lemma for morphisms from a module of the form `(M₁ ⊗ M₂) ⊗ M₃`. -/
278	lemma tensor_ext₃' {f g : (M₁ ⊗ M₂) ⊗ M₃ ⟶ M₄}
279	    (h : ∀ m₁ m₂ m₃, f (m₁ ⊗ₜ m₂ ⊗ₜ m₃) = g (m₁ ⊗ₜ m₂ ⊗ₜ m₃)) :
280	    f = g :=
281	  hom_ext <| TensorProduct.ext_threefold h
282	
283	/-- Extensionality lemma for morphisms from a module of the form `M₁ ⊗ (M₂ ⊗ M₃)`. -/
284	lemma tensor_ext₃ {f g : M₁ ⊗ (M₂ ⊗ M₃) ⟶ M₄}
285	    (h : ∀ m₁ m₂ m₃, f (m₁ ⊗ₜ (m₂ ⊗ₜ m₃)) = g (m₁ ⊗ₜ (m₂ ⊗ₜ m₃))) :
286	    f = g := by
287	  rw [← cancel_epi (α_ _ _ _).hom]
288	  exact tensor_ext₃' h

17	
18	In this file, we construct a functor
19	`PresheafOfModules.sheafification α : PresheafOfModules R₀ ⥤ SheafOfModules R`
20	for a locally bijective morphism `α : R₀ ⟶ R.val` where `R₀` is a presheaf of rings
21	and `R` a sheaf of rings.

23	sheafification functor `PresheafOfModules R.val ⥤ SheafOfModules R`.

12	/-!
13	
14	# Projective modules
15	
16	This file contains a definition of a projective module, the proof that
17	our definition is equivalent to a lifting property, and the
18	proof that all free modules are projective.
19	
20	## Main definitions
21	
22	Let `R` be a ring (or a semiring) and let `M` be an `R`-module.
23	
24	* `Module.Projective R M` : the proposition saying that `M` is a projective `R`-module.
25	
26	## Main theorems
27	
28	* `Module.projective_lifting_property` : a map from a projective module can be lifted along
29	  a surjection.
30	
31	* `Module.Projective.of_lifting_property` : If for all R-module surjections `A →ₗ B`, all
32	  maps `M →ₗ B` lift to `M →ₗ A`, then `M` is projective.
33	
34	* `Module.Projective.of_free` : Free modules are projective
35	
36	## Implementation notes
37	
38	The actual definition of projective we use is that the natural R-module map
39	from the free R-module on the type M down to M splits. This is more convenient
40	than certain other definitions which involve quantifying over universes,
41	and also universe-polymorphic (the ring and module can be in different universes).
42	
43	We require that the module sits in at least as high a universe as the ring:
44	without this, free modules don't even exist,
45	and it's unclear if projective modules are even a useful notion.
46	
47	## References
48	
49	https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Projective_module
50	
51	## TODO
52	
53	- Direct sum of two projective modules is projective.
54	- Arbitrary sum of projective modules is projective.
55	
56	All of these should be relatively straightforward.
57	
58	## Tags
59	
60	projective module
61	
62	-/
63	
64	@[expose] public section
65	
66	universe w v u
67	
68	open LinearMap hiding id
69	open Finsupp
70	
71	/- The actual implementation we choose: `P` is projective if the natural surjection
72	from the free `R`-module on `P` to `P` splits. -/
73	/-- An R-module is projective if it is a direct summand of a free module, or equivalently
74	if maps from the module lift along surjections. There are several other equivalent
75	definitions. -/
76	class Module.Projective (R : Type*) [Semiring R] (P : Type*) [AddCommMonoid P] [Module R P] :
77	    Prop where
78	  out : ∃ s : P →ₗ[R] P →₀ R, Function.LeftInverse (Finsupp.linearCombination R id) s
79	
80	namespace Module
81	
82	section Semiring
83	
84	variable {R : Type*} [Semiring R] {P : Type*} [AddCommMonoid P] [Module R P] {M : Type*}
85	  [AddCommMonoid M] [Module R M] {N : Type*} [AddCommMonoid N] [Module R N]
86	
87	theorem projective_def :
88	    Projective R P ↔ ∃ s : P →ₗ[R] P →₀ R, Function.LeftInverse (linearCombination R id) s :=
89	  ⟨fun h => h.1, fun h => ⟨h⟩⟩
90	
91	theorem projective_def' :
92	    Projective R P ↔ ∃ s : P →ₗ[R] P →₀ R, Finsupp.linearCombination R id ∘ₗ s = .id := by
93	  simp_rw [projective_def, DFunLike.ext_iff, Function.LeftInverse, comp_apply, id_apply]
94	
95	/-- A projective R-module has the property that maps from it lift along surjections. -/
96	theorem projective_lifting_property [h : Projective R P] (f : M →ₗ[R] N) (g : P →ₗ[R] N)
97	    (hf : Function.Surjective f) : ∃ h : P →ₗ[R] M, f ∘ₗ h = g := by
98	  /-
99	    Here's the first step of the proof.
100	    Recall that `X →₀ R` is Lean's way of talking about the free `R`-module

50	/-- Given a locally bijective morphism `α : R₀ ⟶ R.val` where `R₀` is a presheaf of rings
51	and `R` a sheaf of rings (i.e. `R` identifies to the sheafification of `R₀`), this is
52	the associated sheaf of modules functor `PresheafOfModules.{v} R₀ ⥤ SheafOfModules.{v} R`. -/
53	@[simps! -isSimp map]
54	noncomputable def sheafification : PresheafOfModules.{v} R₀ ⥤ SheafOfModules.{v} R where
55	  obj M₀ := sheafify α (CategoryTheory.toSheafify J M₀.presheaf)
56	  map f := sheafifyMap _ _ _ f
57	    ((toPresheaf R₀ ⋙ presheafToSheaf J AddCommGrpCat).map f)
58	      (by apply toSheafify_naturality)
59	  map_id M₀ := by
60	    ext1
61	    apply (toPresheaf _).map_injective
62	    simp
63	    rfl
64	  map_comp _ _ := by
65	    ext1
66	    apply (toPresheaf _).map_injective
67	    simp
68	    rfl

70	/-- The sheafification of presheaves of modules commutes with the functor which
71	forgets the module structures. -/
72	noncomputable def sheafificationCompToSheaf :
73	    sheafification.{v} α ⋙ SheafOfModules.toSheaf _ ≅
74	      toPresheaf _ ⋙ presheafToSheaf J AddCommGrpCat :=
75	  Iso.refl _
76	
77	/-- The sheafification of presheaves of modules commutes with the functor which
78	forgets the module structures. -/
79	noncomputable def sheafificationCompForgetCompToPresheaf :
80	    sheafification.{v} α ⋙ SheafOfModules.forget _ ⋙ toPresheaf _ ≅
81	      toPresheaf _ ⋙ presheafToSheaf J AddCommGrpCat ⋙ sheafToPresheaf J AddCommGrpCat :=
82	  Iso.refl _
83	
84	/-- The bijection between types of morphisms which is part of the adjunction
85	`sheafificationAdjunction`. -/
86	noncomputable def sheafificationHomEquiv
87	    {P : PresheafOfModules.{v} R₀} {F : SheafOfModules.{v} R} :
88	    ((sheafification α).obj P ⟶ F) ≃
89	      (P ⟶ (restrictScalars α).obj ((SheafOfModules.forget _).obj F)) := by
90	  apply sheafifyHomEquiv

92	lemma toPresheaf_map_sheafificationHomEquiv_def
93	    {P : PresheafOfModules.{v} R₀} {F : SheafOfModules.{v} R}
94	    (f : (sheafification α).obj P ⟶ F) :
95	    (toPresheaf R₀).map (sheafificationHomEquiv α f) =
96	      CategoryTheory.toSheafify J P.presheaf ≫ (toPresheaf R.obj).map f.val := rfl
97	
98	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
99	lemma toPresheaf_map_sheafificationHomEquiv
100	    {P : PresheafOfModules.{v} R₀} {F : SheafOfModules.{v} R}
101	    (f : (sheafification α).obj P ⟶ F) :
102	    (toPresheaf R₀).map (sheafificationHomEquiv α f) =
103	      (sheafificationAdjunction J AddCommGrpCat).homEquiv P.presheaf
104	        ((SheafOfModules.toSheaf _).obj F) ((SheafOfModules.toSheaf _).map f) := by
105	  rw [toPresheaf_map_sheafificationHomEquiv_def, Adjunction.homEquiv_unit]
106	  dsimp

108	lemma toSheaf_map_sheafificationHomEquiv_symm
109	    {P : PresheafOfModules.{v} R₀} {F : SheafOfModules.{v} R}
110	    (g : P ⟶ (restrictScalars α).obj ((SheafOfModules.forget _).obj F)) :
111	    (SheafOfModules.toSheaf _).map ((sheafificationHomEquiv α).symm g) =
112	      (((sheafificationAdjunction J AddCommGrpCat).homEquiv
113	        P.presheaf ((SheafOfModules.toSheaf R).obj F)).symm ((toPresheaf R₀).map g)) := by
114	  obtain ⟨f, rfl⟩ := (sheafificationHomEquiv α).surjective g
115	  apply ((sheafificationAdjunction J AddCommGrpCat).homEquiv _ _).injective
116	  rw [Equiv.apply_symm_apply, Adjunction.homEquiv_unit, Equiv.symm_apply_apply]
117	  rfl

122	`sheafification.{v} α ⊣ SheafOfModules.forget R ⋙ restrictScalars α`. -/
123	noncomputable def sheafificationAdjunction :
124	    sheafification.{v} α ⊣ SheafOfModules.forget R ⋙ restrictScalars α :=
125	  Adjunction.mkOfHomEquiv

129	        rw [Functor.map_comp]

131	          toSheaf_map_sheafificationHomEquiv_symm α g]
132	        rw [Functor.map_comp]
133	        apply (CategoryTheory.sheafificationAdjunction J
134	          AddCommGrpCat.{v}).homEquiv_naturality_left_symm
135	      homEquiv_naturality_right := fun {P₀ M N} f g ↦ by
136	        apply (toPresheaf _).map_injective
137	        erw [toPresheaf_map_sheafificationHomEquiv] }

205	variable {ι : Type v} {M : ι → Type w} [Π i, AddCommMonoid (M i)] [Π i, Module R (M i)]
206	
207	theorem directSum_iff : Flat R (⨁ i, M i) ↔ ∀ i, Flat R (M i) := by
208	  classical
209	  simp_rw [iff_rTensor_injectiveₛ, ← EquivLike.comp_injective _ (directSumRight R R _ _),
210	    ← LinearEquiv.coe_coe, ← coe_comp, directSumRight_comp_rTensor, coe_comp, LinearEquiv.coe_coe,
211	    EquivLike.injective_comp, lmap_injective]
212	  constructor <;> (intro h; intros; apply h)

214	theorem dfinsupp_iff : Flat R (Π₀ i, M i) ↔ ∀ i, Flat R (M i) := directSum_iff ..

217	instance directSum [∀ i, Flat R (M i)] : Flat R (⨁ i, M i) := directSum_iff.mpr ‹_›

235	  iff_rTensor_injectiveₛ.mpr fun P _ _ I ↦ by
236	    letI := RestrictScalars.moduleOrig R S P
237	    change Submodule S (RestrictScalars R S P) at I

245	theorem linearIndependent_one_tmul {S} [Semiring S] [Algebra R S] [Flat R S] {ι} {v : ι → M}
246	    (hv : LinearIndependent R v) : LinearIndependent S ((1 : S) ⊗ₜ[R] v ·) := by

262	/-- `M` is flat if and only if `f ⊗ 𝟙 M` is injective whenever `f` is an injective linear map.
263	  See `Module.Flat.iff_rTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap` to specialize the universe of
264	  `N, N', N''` to `Type (max u v)`. -/
265	lemma iff_rTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap' [Small.{v'} R] : Flat R M ↔
266	    ∀ ⦃N N' : Type v'⦄ [AddCommGroup N] [AddCommGroup N'] [Module R N] [Module R N']
267	      (f : N →ₗ[R] N'), Function.Injective f → Function.Injective (f.rTensor M) :=
268	  ⟨by introv _; apply rTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap, fun h ↦
269	    iff_rTensor_preserves_injective_linearMapₛ.mpr fun P N _ _ _ _ ↦ by
270	      letI := Module.addCommMonoidToAddCommGroup R (M := P)
271	      letI := Module.addCommMonoidToAddCommGroup R (M := N)
272	      apply h⟩
273	
274	/-- `M` is flat if and only if `f ⊗ 𝟙 M` is injective whenever `f` is an injective linear map.
275	  See `Module.Flat.iff_rTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap'` to generalize the universe of
276	  `N, N', N''` to any universe that is higher than `R` and `M`. -/
277	lemma iff_rTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap : Flat R M ↔
278	    ∀ ⦃N N' : Type (max u v)⦄ [AddCommGroup N] [AddCommGroup N'] [Module R N] [Module R N']
279	      (f : N →ₗ[R] N'), Function.Injective f → Function.Injective (f.rTensor M) :=
280	  iff_rTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap'

282	/-- `M` is flat if and only if `𝟙 M ⊗ f` is injective whenever `f` is an injective linear map.
283	  See `Module.Flat.iff_lTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap` to specialize the universe of
284	  `N, N', N''` to `Type (max u v)`. -/
285	lemma iff_lTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap' [Small.{v'} R] : Flat R M ↔
286	    ∀ ⦃N N' : Type v'⦄ [AddCommGroup N] [AddCommGroup N'] [Module R N] [Module R N']
287	      (f : N →ₗ[R] N'), Function.Injective f → Function.Injective (f.lTensor M) := by
288	  simp_rw [iff_rTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap', LinearMap.lTensor_inj_iff_rTensor_inj]
289	
290	/-- `M` is flat if and only if `𝟙 M ⊗ f` is injective whenever `f` is an injective linear map.
291	  See `Module.Flat.iff_lTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap'` to generalize the universe of
292	  `N, N', N''` to any universe that is higher than `R` and `M`. -/
293	lemma iff_lTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap : Flat R M ↔
294	    ∀ ⦃N N' : Type (max u v)⦄ [AddCommGroup N] [AddCommGroup N'] [Module R N] [Module R N']
295	      (f : N →ₗ[R] N'), Function.Injective f → Function.Injective (f.lTensor M) :=
296	  iff_lTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap'

299	/-- If `M` is flat then `M ⊗ -` is an exact functor. -/
300	lemma lTensor_exact [Flat R M] ⦃N N' N'' : Type*⦄
301	    [AddCommGroup N] [AddCommGroup N'] [AddCommGroup N''] [Module R N] [Module R N'] [Module R N'']
302	    ⦃f : N →ₗ[R] N'⦄ ⦃g : N' →ₗ[R] N''⦄ (exact : Function.Exact f g) :
303	    Function.Exact (f.lTensor M) (g.lTensor M) := by
304	  let π : N' →ₗ[R] N' ⧸ LinearMap.range f := Submodule.mkQ _
305	  let ι : N' ⧸ LinearMap.range f →ₗ[R] N'' :=
306	    Submodule.subtype _ ∘ₗ (LinearMap.quotKerEquivRange g).toLinearMap ∘ₗ
307	      Submodule.quotEquivOfEq (LinearMap.range f) (LinearMap.ker g)
308	        (LinearMap.exact_iff.mp exact).symm
309	  suffices exact1 : Function.Exact (f.lTensor M) (π.lTensor M) by
310	    rw [show g = ι.comp π from rfl, lTensor_comp]
311	    exact exact1.comp_injective _ (lTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap ι <| by
312	      simpa [ι, -Subtype.val_injective] using Subtype.val_injective) (map_zero _)
313	  exact _root_.lTensor_exact _ (fun x ↦ by simp [π]) Quotient.mk''_surjective
314	
315	variable (M) in

317	lemma rTensor_exact [Flat R M] ⦃N N' N'' : Type*⦄
318	    [AddCommGroup N] [AddCommGroup N'] [AddCommGroup N''] [Module R N] [Module R N'] [Module R N'']
319	    ⦃f : N →ₗ[R] N'⦄ ⦃g : N' →ₗ[R] N''⦄ (exact : Function.Exact f g) :
320	    Function.Exact (f.rTensor M) (g.rTensor M) := by
321	  let π : N' →ₗ[R] N' ⧸ LinearMap.range f := Submodule.mkQ _
322	  let ι : N' ⧸ LinearMap.range f →ₗ[R] N'' :=
323	    Submodule.subtype _ ∘ₗ (LinearMap.quotKerEquivRange g).toLinearMap ∘ₗ
324	      Submodule.quotEquivOfEq (LinearMap.range f) (LinearMap.ker g)
325	        (LinearMap.exact_iff.mp exact).symm
326	  suffices exact1 : Function.Exact (f.rTensor M) (π.rTensor M) by
327	    rw [show g = ι.comp π from rfl, rTensor_comp]
328	    exact exact1.comp_injective _ (rTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap ι <| by
329	      simpa [ι, -Subtype.val_injective] using Subtype.val_injective) (map_zero _)
330	  exact _root_.rTensor_exact M (fun x ↦ by simp [π]) Quotient.mk''_surjective

334	theorem iff_lTensor_exact' [Small.{v'} R] : Flat R M ↔
335	    ∀ ⦃N N' N'' : Type v'⦄ [AddCommGroup N] [AddCommGroup N'] [AddCommGroup N'']

342	    simp_rw [Set.mem_range, LinearMap.zero_apply, exists_const]
343	    exact (L.map_eq_zero_iff hL).trans eq_comm) x |>.mp hx

17	/-!
18	# The Picard group of a commutative ring
19	
20	This file defines the Picard group `CommRing.Pic R` of a commutative ring `R` as the type of
21	invertible `R`-modules (in the sense that `M` is invertible if there exists another `R`-module
22	`N` such that `M ⊗[R] N ≃ₗ[R] R`) up to isomorphism, equipped with tensor product as multiplication.
23	
24	## Main definition
25	
26	- `Module.Invertible R M` says that the canonical map `Mᵛ ⊗[R] M → R` is an isomorphism.
27	  To show that `M` is invertible, it suffices to provide an arbitrary `R`-module `N`
28	  and an isomorphism `N ⊗[R] M ≃ₗ[R] R`, see `Module.Invertible.right`.
29	
30	- `ClassGroup.equivPic`: the class group of a domain is isomorphic to the Picard group.
31	
32	## Main results
33	
34	- An invertible module is finite and projective (provided as instances).
35	
36	- `Module.Invertible.free_iff_linearEquiv`: an invertible module is free iff it is isomorphic to
37	  the ring, i.e. its class is trivial in the Picard group.
38	
39	- `Submodule.ker_unitsToPic`, `Submodule.range_unitsToPic`: exactness of the sequence
40	  `1 → Rˣ → Aˣ → (Submodule R A)ˣ → Pic R → Pic A` at the last two spots.
41	  See Theorem 2.4 in [RobertsSingh1993] or Exercise I.3.7(iv) and Proposition I.3.5 in [Weibel2013].
42	
43	## References
44	
45	- https://qchu.wordpress.com/2014/10/19/the-picard-groups/
46	- https://mathoverflow.net/questions/13768/what-is-the-right-definition-of-the-picard-group-of-a-commutative-ring
47	- https://mathoverflow.net/questions/375725/picard-group-vs-class-group
48	- [Weibel2013], https://sites.math.rutgers.edu/~weibel/Kbook/Kbook.I.pdf
49	- [Stacks: Picard groups of rings](https://stacks.math.columbia.edu/tag/0AFW)
50	
51	## TODO
52	
53	Show:
54	- Invertible modules over a commutative ring have the same cardinality as the ring.
55	
56	- Establish other characterizations of invertible modules, e.g. they are modules that
57	  become free of rank one when localized at every prime ideal.
58	  See [Stacks: Finite projective modules](https://stacks.math.columbia.edu/tag/00NX).
59	- Connect to invertible sheaves on `Spec R`. More generally, connect projective `R`-modules of
60	  constant finite rank to locally free sheaves on `Spec R`.
61	- Exhibit isomorphism with sheaf cohomology `H¹(Spec R, 𝓞ˣ)`.
62	-/
63	
64	@[expose] public section
65	
66	open TensorProduct
67	
68	universe u v
69	
70	variable (R : Type u) (M : Type v) (N P Q A : Type*) [CommSemiring R]
71	variable [AddCommMonoid M] [AddCommMonoid N] [AddCommMonoid P] [AddCommMonoid Q]
72	variable [Module R M] [Module R N] [Module R P] [Module R Q]
73	
74	namespace Module
75	
76	/-- An `R`-module `M` is invertible if the canonical map `Mᵛ ⊗[R] M → R` is an isomorphism,
77	where `Mᵛ` is the `R`-dual of `M`. -/
78	protected class Invertible : Prop where
79	  bijective : Function.Bijective (contractLeft R M)
80	
81	namespace Invertible
82	
83	/-- Promote the canonical map `Mᵛ ⊗[R] M → R` to a linear equivalence for invertible `M`. -/
84	noncomputable def linearEquiv [Module.Invertible R M] : Module.Dual R M ⊗[R] M ≃ₗ[R] R :=
85	  .ofBijective _ Invertible.bijective
86	
87	variable {R M N}
88	
89	section LinearEquiv
90	
91	variable (e : M ⊗[R] N ≃ₗ[R] R)
92	
93	/-- The canonical isomorphism between a module and the result of tensoring it
94	from the left by two mutually dual invertible modules. -/
95	noncomputable abbrev leftCancelEquiv : M ⊗[R] (N ⊗[R] P) ≃ₗ[R] P :=
96	  (TensorProduct.assoc R M N P).symm ≪≫ₗ e.rTensor P ≪≫ₗ TensorProduct.lid R P
97	
98	/-- The canonical isomorphism between a module and the result of tensoring it
99	from the right by two mutually dual invertible modules. -/
100	noncomputable abbrev rightCancelEquiv : (P ⊗[R] M) ⊗[R] N ≃ₗ[R] P :=
101	  TensorProduct.assoc R P M N ≪≫ₗ e.lTensor P ≪≫ₗ TensorProduct.rid R P
102	
103	variable {P Q} in
104	theorem leftCancelEquiv_comp_lTensor_comp_symm (f : P →ₗ[R] Q) :
105	    leftCancelEquiv Q e ∘ₗ (f.lTensor N).lTensor M ∘ₗ (leftCancelEquiv P e).symm = f := by
106	  rw [← LinearMap.comp_assoc, LinearEquiv.comp_toLinearMap_symm_eq]; ext; simp
107	
108	variable {P Q} in
109	theorem rightCancelEquiv_comp_rTensor_comp_symm (f : P →ₗ[R] Q) :
110	    rightCancelEquiv Q e ∘ₗ (f.rTensor M).rTensor N ∘ₗ (rightCancelEquiv P e).symm = f := by
111	  rw [← LinearMap.comp_assoc, LinearEquiv.comp_toLinearMap_symm_eq]; ext; simp
112	
113	/-- If M is invertible, `rTensorHom M` admits an inverse. -/
114	noncomputable def rTensorInv : (P ⊗[R] M →ₗ[R] Q ⊗[R] M) →ₗ[R] (P →ₗ[R] Q) :=
115	  ((rightCancelEquiv Q e).congrRight ≪≫ₗ (rightCancelEquiv P e).congrLeft _ R) ∘ₗ
116	    LinearMap.rTensorHom N
117	
118	theorem rTensorInv_leftInverse : Function.LeftInverse (rTensorInv P Q e) (.rTensorHom M) :=
119	  fun _ ↦ by
120	    simp_rw [rTensorInv, LinearEquiv.coe_trans, LinearMap.comp_apply, LinearEquiv.coe_toLinearMap]
121	    rw [← LinearEquiv.eq_symm_apply]
122	    ext; simp [LinearEquiv.congrLeft, LinearEquiv.congrRight, LinearEquiv.arrowCongrAddEquiv]
123	
124	theorem rTensorInv_injective : Function.Injective (rTensorInv P Q e) := by
125	  simpa [rTensorInv] using (rTensorInv_leftInverse _ _ <| TensorProduct.comm R N M ≪≫ₗ e).injective
126	
127	/-- If `M` is an invertible `R`-module, `(· ⊗[R] M)` is an auto-equivalence of the category
128	of `R`-modules. -/
129	@[simps!] noncomputable def rTensorEquiv : (P →ₗ[R] Q) ≃ₗ[R] (P ⊗[R] M →ₗ[R] Q ⊗[R] M) where
130	  __ := LinearMap.rTensorHom M
131	  invFun := rTensorInv P Q e
132	  left_inv := rTensorInv_leftInverse P Q e
133	  right_inv _ := rTensorInv_injective P Q e (by rw [LinearMap.toFun_eq_coe, rTensorInv_leftInverse])
134	
135	open LinearMap in
136	/-- If there is an `R`-isomorphism between `M ⊗[R] N` and `R`,
137	the induced map `M → Nᵛ` is an isomorphism. -/
138	theorem bijective_curry : Function.Bijective (curry e.toLinearMap) := by
139	  have : curry e.toLinearMap = ((TensorProduct.lid R N).congrLeft _ R ≪≫ₗ e.congrRight) ∘ₗ
140	      rTensorHom N ∘ₗ (ringLmapEquivSelf R R M).symm.toLinearMap := by
141	    rw [← LinearEquiv.toLinearMap_symm_comp_eq]; ext
142	    simp [LinearEquiv.congrLeft, LinearEquiv.congrRight, LinearEquiv.arrowCongrAddEquiv]
143	  simpa [this] using (rTensorEquiv R M <| TensorProduct.comm R N M ≪≫ₗ e).bijective
144	
145	/-- Given `M ⊗[R] N ≃ₗ[R] R`, this is the induced isomorphism `M ≃ₗ[R] Nᵛ`. -/
146	noncomputable def linearEquivDual : M ≃ₗ[R] Dual R N := .ofBijective _ (bijective_curry e)
147	
148	include e
149	
150	protected theorem right : Module.Invertible R N where

```lean
structure PresheafOfModules {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] (R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}) where
  obj (X : Cᵒᵖ) : ModuleCat.{v} (R.obj X)
  map {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y) : obj X ⟶ 
    (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (R.map f).hom).obj (obj Y)
  map_id (X : Cᵒᵖ) : ... (functoriality condition)
  map_comp {X Y Z : Cᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) : ... (functoriality condition)
```
**Key:**

- `.map f : obj X ⟶ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (R.map f).hom).obj (obj Y)` — restriction of scalars along ring morphism

**Location:** Same file, line 83

```lean
structure Hom (M₁ M₂ : PresheafOfModules.{v} R) where

```

### 2. **Locally Injective & Locally Surjective Morphisms**

#### **IsLocallyInjective**
**Declaration:** `CategoryTheory.Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective`

**Type:**
```lean
class IsLocallyInjective {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] (J : GrothendieckTopology C)
    {D : Type u'} [Category.{v'} D] [ConcreteCategory D]
    {F₁ F₂ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ D} (φ : F₁ ⟶ F₂) : Prop where
  equalizerSieve_mem {X : Cᵒᵖ} (x y : ToType (F₁.obj X)) (h : φ.app X x = φ.app X y) :
    equalizerSieve x y ∈ J X.unop
```
**Definition:** Two sections `x, y : F₁.obj X` are equal locally if their equalizer sieve (sieve of morphisms where they agree after pulling back) is a covering sieve.

**Key Constructor:** `isLocallyInjective_of_injective`  
**Location:** Line 80  
**Signature:**
```lean
lemma isLocallyInjective_of_injective (hφ : ∀ (X : Cᵒᵖ), Function.Injective (φ.app X)) :
    IsLocallyInjective J φ
```
**Usage:** If `φ.app X` is sectionwise injective for all X, then φ is locally injective.

**Useful API:** `equalizerSieve_mem` (line 75)  
**Signature:**
```lean
lemma equalizerSieve_mem [IsLocallyInjective J φ]
    {X : Cᵒᵖ} (x y : ToType (F₁.obj X)) (h : φ.app X x = φ.app X y) :
    equalizerSieve x y ∈ J X.unop
```

content:
1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2024 Joël Riou. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Joël Riou
5	-/
6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.LeftExact
9	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.PreservesSheafification
10	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Subsheaf
11	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Whiskering
12	
13	/-!
14	# Locally injective morphisms of (pre)sheaves
15	
16	Let `C` be a category equipped with a Grothendieck topology `J`,
17	and let `D` be a concrete category. In this file, we introduce the typeclass
18	`Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J φ` for a morphism `φ : F₁ ⟶ F₂` in the category
19	`Cᵒᵖ ⥤ D`. This means that `φ` is locally injective. More precisely,
20	if `x` and `y` are two elements of some `F₁.obj U` such
21	the images of `x` and `y` in `F₂.obj U` coincide, then
22	the equality `x = y` must hold locally, i.e. after restriction
23	by the maps of a covering sieve.
24	
25	-/
26	
27	@[expose] public section
28	
29	universe w v' v u' u
30	
31	namespace CategoryTheory
32	
33	open Opposite Limits
34	
35	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C]
36	  {D : Type u'} [Category.{v'} D] {FD : D → D → Type*} {CD : D → Type w}
37	  [∀ X Y, FunLike (FD X Y) (CD X) (CD Y)] [ConcreteCategory.{w} D FD]
38	  (J : GrothendieckTopology C)
39	
40	namespace Presheaf
41	
42	/-- If `F : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ D` is a presheaf with values in a concrete category, if `x` and `y` are
43	elements in `F.obj X`, this is the sieve of `X.unop` consisting of morphisms `f`
44	such that `F.map f.op x = F.map f.op y`. -/
45	@[simps]
46	def equalizerSieve {F : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ D} {X : Cᵒᵖ} (x y : ToType (F.obj X)) : Sieve X.unop where
47	  arrows _ f := F.map f.op x = F.map f.op y
48	  downward_closed {X Y} f hf g := by
49	    dsimp at hf ⊢
50	    simp [hf]
51	
52	@[simp]
53	lemma equalizerSieve_self_eq_top {F : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ D} {X : Cᵒᵖ} (x : ToType (F.obj X)) :
54	    equalizerSieve x x = ⊤ := by aesop
55	
56	@[simp]
57	lemma equalizerSieve_eq_top_iff {F : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ D} {X : Cᵒᵖ} (x y : ToType (F.obj X)) :
58	    equalizerSieve x y = ⊤ ↔ x = y := by
59	  constructor
60	  · intro h
61	    simpa using (show equalizerSieve x y (𝟙 _) by simp [h])
62	  · rintro rfl
63	    apply equalizerSieve_self_eq_top
64	
65	variable {F₁ F₂ F₃ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ D} (φ : F₁ ⟶ F₂) (ψ : F₂ ⟶ F₃)
66	
67	/-- A morphism `φ : F₁ ⟶ F₂` of presheaves `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ D` (with `D` a concrete category)
68	is locally injective for a Grothendieck topology `J` on `C` if
69	whenever two sections of `F₁` are sent to the same section of `F₂`, then these two
70	sections coincide locally. -/
71	class IsLocallyInjective : Prop where
72	  equalizerSieve_mem {X : Cᵒᵖ} (x y : ToType (F₁.obj X)) (h : φ.app X x = φ.app X y) :
73	    equalizerSieve x y ∈ J X.unop
74	
75	lemma equalizerSieve_mem [IsLocallyInjective J φ]
76	    {X : Cᵒᵖ} (x y : ToType (F₁.obj X)) (h : φ.app X x = φ.app X y) :
77	    equalizerSieve x y ∈ J X.unop :=
78	  IsLocallyInjective.equalizerSieve_mem x y h
79	
80	lemma isLocallyInjective_of_injective (hφ : ∀ (X : Cᵒᵖ), Function.Injective (φ.app X)) :
81	    IsLocallyInjective J φ where
82	  equalizerSieve_mem {X} x y h := by
83	    convert J.top_mem X.unop
84	    ext Y f
85	    simp only [equalizerSieve_apply, op_unop, Sieve.top_apply, iff_true]
86	    apply hφ
87	    simp [h]
88	
89	instance [IsIso φ] : IsLocallyInjective J φ :=
90	  isLocallyInjective_of_injective J φ (fun X => Function.Bijective.injective (by
91	    rw [bijective_iff_isIso_ofHom]
92	    infer_instance))
93	
94	instance isLocallyInjective_forget [IsLocallyInjective J φ] :
95	    IsLocallyInjective J (Functor.whiskerRight φ (forget D)) where
96	  equalizerSieve_mem x y h := equalizerSieve_mem J φ x y h
97	
98	lemma isLocallyInjective_forget_iff :
99	    IsLocallyInjective J (Functor.whiskerRight φ (forget D)) ↔ IsLocallyInjective J φ := by
100	  constructor
101	  · intro
102	    exact ⟨fun x y h => equalizerSieve_mem J (Functor.whiskerRight φ (forget D)) x y h⟩
103	  · intro
104	    infer_instance
105	
106	lemma isLocallyInjective_iff_equalizerSieve_mem_imp :
107	    IsLocallyInjective J φ ↔ ∀ ⦃X : Cᵒᵖ⦄ (x y : ToType (F₁.obj X)),
108	      equalizerSieve (φ.app _ x) (φ.app _ y) ∈ J X.unop → equalizerSieve x y ∈ J X.unop := by
109	  constructor
110	  · intro _ X x y h
111	    let S := equalizerSieve (φ.app _ x) (φ.app _ y)
112	    let T : ∀ ⦃Y : C⦄ ⦃f : Y ⟶ X.unop⦄ (_ : S f), Sieve Y := fun Y f _ =>
113	      equalizerSieve (F₁.map f.op x) ((F₁.map f.op y))
114	    refine J.superset_covering ?_ (J.transitive h (Sieve.bind S.1 T) ?_)
115	    · rintro Y f ⟨Z, a, g, hg, ha, rfl⟩
116	      simpa using ha
117	    · intro Y f hf
118	      refine J.superset_covering (Sieve.le_pullback_bind S.1 T _ hf)
119	        (equalizerSieve_mem J φ _ _ ?_)
120	      rw [NatTrans.naturality_apply, NatTrans.naturality_apply]
121	      exact hf
122	  · intro hφ
123	    exact ⟨fun {X} x y h => hφ x y (by simp [h])⟩
124	
125	lemma equalizerSieve_mem_of_equalizerSieve_app_mem
126	    {X : Cᵒᵖ} (x y : ToType (F₁.obj X)) (h : equalizerSieve (φ.app _ x) (φ.app _ y) ∈ J X.unop)
127	    [IsLocallyInjective J φ] :
128	    equalizerSieve x y ∈ J X.unop :=
129	  (isLocallyInjective_iff_equalizerSieve_mem_imp J φ).1 inferInstance x y h
130	
131	instance isLocallyInjective_comp [IsLocallyInjective J φ] [IsLocallyInjective J ψ] :
132	    IsLocallyInjective J (φ ≫ ψ) where
133	  equalizerSieve_mem {X} x y h := by
134	    apply equalizerSieve_mem_of_equalizerSieve_app_mem J φ
135	    exact equalizerSieve_mem J ψ _ _ (by simpa using h)
136	
137	lemma isLocallyInjective_of_isLocallyInjective [IsLocallyInjective J (φ ≫ ψ)] :
138	    IsLocallyInjective J φ where
139	  equalizerSieve_mem {X} x y h := equalizerSieve_mem J (φ ≫ ψ) x y (by simp [h])
140	
141	variable {φ ψ}
142	
143	lemma isLocallyInjective_of_isLocallyInjective_fac {φψ : F₁ ⟶ F₃} (fac : φ ≫ ψ = φψ)
144	    [IsLocallyInjective J φψ] : IsLocallyInjective J φ := by
145	  subst fac
146	  exact isLocallyInjective_of_isLocallyInjective J φ ψ
147	
148	lemma isLocallyInjective_iff_of_fac {φψ : F₁ ⟶ F₃} (fac : φ ≫ ψ = φψ) [IsLocallyInjective J ψ] :
149	    IsLocallyInjective J φψ ↔ IsLocallyInjective J φ := by
150	  constructor
151	  · intro
152	    exact isLocallyInjective_of_isLocallyInjective_fac J fac
153	  · intro
154	    rw [← fac]
155	    infer_instance
156	
157	variable (φ ψ)
158	
159	lemma isLocallyInjective_comp_iff [IsLocallyInjective J ψ] :
160	    IsLocallyInjective J (φ ≫ ψ) ↔ IsLocallyInjective J φ :=
161	  isLocallyInjective_iff_of_fac J rfl
162	
163	lemma isLocallyInjective_iff_injective_of_separated
164	    (hsep : Presieve.IsSeparated J (F₁ ⋙ forget D)) :
165	    IsLocallyInjective J φ ↔ ∀ (X : Cᵒᵖ), Function.Injective (φ.app X) := by
166	  constructor
167	  · intro _ X x y h
168	    exact (hsep _ (equalizerSieve_mem J φ x y h)).ext (fun _ _ hf => hf)
169	  · apply isLocallyInjective_of_injective
170	
171	instance (F : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ Type w) (G : Subfunctor F) :
172	    IsLocallyInjective J G.ι :=
173	  isLocallyInjective_of_injective _ _ (fun X => by
174	    intro ⟨x, _⟩ ⟨y, _⟩ h
175	    exact Subtype.ext h)
176	
177	section
178	
179	open GrothendieckTopology.Plus
180	
181	instance isLocallyInjective_toPlus (P : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ Type (max u v)) :
182	    IsLocallyInjective J (J.toPlus P) where
183	  equalizerSieve_mem {X} x y h := by
184	    rw [toPlus_eq_mk, toPlus_eq_mk, eq_mk_iff_exists] at h
185	    obtain ⟨W, h₁, h₂, eq⟩ := h
186	    exact J.superset_covering (fun Y f hf => congr_fun (congr_arg Subtype.val eq) ⟨Y, f, hf⟩) W.2
187	
188	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
189	instance isLocallyInjective_toSheafify (P : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ Type (max u v)) :
190	    IsLocallyInjective J (J.toSheafify P) := by
191	  dsimp [GrothendieckTopology.toSheafify]
192	  rw [GrothendieckTopology.plusMap_toPlus]
193	  infer_instance
194	
195	instance isLocallyInjective_toSheafify' {CD : D → Type (max u v)}
196	    [∀ X Y, FunLike (FD X Y) (CD X) (CD Y)] [ConcreteCategory.{max u v} D FD]
197	    (P : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ D) [HasWeakSheafify J D] [J.HasSheafCompose (forget D)]
198	    [J.PreservesSheafification (forget D)] :
199	    IsLocallyInjective J (toSheafify J P) := by
200	  rw [← isLocallyInjective_forget_iff, ← sheafComposeIso_hom_fac,
201	    ← toSheafify_plusPlusIsoSheafify_hom]
202	  infer_instance
203	
204	end
205	
206	end Presheaf
207	
208	namespace Sheaf
209	
210	variable {J}
211	variable {F₁ F₂ : Sheaf J D} (φ : F₁ ⟶ F₂)
212	
213	/-- If `φ : F₁ ⟶ F₂` is a morphism of sheaves, this is an abbreviation for
214	`Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J φ.val`. Under suitable assumptions, it
215	is equivalent to the injectivity of all maps `φ.val.app X`,
216	see `isLocallyInjective_iff_injective`. -/
217	abbrev IsLocallyInjective := Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J φ.hom
218	
219	lemma isLocallyInjective_sheafToPresheaf_map_iff :
220	    Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J ((sheafToPresheaf J D).map φ) ↔ IsLocallyInjective φ := by rfl
221	
222	instance isLocallyInjective_of_iso [IsIso φ] : IsLocallyInjective φ := by
223	  change Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J ((sheafToPresheaf _ _).map φ)
224	  infer_instance
225	
226	lemma mono_of_injective
227	    (hφ : ∀ (X : Cᵒᵖ), Function.Injective (φ.hom.app X)) : Mono φ :=
228	  have : ∀ X, Mono (φ.hom.app X) := fun X ↦ ConcreteCategory.mono_of_injective _ (hφ X)
229	  (sheafToPresheaf _ _).mono_of_mono_map (NatTrans.mono_of_mono_app φ.1)
230	
231	variable [J.HasSheafCompose (forget D)]
232	
233	instance isLocallyInjective_forget [IsLocallyInjective φ] :
234	    IsLocallyInjective ((sheafCompose J (forget D)).map φ) :=
235	  Presheaf.isLocallyInjective_forget J φ.1
236	
237	lemma isLocallyInjective_iff_injective :
238	    IsLocallyInjective φ ↔ ∀ (X : Cᵒᵖ), Function.Injective (φ.hom.app X) :=
239	  Presheaf.isLocallyInjective_iff_injective_of_separated _ _ (by
240	    apply Presieve.IsSheaf.isSeparated
241	    rw [← isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_of_type]
242	    exact ((sheafCompose J (forget D)).obj F₁).2)
243	
244	lemma mono_of_isLocallyInjective [IsLocallyInjective φ] : Mono φ := by
245	  apply mono_of_injective
246	  rw [← isLocallyInjective_iff_injective]
247	  infer_instance
248	
249	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
250	instance {F G : Sheaf J (Type w)} (f : F ⟶ G) :
251	    IsLocallyInjective (Sheaf.imageι f) := by
252	  dsimp [Sheaf.imageι]
253	  infer_instance
254	
255	end Sheaf
256	
257	end CategoryTheory
258

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2022 Andrew Yang. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Andrew Yang, Joël Riou
5	-/
6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Subsheaf
9	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.CompatibleSheafification
10	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.LocallyInjective
11	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.ShrinkYoneda
12	/-!
13	
14	# Locally surjective morphisms
15	
16	## Main definitions
17	
18	- `IsLocallySurjective` : A morphism of presheaves valued in a concrete category is locally
19	  surjective with respect to a Grothendieck topology if every section in the target is locally
20	  in the set-theoretic image, i.e. the image sheaf coincides with the target.
21	
22	## Main results
23	
24	- `Presheaf.isLocallySurjective_toSheafify`: `toSheafify` is locally surjective.
25	- `Sheaf.isLocallySurjective_iff_epi`: a morphism of sheaves of types is locally
26	  surjective iff it is epi.
27	
28	-/
29	
30	@[expose] public section
31	
32	
33	universe w v u v' u' w'
34	
35	open Opposite CategoryTheory CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology CategoryTheory.Functor Limits
36	
37	namespace CategoryTheory
38	
39	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] (J : GrothendieckTopology C)
40	
41	variable {A : Type u'} [Category.{v'} A] {FA : A → A → Type*} {CA : A → Type w'}
42	variable [∀ X Y, FunLike (FA X Y) (CA X) (CA Y)] [ConcreteCategory.{w'} A FA]
43	
44	namespace Presheaf
45	
46	/-- Given `f : F ⟶ G`, a morphism between presieves, and `s : G.obj (op U)`, this is the sieve
47	of `U` consisting of the `i : V ⟶ U` such that `s` restricted along `i` is in the image of `f`. -/
48	@[simps -isSimp]
49	def imageSieve {F G : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f : F ⟶ G) {U : C} (s : ToType (G.obj (op U))) : Sieve U where
50	  arrows V i := ∃ t : ToType (F.obj (op V)), f.app _ t = G.map i.op s
51	  downward_closed := by
52	    rintro V W i ⟨t, ht⟩ j
53	    refine ⟨F.map j.op t, ?_⟩
54	    rw [op_comp, G.map_comp, ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← ht, NatTrans.naturality_apply f]
55	
56	lemma pullback_imageSieve
57	    {F G : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f : F ⟶ G) {U : C} (s : ToType (G.obj (op U)))
58	    {V : C} (g : V ⟶ U) :
59	    (imageSieve f s).pullback g = imageSieve f (G.map g.op s) := by
60	  ext W g
61	  simp [imageSieve]
62	
63	theorem imageSieve_eq_sieveOfSection {F G : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f : F ⟶ G) {U : C}
64	    (s : ToType (G.obj (op U))) :
65	    imageSieve f s = (Subfunctor.range (whiskerRight f (forget A))).sieveOfSection s :=
66	  rfl
67	
68	theorem imageSieve_whisker_forget {F G : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f : F ⟶ G) {U : C} (s : ToType (G.obj (op U))) :
69	    imageSieve (whiskerRight f (forget A)) s = imageSieve f s :=
70	  rfl
71	
72	theorem imageSieve_app {F G : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f : F ⟶ G) {U : C} (s : ToType (F.obj (op U))) :
73	    imageSieve f (f.app _ s) = ⊤ := by
74	  ext V i
75	  simp only [Sieve.top_apply, iff_true, imageSieve_apply]
76	  exact ⟨F.map i.op s, NatTrans.naturality_apply f i.op s⟩
77	
78	/-- If a morphism `g : V ⟶ U.unop` belongs to the sieve `imageSieve f s g`, then
79	this is choice of a preimage of `G.map g.op s` in `F.obj (op V)`, see
80	`app_localPreimage`. -/
81	noncomputable def localPreimage {F G : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f : F ⟶ G) {U : Cᵒᵖ} (s : ToType (G.obj U))
82	    {V : C} (g : V ⟶ U.unop) (hg : imageSieve f s g) :
83	    ToType (F.obj (op V)) :=
84	  hg.choose
85	
86	@[simp]
87	lemma app_localPreimage {F G : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f : F ⟶ G) {U : Cᵒᵖ} (s : ToType (G.obj U))
88	    {V : C} (g : V ⟶ U.unop) (hg : imageSieve f s g) :
89	    f.app _ (localPreimage f s g hg) = G.map g.op s :=
90	  hg.choose_spec
91	
92	/-- A morphism of presheaves `f : F ⟶ G` is locally surjective with respect to a Grothendieck
93	topology if every section of `G` is locally in the image of `f`. -/
94	class IsLocallySurjective {F G : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f : F ⟶ G) : Prop where
95	  imageSieve_mem {U : C} (s : ToType (G.obj (op U))) : imageSieve f s ∈ J U
96	
97	lemma imageSieve_mem {F G : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f : F ⟶ G) [IsLocallySurjective J f] {U : Cᵒᵖ}
98	    (s : ToType (G.obj U)) : imageSieve f s ∈ J U.unop :=
99	  IsLocallySurjective.imageSieve_mem _
100	
101	instance {F G : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f : F ⟶ G) [IsLocallySurjective J f] :
102	    IsLocallySurjective J (whiskerRight f (forget A)) where
103	  imageSieve_mem s := imageSieve_mem J f s
104	
105	theorem isLocallySurjective_iff_range_sheafify_eq_top {F G : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f : F ⟶ G) :
106	    IsLocallySurjective J f ↔ (Subfunctor.range (whiskerRight f (forget A))).sheafify J = ⊤ := by
107	  simp only [Subfunctor.ext_iff, funext_iff, Set.ext_iff, Subfunctor.top_obj,
108	    Set.top_eq_univ, Set.mem_univ, iff_true]
109	  exact ⟨fun H _ => H.imageSieve_mem, fun H => ⟨H _⟩⟩
110	
111	theorem isLocallySurjective_iff_range_sheafify_eq_top' {F G : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ Type w} (f : F ⟶ G) :
112	    IsLocallySurjective J f ↔ (Subfunctor.range f).sheafify J = ⊤ := by
113	  apply isLocallySurjective_iff_range_sheafify_eq_top
114	
115	theorem isLocallySurjective_iff_whisker_forget {F G : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f : F ⟶ G) :
116	    IsLocallySurjective J f ↔ IsLocallySurjective J (whiskerRight f (forget A)) := by
117	  simp only [isLocallySurjective_iff_range_sheafify_eq_top]
118	  rfl
119	
120	theorem isLocallySurjective_of_surjective {F G : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f : F ⟶ G)
121	    (H : ∀ U, Function.Surjective (f.app U)) : IsLocallySurjective J f where
122	  imageSieve_mem {U} s := by
123	    obtain ⟨t, rfl⟩ := H _ s
124	    rw [imageSieve_app]
125	    exact J.top_mem _
126	
127	instance isLocallySurjective_of_iso {F G : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f : F ⟶ G) [IsIso f] :
128	    IsLocallySurjective J f := by
129	  apply isLocallySurjective_of_surjective
130	  intro U
131	  apply Function.Bijective.surjective
132	  rw [bijective_iff_isIso_ofHom]
133	  infer_instance
134	
135	instance isLocallySurjective_comp {F₁ F₂ F₃ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f₁ : F₁ ⟶ F₂) (f₂ : F₂ ⟶ F₃)
136	    [IsLocallySurjective J f₁] [IsLocallySurjective J f₂] :
137	    IsLocallySurjective J (f₁ ≫ f₂) where
138	  imageSieve_mem s := by
139	    have : (Sieve.bind (imageSieve f₂ s) fun _ _ h => imageSieve f₁ h.choose) ≤
140	        imageSieve (f₁ ≫ f₂) s := by
141	      rintro V i ⟨W, i, j, H, ⟨t', ht'⟩, rfl⟩
142	      refine ⟨t', ?_⟩
143	      rw [op_comp, F₃.map_comp, NatTrans.comp_app, ConcreteCategory.comp_apply,
144	        ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ht', NatTrans.naturality_apply, H.choose_spec]
145	    apply J.superset_covering this
146	    apply J.bind_covering
147	    · apply imageSieve_mem
148	    · intros; apply imageSieve_mem
149	
150	lemma isLocallySurjective_of_isLocallySurjective
151	    {F₁ F₂ F₃ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f₁ : F₁ ⟶ F₂) (f₂ : F₂ ⟶ F₃)
152	    [IsLocallySurjective J (f₁ ≫ f₂)] :
153	    IsLocallySurjective J f₂ where
154	  imageSieve_mem {X} x := by
155	    refine J.superset_covering ?_ (imageSieve_mem J (f₁ ≫ f₂) x)
156	    intro Y g hg
157	    exact ⟨f₁.app _ (localPreimage (f₁ ≫ f₂) x g hg),
158	      by simpa using app_localPreimage (f₁ ≫ f₂) x g hg⟩
159	
160	lemma isLocallySurjective_of_isLocallySurjective_fac

184	lemma isLocallySurjective_of_le {K : GrothendieckTopology C} (hJK : J ≤ K) {F G : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A}
185	    (f : F ⟶ G) (h : IsLocallySurjective J f) : IsLocallySurjective K f where
186	  imageSieve_mem s := by apply hJK; exact h.1 _
187	
188	lemma isLocallyInjective_of_isLocallyInjective_of_isLocallySurjective
189	    {F₁ F₂ F₃ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f₁ : F₁ ⟶ F₂) (f₂ : F₂ ⟶ F₃)
190	    [IsLocallyInjective J (f₁ ≫ f₂)] [IsLocallySurjective J f₁] :
191	    IsLocallyInjective J f₂ where
192	  equalizerSieve_mem {X} x₁ x₂ h := by
193	    let S := imageSieve f₁ x₁ ⊓ imageSieve f₁ x₂
194	    have hS : S ∈ J X.unop := by
195	      apply J.intersection_covering
196	      all_goals apply imageSieve_mem
197	    let T : ∀ ⦃Y : C⦄ (f : Y ⟶ X.unop) (_ : S f), Sieve Y := fun Y f hf =>
198	      equalizerSieve (localPreimage f₁ x₁ f hf.1) (localPreimage f₁ x₂ f hf.2)
199	    refine J.superset_covering ?_ (J.transitive hS (Sieve.bind S.1 T) ?_)
200	    · rintro Y f ⟨Z, a, g, hg, ha, rfl⟩
201	      simpa using congr_arg (f₁.app _) ha
202	    · intro Y f hf
203	      apply J.superset_covering (Sieve.le_pullback_bind _ _ _ hf)
204	      apply equalizerSieve_mem J (f₁ ≫ f₂)
205	      dsimp
206	      rw [ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, app_localPreimage,
207	        app_localPreimage, NatTrans.naturality_apply, NatTrans.naturality_apply, h]
208	
209	lemma isLocallyInjective_of_isLocallyInjective_of_isLocallySurjective_fac
210	    {F₁ F₂ F₃ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} {f₁ : F₁ ⟶ F₂} {f₂ : F₂ ⟶ F₃} (f₃ : F₁ ⟶ F₃) (fac : f₁ ≫ f₂ = f₃)
211	    [IsLocallyInjective J f₃] [IsLocallySurjective J f₁] :
212	    IsLocallyInjective J f₂ := by
213	  subst fac
214	  exact isLocallyInjective_of_isLocallyInjective_of_isLocallySurjective J f₁ f₂
215	
216	lemma isLocallySurjective_of_isLocallySurjective_of_isLocallyInjective
217	    {F₁ F₂ F₃ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f₁ : F₁ ⟶ F₂) (f₂ : F₂ ⟶ F₃)
218	    [IsLocallySurjective J (f₁ ≫ f₂)] [IsLocallyInjective J f₂] :
219	    IsLocallySurjective J f₁ where
220	  imageSieve_mem {X} x := by
221	    let S := imageSieve (f₁ ≫ f₂) (f₂.app _ x)
222	    let T : ∀ ⦃Y : C⦄ (f : Y ⟶ X) (_ : S f), Sieve Y := fun Y f hf =>
223	      equalizerSieve (f₁.app _ (localPreimage (f₁ ≫ f₂) (f₂.app _ x) f hf)) (F₂.map f.op x)
224	    refine J.superset_covering ?_ (J.transitive (imageSieve_mem J (f₁ ≫ f₂) (f₂.app _ x))
225	      (Sieve.bind S.1 T) ?_)
226	    · rintro Y _ ⟨Z, a, g, hg, ha, rfl⟩
227	      exact ⟨F₁.map a.op (localPreimage (f₁ ≫ f₂) _ _ hg), by simpa using ha⟩
228	    · intro Y f hf
229	      apply J.superset_covering (Sieve.le_pullback_bind _ _ _ hf)
230	      apply equalizerSieve_mem J f₂
231	      rw [NatTrans.naturality_apply, ← app_localPreimage (f₁ ≫ f₂) _ _ hf,
232	        NatTrans.comp_app, ConcreteCategory.comp_apply]
233	
234	lemma isLocallySurjective_of_isLocallySurjective_of_isLocallyInjective_fac
235	    {F₁ F₂ F₃ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} {f₁ : F₁ ⟶ F₂} {f₂ : F₂ ⟶ F₃} (f₃ : F₁ ⟶ F₃) (fac : f₁ ≫ f₂ = f₃)
236	    [IsLocallySurjective J f₃] [IsLocallyInjective J f₂] :
237	    IsLocallySurjective J f₁ := by
238	  subst fac
239	  exact isLocallySurjective_of_isLocallySurjective_of_isLocallyInjective J f₁ f₂
240	
241	lemma comp_isLocallyInjective_iff
242	    {F₁ F₂ F₃ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f₁ : F₁ ⟶ F₂) (f₂ : F₂ ⟶ F₃)
243	    [IsLocallyInjective J f₁] [IsLocallySurjective J f₁] :
244	    IsLocallyInjective J (f₁ ≫ f₂) ↔ IsLocallyInjective J f₂ := by

246	  · intro
247	    exact isLocallyInjective_of_isLocallyInjective_of_isLocallySurjective J f₁ f₂

252	    {F₁ F₂ F₃ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f₁ : F₁ ⟶ F₂) (f₂ : F₂ ⟶ F₃)
253	    [IsLocallyInjective J f₂] [IsLocallySurjective J f₂] :

261	instance {F₁ F₂ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ Type w} (f : F₁ ⟶ F₂) :
262	    IsLocallySurjective J (Subfunctor.toRangeSheafify J f) where
263	  imageSieve_mem {X} := by
264	    rintro ⟨s, hs⟩
265	    refine J.superset_covering ?_ hs
266	    rintro Y g ⟨t, ht⟩
267	    exact ⟨t, Subtype.ext ht⟩
268	
269	/-- The image of `F` in `J.sheafify F` is isomorphic to the sheafification. -/
270	noncomputable def sheafificationIsoImagePresheaf (F : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ Type (max u v)) :
271	    J.sheafify F ≅ ((Subfunctor.range (J.toSheafify F)).sheafify J).toFunctor where
272	  hom :=
273	    J.sheafifyLift (Subfunctor.toRangeSheafify J _)
274	      ((isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_of_type J _).mpr <|
275	        Subfunctor.sheafify_isSheaf _ <|
276	          (isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_of_type J _).mp <| GrothendieckTopology.sheafify_isSheaf J _)
277	  inv := Subfunctor.ι _
278	  hom_inv_id :=
279	    J.sheafify_hom_ext _ _ (J.sheafify_isSheaf _) (by simp [Subfunctor.toRangeSheafify])
280	  inv_hom_id := by
281	    rw [← cancel_mono (Subfunctor.ι _), Category.id_comp, Category.assoc]
282	    refine Eq.trans ?_ (Category.comp_id _)
283	    congr 1
284	    exact J.sheafify_hom_ext _ _ (J.sheafify_isSheaf _) (by simp [Subfunctor.toRangeSheafify])

288	open GrothendieckTopology.Plus

290	instance isLocallySurjective_toPlus (P : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ Type (max u v)) :
291	    IsLocallySurjective J (J.toPlus P) where
292	  imageSieve_mem x := by
293	    obtain ⟨S, x, rfl⟩ := exists_rep x
294	    refine J.superset_covering (fun Y f hf => ⟨x.1 ⟨Y, f, hf⟩, ?_⟩) S.2
295	    rw [toPlus_eq_mk, res_mk_eq_mk_pullback, eq_mk_iff_exists]
296	    refine ⟨S.pullback f, homOfLE le_top, 𝟙 _, ?_⟩
297	    ext ⟨Z, g, hg⟩
298	    simpa using x.2 { fst.hf := hf, snd.hf := S.1.downward_closed hf g, r.g₁ := g, r.g₂ := 𝟙 Z, .. }
299

302	    IsLocallySurjective J (J.toSheafify P) := by
303	  dsimp [GrothendieckTopology.toSheafify]

307	instance isLocallySurjective_toSheafify' {D : Type*} [Category* D] {FD : D → D → Type*}
308	    {CD : D → Type (max u v)} [∀ X Y, FunLike (FD X Y) (CD X) (CD Y)]
309	    [ConcreteCategory.{max u v} D FD]
310	    (P : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ D) [HasWeakSheafify J D] [J.HasSheafCompose (forget D)]
311	    [J.PreservesSheafification (forget D)] :
312	    IsLocallySurjective J (toSheafify J P) := by
313	  rw [isLocallySurjective_iff_whisker_forget,
314	    ← sheafComposeIso_hom_fac, ← toSheafify_plusPlusIsoSheafify_hom]
315	  infer_instance

353	theorem isLocallySurjective_iff_isIso {F G : Sheaf J (Type w)} (f : F ⟶ G) :
354	    IsLocallySurjective f ↔ IsIso (Sheaf.imageι f) := by
355	  dsimp only [IsLocallySurjective]

360	instance epi_of_isLocallySurjective' {F₁ F₂ : Sheaf J (Type w)} (φ : F₁ ⟶ F₂)
361	    [IsLocallySurjective φ] : Epi φ where
362	  left_cancellation {Z} f₁ f₂ h := by
363	    ext X x
364	    apply (((isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_of_type _ _).1 Z.2).isSeparated _
365	      (Presheaf.imageSieve_mem J φ.hom x)).ext
366	    rintro Y f ⟨s : F₁.obj.obj (op Y), hs : φ.hom.app _ s = F₂.obj.map f.op x⟩
367	    dsimp
368	    have h₁ := ConcreteCategory.congr_hom (f₁.hom.naturality f.op) x
369	    have h₂ := ConcreteCategory.congr_hom (f₂.hom.naturality f.op) x
370	    dsimp at h₁ h₂
371	    rw [← h₁, ← h₂, ← hs]
372	    exact ConcreteCategory.congr_hom (congr_app ((sheafToPresheaf J _).congr_map h) (op Y)) s
373

378	    [HasSheafify J (Type w)] :
379	    IsLocallySurjective φ ↔ Epi φ := by
380	  constructor
381	  · intro
382	    infer_instance
383	  · intro
384	    have := epi_of_epi_fac (Sheaf.toImage_ι φ)
385	    rw [isLocallySurjective_iff_isIso φ]
386	    apply isIso_of_mono_of_epi

392	variable {R R' : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ Type w} (φ : R ⟶ R') {X : Cᵒᵖ} (r' : R'.obj X)
393	
394	/-- Given a morphism `φ : R ⟶ R'` of presheaves of types and `r' : R'.obj X`,
395	this is the family of elements of `R` defined over the sieve `Presheaf.imageSieve φ r'`
396	which sends a map in this sieve to an arbitrary choice of a preimage of the
397	restriction of `r'`. -/
398	noncomputable def localPreimage :
399	    FamilyOfElements R (Presheaf.imageSieve φ r').arrows :=
400	  fun _ f hf => Presheaf.localPreimage φ r' f hf
401	
402	lemma isAmalgamation_map_localPreimage :
403	    ((localPreimage φ r').map φ).IsAmalgamation r' :=
404	  fun _ f hf => (Presheaf.app_localPreimage φ r' f hf).symm
405	
406	end Presieve.FamilyOfElements

410	variable {S : C} {ι : Type*} [Small.{w} ι] {X : ι → C} (f : ∀ i, X i ⟶ S)

413	
414	lemma imageSieve_cofanIsColimitDesc_shrinkYoneda_map
415	    {c : Cofan (fun i ↦ shrinkYoneda.{w}.obj (X i))} (hc : IsColimit c)
416	    {U : C} (g : U ⟶ S) :
417	    Presheaf.imageSieve
418	      (Cofan.IsColimit.desc hc (fun i ↦ shrinkYoneda.{w}.map (f i))) (U := U)
419	        (shrinkYonedaObjObjEquiv.symm g) = Sieve.pullback g (Sieve.ofArrows X f) := by
420	  ext V v
421	  simp only [Sieve.pullback_apply, Sieve.generate_apply]
422	  refine ⟨fun hv ↦ ?_, ?_⟩
423	  · obtain ⟨w, hw⟩ := hv
424	    obtain ⟨⟨i⟩, a, rfl⟩ := Types.jointly_surjective_of_isColimit
425	      (isColimitOfPreserves ((evaluation _ _).obj (op V)) hc) w
426	    obtain ⟨a : V ⟶ X i, rfl⟩ := shrinkYonedaObjObjEquiv.symm.surjective a
427	    refine ⟨_, a, _, ⟨i⟩, shrinkYonedaObjObjEquiv.symm.injective ?_⟩
428	    rw [← shrinkYoneda_map_app_shrinkYonedaObjObjEquiv_symm]
429	    convert hw using 1
430	    · exact (ConcreteCategory.congr_hom (NatTrans.congr_app
431	        ((Cofan.IsColimit.fac hc (fun i ↦ shrinkYoneda.{w}.map (f i))) i) (op V))
432	          (shrinkYonedaObjObjEquiv.symm a)).symm
433	    · exact (shrinkYoneda_obj_map_shrinkYonedaObjObjEquiv_symm v.op g).symm
434	  · rintro ⟨_, a, _, ⟨i⟩, fac⟩
435	    refine ⟨(c.inj i).app (op V) (shrinkYonedaObjObjEquiv.symm a),
436	      (ConcreteCategory.congr_hom (NatTrans.congr_app
437	      ((Cofan.IsColimit.fac hc (fun i ↦ shrinkYoneda.{w}.map (f i))) i) (op V))
438	        (shrinkYonedaObjObjEquiv.symm a)).trans ?_⟩
439	    rw [shrinkYoneda_map_app_shrinkYonedaObjObjEquiv_symm a (f i), fac]
440	    exact (shrinkYoneda_obj_map_shrinkYonedaObjObjEquiv_symm v.op g).symm
441	
442	end Presheaf

444	namespace GrothendieckTopology
445	
446	lemma ofArrows_mem_iff_isLocallySurjective_cofanIsColimitDesc_shrinkYoneda_map
447	    [LocallySmall.{w} C] {S : C} {ι : Type*} [Small.{w} ι] {X : ι → C}
448	    (f : ∀ i, X i ⟶ S)
449	    {c : Cofan (fun i ↦ shrinkYoneda.{w}.obj (X i))} (hc : IsColimit c) :
450	    Sieve.ofArrows _ f ∈ J S ↔
451	      Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J
452	        (Cofan.IsColimit.desc hc (fun i ↦ shrinkYoneda.{w}.map (f i))) := by
453	  refine ⟨fun hf ↦ ⟨fun {U u} ↦ ?_⟩, fun hf ↦ ?_⟩
454	  · obtain ⟨u, rfl⟩ := shrinkYonedaObjObjEquiv.symm.surjective u
455	    replace hf := J.pullback_stable u hf
456	    rwa [← Presheaf.imageSieve_cofanIsColimitDesc_shrinkYoneda_map f hc u] at hf
457	  · rw [← Sieve.pullback_id (S := Sieve.ofArrows X f),
458	      ← Presheaf.imageSieve_cofanIsColimitDesc_shrinkYoneda_map f hc (𝟙 S)]
459	    exact Presheaf.imageSieve_mem J (Cofan.IsColimit.desc hc (fun i ↦ shrinkYoneda.{w}.map (f i)))
460	      (shrinkYonedaObjObjEquiv.symm (𝟙 S))
461	
462	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
463	lemma ofArrows_mem_iff_isLocallySurjective_cofanIsColimitDesc_uliftYoneda_map
464	    {S : C} {ι : Type*} [Small.{max w v} ι] {X : ι → C}
465	    (f : ∀ i, X i ⟶ S)
466	    {c : Cofan (fun i ↦ uliftYoneda.{w}.obj (X i))} (hc : IsColimit c) :
467	    Sieve.ofArrows _ f ∈ J S ↔
468	      Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J
469	        (Cofan.IsColimit.desc hc (fun i ↦ uliftYoneda.{w}.map (f i))) := by
470	  let e : Discrete.functor (fun i ↦ uliftYoneda.{w}.obj (X i)) ≅
471	      Discrete.functor (fun i ↦ shrinkYoneda.{max w v}.obj (X i)) :=
472	    Discrete.natIso (fun i ↦ uliftYonedaIsoShrinkYoneda.{w}.app (X i.as))
473	  let hc' := (IsColimit.precomposeInvEquiv e _).2 hc
474	  rw [ofArrows_mem_iff_isLocallySurjective_cofanIsColimitDesc_shrinkYoneda_map.{max w v} J f hc']
475	  have :
476	      Cofan.IsColimit.desc hc (fun i ↦ uliftYoneda.map (f i)) ≫
477	        uliftYonedaIsoShrinkYoneda.hom.app _ =
478	      Cofan.IsColimit.desc hc' (fun i ↦ shrinkYoneda.map (f i)) :=
479	    Cofan.IsColimit.hom_ext hc _ _ (fun i ↦ by
480	      rw [Cofan.IsColimit.fac_assoc, NatTrans.naturality,
481	        ← Cofan.IsColimit.fac hc' (fun i ↦ shrinkYoneda.map (f i)) i]
482	      simp [Cofan.inj, e])
483	  rw [← this, Presheaf.isLocallySurjective_comp_iff J]
484	
485	lemma ofArrows_mem_iff_isLocallySurjective_sigmaDesc_shrinkYoneda_map [LocallySmall.{w} C]
486	    {S : C} {ι : Type*} [Small.{w} ι] {X : ι → C} (f : ∀ i, X i ⟶ S) :
487	    Sieve.ofArrows _ f ∈ J S ↔
488	      Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J (Sigma.desc (fun i ↦ shrinkYoneda.{w}.map (f i))) :=
489	  ofArrows_mem_iff_isLocallySurjective_cofanIsColimitDesc_shrinkYoneda_map J f
490	    (coproductIsCoproduct _)
491	
492	lemma ofArrows_mem_iff_isLocallySurjective_sigmaDesc_uliftYoneda_map
493	    {S : C} {ι : Type*} [Small.{max w v} ι] {X : ι → C} (f : ∀ i, X i ⟶ S) :
494	    Sieve.ofArrows _ f ∈ J S ↔
495	      Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J (Sigma.desc (fun i ↦ uliftYoneda.{w}.map (f i))) :=
496	  ofArrows_mem_iff_isLocallySurjective_cofanIsColimitDesc_uliftYoneda_map J f
497	    (coproductIsCoproduct _)

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.LocallySurjective
9	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Localization
10	
11	/-!
12	# Locally bijective morphisms of presheaves
13	
14	Let `C` be a category equipped with a Grothendieck topology `J`.
15	Let `A` be a concrete category.
16	In this file, we introduce a type class `J.WEqualsLocallyBijective A` which says
17	that the class `J.W` (of morphisms of presheaves which become isomorphisms
18	after sheafification) is the class of morphisms that are both locally injective
19	and locally surjective (i.e. locally bijective). We prove that this holds iff
20	for any presheaf `P : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A`, the sheafification map `toSheafify J P` is locally bijective.
21	We show that this holds under certain universe assumptions.
22

41	/-- A morphism of sheaves of types is locally bijective iff it is an isomorphism.
42	(This is generalized below as `isLocallyBijective_iff_isIso`.) -/
43	private lemma isLocallyBijective_iff_isIso' :
44	    IsLocallyInjective f ∧ IsLocallySurjective f ↔ IsIso f := by
45	  constructor
46	  · rintro ⟨h₁, _⟩
47	    rw [isLocallyInjective_iff_injective] at h₁
48	    suffices ∀ (X : Cᵒᵖ), Function.Surjective (f.hom.app X) by
49	      rw [← isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso _ (sheafToPresheaf _ _), NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app]
50	      intro X
51	      rw [isIso_iff_bijective]
52	      exact ⟨h₁ X, this X⟩
53	    intro X s
54	    have H := (isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_of_type J F.obj).1 F.property _
55	      (Presheaf.imageSieve_mem J f.hom s)
56	    let t : Presieve.FamilyOfElements F.obj (Presheaf.imageSieve f.hom s).arrows :=
57	      fun Y g hg => Presheaf.localPreimage f.hom s g hg
58	    have ht : t.Compatible := by
59	      intro Y₁ Y₂ W g₁ g₂ f₁ f₂ hf₁ hf₂ w
60	      apply h₁
61	      have eq₁ := NatTrans.naturality_apply f.hom g₁.op (t f₁ hf₁)
62	      have eq₂ := NatTrans.naturality_apply f.hom g₂.op (t f₂ hf₂)
63	      have eq₃ := congr_arg (G.obj.map g₁.op) (Presheaf.app_localPreimage f.hom s _ hf₁)
64	      have eq₄ := congr_arg (G.obj.map g₂.op) (Presheaf.app_localPreimage f.hom s _ hf₂)
65	      refine eq₁.trans (eq₃.trans (Eq.trans ?_ (eq₄.symm.trans eq₂.symm)))
66	      rw [← Functor.map_comp_apply, ← Functor.map_comp_apply]
67	      simp only [← op_comp, w]
68	    refine ⟨H.amalgamate t ht, ?_⟩
69	    · apply (((isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_of_type J G.obj).1 G.property).isSeparated _
70	        (Presheaf.imageSieve_mem J f.hom s)).ext
71	      intro Y g hg
72	      rw [← NatTrans.naturality_apply, H.valid_glue ht]
73	      exact Presheaf.app_localPreimage f.hom s g hg
74	  · intro
75	    constructor <;> infer_instance

102	/-- Given a category `C` equipped with a Grothendieck topology `J` and a concrete category `A`,
103	this property holds if a morphism in `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A` satisfies `J.W` (i.e. becomes an iso after
104	sheafification) iff it is both locally injective and locally surjective. -/
105	class WEqualsLocallyBijective : Prop where
106	  iff {X Y : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f : X ⟶ Y) :
107	    J.W f ↔ Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J f ∧ Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J f
108

112	variable [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective A] {X Y : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f : X ⟶ Y)
113	
114	lemma W_iff_isLocallyBijective :
115	    J.W f ↔ Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J f ∧ Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J f := by
116	  apply WEqualsLocallyBijective.iff
117	
118	lemma W_of_isLocallyBijective [Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J f]
119	    [Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J f] : J.W f := by
120	  rw [W_iff_isLocallyBijective]
121	  constructor <;> infer_instance
122	
123	variable {J f}
124	
125	lemma W.isLocallyInjective (hf : J.W f) : Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J f :=
126	  ((J.W_iff_isLocallyBijective f).1 hf).1
127	
128	lemma W.isLocallySurjective (hf : J.W f) : Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J f :=
129	  ((J.W_iff_isLocallyBijective f).1 hf).2
130	
131	variable [HasWeakSheafify J A] (P : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A)
132	
133	instance : Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J (CategoryTheory.toSheafify J P) :=
134	  (J.W_toSheafify P).isLocallyInjective
135	
136	instance : Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J (CategoryTheory.toSheafify J P) :=
137	  (J.W_toSheafify P).isLocallySurjective
138	
139	end

141	lemma WEqualsLocallyBijective.mk' [HasWeakSheafify J A] [(forget A).ReflectsIsomorphisms]
142	    [J.HasSheafCompose (forget A)]
143	    [∀ (P : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A), Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J (CategoryTheory.toSheafify J P)]
144	    [∀ (P : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A), Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J (CategoryTheory.toSheafify J P)] :
145	    J.WEqualsLocallyBijective A where
146	  iff {P Q} f := by
147	    rw [W_iff, ← Sheaf.isLocallyBijective_iff_isIso (A := A),
148	      ← Presheaf.isLocallyInjective_comp_iff J f (CategoryTheory.toSheafify J Q),
149	      ← Presheaf.isLocallySurjective_comp_iff J f (CategoryTheory.toSheafify J Q),
150	      CategoryTheory.toSheafify_naturality, Presheaf.comp_isLocallyInjective_iff,
151	      Presheaf.comp_isLocallySurjective_iff]
152	
153	instance {D : Type w} [Category.{w'} D] {FD : D → D → Type*} {CD : D → Type (max u v)}
154	    [∀ X Y, FunLike (FD X Y) (CD X) (CD Y)] [ConcreteCategory.{max u v} D FD]
155	    [HasWeakSheafify J D] [J.HasSheafCompose (forget D)]
156	    [J.PreservesSheafification (forget D)] [(forget D).ReflectsIsomorphisms] :
157	    J.WEqualsLocallyBijective D := by
158	  apply WEqualsLocallyBijective.mk'

160	instance : J.WEqualsLocallyBijective (Type (max u v)) :=
161	  inferInstance

# Mathlib Locally Injective / Surjective API — full report

All files at mathlib commit `b80f227`, under `.lake/packages/mathlib/`.

## Key terminology

`ToType (F.obj X)` is the underlying type of the object `F.obj X` in the concrete category. Elements are written `x : ToType (F.obj X)`, and a natural transformation acts via `φ.app X x` (the `FunLike` coercion of the concrete hom `φ.app X`). For `AddCommGrpCat`-valued presheaves (`Ab = AddCommGrpCat`), this is `Preadditive`/additive, but **the locally-injective/surjective API itself is stated purely concretely** — it does not assume additivity. There is no additive-specialized variant (see Q2).

---

## Recommended proof skeletons

Target: `F : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A` (presheaves of modules / `Ab`) sectionwise flat, `g : G₁ ⟶ G₂` locally injective (resp. surjective). Show `F ◁ g`, whose `app X` is `LinearMap.lTensor (F.obj X) (g.app X)` (as a hom of `Ab`-valued presheaves via `toPresheaf`), is locally injective (resp. surjective).

First normalize the carrier: by `toPresheaf_map_app_apply`, the underlying function of `(F ◁ g).app X` on `ToType` is literally `(LinearMap.lTensor (F.obj X) (g.app X))` applied to elements, so all `equalizerSieve`/`imageSieve` conditions are about that concrete map. Use the `simp` lemmas `toPresheaf_map_app_apply`, `presheaf_map_apply_coe` to discharge the `.app`/`.map` bookkeeping, and `NatTrans.naturality_apply` to commute restrictions past `g`.

### Locally injective
Build `⟨fun {X} ξ η h => …⟩` where `h : (F◁g).app X ξ = (F◁g).app X η`, goal `equalizerSieve ξ η ∈ J X.unop`.
1. A general element of `(F.obj X) ⊗ (G₁.obj X)` is a finite sum of simple tensors `Σ aᵢ ⊗ bᵢ`. Reduce to controlling each `g.app X bᵢ`. For each section `b` appearing, `g` locally injective does **not** directly help unless `g.app X b = g.app X b'`; the real content is flatness.
2. The clean route: flatness of `F.obj X` means `LinearMap.lTensor (F.obj X) (g.app X)` is injective **whenever `g.app X` is injective** — but `g.app X` need not be injective globally. So instead, for the elements `ξ, η`, choose (via `J`) a covering sieve refining where the finitely many sections involved become controlled by `g`'s local injectivity: for each generating map `f : V → X.unop` in `equalizerSieve_mem J g …`, restrict and use that `F.map f.op` is flat so `lTensor` stays injective on restrictions, giving `F.map f.op ξ = F.map f.op η` on that cover.
3. Concretely: take `S := ⨅ equalizerSieve (componentwise)` covering by `J.intersection_covering` over the finitely many `g`-equalities supplied by `equalizerSieve_mem J g`, then `J.superset_covering` into `equalizerSieve ξ η`. Each step where you restrict `ξ, η` along `f` and need to land back uses `NatTrans.naturality_apply` and the `lTensor` naturality (`lTensor` commutes with base-change `F.map f.op ⊗ -`), and flatness gives injectivity of `lTensor` of an injective map on the cover. Close membership with `J.superset_covering`/`J.transitive` exactly as in `isLocallyInjective_iff_equalizerSieve_mem_imp` (lines 106–123), which is the structural template: it `J.transitive`-splits along `equalizerSieve (g …)` then `J.superset_covering`s back.

### Locally surjective
Build `⟨fun {U} s => …⟩`, `s : ToType ((F ◁ G₂).obj (op U))`, goal `imageSieve (F◁g) s ∈ J U`.
1. Write `s = Σ aᵢ ⊗ bᵢ` with `aᵢ : F.obj`, `bᵢ : G₂.obj`. For each `bᵢ`, `g` locally surjective gives `imageSieve g bᵢ ∈ J U` (via `imageSieve_mem J g`), with `localPreimage g bᵢ f hf` a preimage on each `f` in that sieve (`app_localPreimage`).
2. Intersect the finitely many sieves `⨅ᵢ imageSieve g bᵢ` — covering by repeated `J.intersection_covering`. On the intersection `S`, every `bᵢ` simultaneously has a preimage `cᵢ := localPreimage g bᵢ f (hf.i)`, so `Σ aᵢ ⊗ cᵢ` is a preimage of `s|_V` under `lTensor (F.obj V) (g.app V)`: check with `app_localPreimage`, `toPresheaf_map_app_apply`, `NatTrans.naturality_apply` and bilinearity of `⊗`. Hence `S ≤ imageSieve (F◁g) s`.
3. Conclude `imageSieve (F◁g) s ∈ J U` by `J.superset_covering S_le hS`. Flatness is **not** needed for surjectivity (`lTensor` of a surjection is always surjective); only the sieve-intersection bookkeeping. The internal proof `isLocallySurjective_comp` (135–148) and `isLocallyInjective_of_..._surjective` (188–207) are the precise `Sieve.bind`/`bind_covering`/`superset_covering` templates; for the finite-sum case `intersection_covering` replaces `bind`.

Which lemma closes each step, summarized: existence of per-component cover → `imageSieve_mem`/`equalizerSieve_mem J g`; combine finitely many covers → `J.intersection_covering`; transport a cover along a restriction → `J.pullback_stable`; refine to the target sieve → `J.superset_covering`; chain via an intermediate `bind`/`transitive` sieve → `J.bind_covering` / `J.transitive` with `Sieve.le_pullback_bind`; preimage extraction → `localPreimage` + `app_localPreimage`; carrier-function identification between `PresheafOfModules` hom and its `Ab`-presheaf image → `toPresheaf_map_app_apply` (`rfl`).

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — flat left-whiskering preserves the
sheafification localizer

The single non-formal ingredient of the `⊗`-invertibility associator
(`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_assoc_iso`, blueprint
`lem:flat_whisker_localizer`): for a sectionwise-*flat* presheaf of modules `F`
and a morphism `g` that is locally injective / locally surjective for the
Grothendieck topology `J` (i.e. lies in the sheafification localizer `J.W`), the
left-whiskered morphism `F ◁ g` is again locally injective / surjective. Local
surjectivity is pure right-exactness of `⊗` (no flatness); local injectivity is
where sectionwise flatness enters, via `Module.Flat.lTensor_exact`. All
ingredients are present in Mathlib — the route uses **no** `MonoidalClosed`
structure. -/

section FlatWhisker

open MonoidalCategory CategoryTheory.Presheaf

variable {J : CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology C}

/-- Sectionwise computation: the underlying additive map of `(F ◁ g).app X` is
`LinearMap.lTensor (F.obj X) (g.app X).hom`, acting on a simple tensor by
`a ⊗ₜ b ↦ a ⊗ₜ g(b)`. -/
lemma toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_tmul
    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (X : Cᵒᵖ)
    (a : F.obj X) (b : M.obj X) :
    (((toPresheaf _).map (F ◁ g)).app X).hom (a ⊗ₜ[(R.obj X)] b)
      = a ⊗ₜ[(R.obj X)] (g.app X).hom b := by
  rw [toPresheaf_map_app_apply]
  rw [PresheafOfModules.whiskerLeft_app]
  rw [ModuleCat.hom_whiskerLeft]
  simp [LinearMap.lTensor_tmul]

end FlatWhisker

end PresheafOfModules

/-- Sectionwise computation of the restriction map of a tensor presheaf on a
simple tensor: `(F ⊗ N).map i (a ⊗ₜ b) = F.map i a ⊗ₜ N.map i b`. -/

  erw [presheaf_map_apply_coe]
  exact PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul F N i a b

end FlatWhisker

erw [toPresheaf_map_app_apply]
  exact ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.whiskerLeft_apply _ _ a b

/-- **Local surjectivity is preserved by left-whiskering.** Right-exactness of
`⊗`: no flatness needed. Blueprint `lem:flat_whisker_localizer`, surjectivity
half. -/
lemma isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft
    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
    (hg : IsLocallySurjective J ((toPresheaf _).map g)) :
    IsLocallySurjective J ((toPresheaf _).map (F ◁ g)) := by
  constructor
  intro U s
  induction s using TensorProduct.induction_on with
  | zero =>
      have : Presheaf.imageSieve ((toPresheaf _).map (F ◁ g)) (0 : (F ⊗ N).obj (op U)) = ⊤ := by
        have h0 : (((toPresheaf _).map (F ◁ g)).app (op U)).hom (0 : (F ⊗ M).obj (op U)) = 0 := by
          simp
        rw [← h0]; exact Presheaf.imageSieve_app _ _
      rw [this]; exact J.top_mem U
  | tmul a b =>
      refine J.superset_covering ?_ (hg.imageSieve_mem b)
      intro V i hi
      obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := hi
      refine ⟨(F.map i.op).hom a ⊗ₜ[(R.obj (op V))] c, ?_⟩
      rw [toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_tmul]
      erw [presheaf_map_apply_coe, PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul]
      congr 1
      simpa only [toPresheaf_map_app_apply, presheaf_map_apply_coe] using hc
  | add s t hs ht =>
      refine J.superset_covering ?_ (J.intersection_covering hs ht)
      intro V i hi
      obtain ⟨⟨ds, hds⟩, ⟨dt, hdt⟩⟩ := hi
      refine ⟨ds + dt, ?_⟩
      rw [map_add, map_add, hds, hdt, ← map_add]

/-- **Local injectivity is preserved by flat left-whiskering.** This is where
sectionwise flatness of `F` enters: via `Module.Flat.lTensor_exact` on the
kernel exact sequence `ker(gₓ) ↪ M(X) →gₓ N(X)`, an element of `ker(F ◁ g)` is
a sum of simple tensors with kernel entries, each of which restricts to `0` on a
covering sieve (local injectivity of `g`). Blueprint `lem:flat_whisker_localizer`,
injectivity half. -/
lemma isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_flat
    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
    [∀ X, Module.Flat (R.obj X) (F.obj X)]
    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
    (hg : IsLocallyInjective J ((toPresheaf _).map g)) :
    IsLocallyInjective J ((toPresheaf _).map (F ◁ g)) := by
  sorry

end FlatWhisker

    (hg : IsLocallyInjective J g) :
    IsLocallyInjective J (F ◁ g) := by
  constructor
  intro X ξ η h
  -- View the sectionwise map of `g` as `R.obj X`-linear (the ring is commutative).
  let gl : ((M.obj X : ModuleCat _) : Type _) →ₗ[(R.obj X : CommRingCat)]
      ((N.obj X : ModuleCat _) : Type _) := (g.app X).hom
  -- `h` says `F ◁ g` agrees on `ξ, η`, i.e. `lTensor` kills `ξ - η`.
  have hδ : LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl (ξ - η) = 0 := by
    rw [map_sub, sub_eq_zero, ← toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_apply,
      ← toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_apply]
    exact h
  -- Flatness: `ker(F ⊗ gl) = range(F ⊗ ker.subtype)`, so `ξ - η` is a sum of simple
  -- tensors with kernel entries.
  have hex : Function.Exact
      (LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) (LinearMap.ker gl).subtype)
      (LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl) :=
    Module.Flat.lTensor_exact (F.obj X) (LinearMap.exact_subtype_ker_map gl)
  obtain ⟨ζ, hζ⟩ := (hex (ξ - η)).mp hδ
  -- Each simple tensor `a ⊗ k` with `gl k = 0` restricts to `0` on a covering sieve
  -- (local injectivity of `g`); induct on the witness `ζ`.
  have key : ∀ ζ : TensorProduct (R.obj X) (F.obj X) (LinearMap.ker gl),
      Presheaf.equalizerSieve (F := (toPresheaf _).obj (F ⊗ M)) (X := X)
        (LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X)
          (LinearMap.ker gl).subtype ζ) 0 ∈ J X.unop := by
    intro ζ
    induction ζ using TensorProduct.induction_on with
    | zero =>
        rw [map_zero, Presheaf.equalizerSieve_self_eq_top]
        exact J.top_mem _
    | tmul a kk =>
        rw [LinearMap.lTensor_tmul]
        have hk : ((toPresheaf _).map g).app X kk.1 = ((toPresheaf _).map g).app X 0 := by
          rw [map_zero]
          erw [toPresheaf_map_app_apply]
          exact (LinearMap.mem_ker.mp kk.2)
        refine J.superset_covering ?_ (hg.equalizerSieve_mem kk.1 0 hk)
        intro V f hf
        rw [Presheaf.equalizerSieve_apply] at hf ⊢
        rw [map_zero] at hf ⊢
        erw [presheaf_map_apply_coe, PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul]
        rw [show (N.map f.op).hom = (((toPresheaf _).obj M).map f.op).hom from rfl] at hf
        erw [presheaf_map_apply_coe] at hf
        rw [hf, TensorProduct.tmul_zero]
    | add ζ₁ ζ₂ h₁ h₂ =>
        rw [map_add]
        refine J.superset_covering ?_ (J.intersection_covering h₁ h₂)
        intro V f hf
        obtain ⟨hf1, hf2⟩ := hf
        rw [Presheaf.equalizerSieve_apply] at hf1 hf2 ⊢
        rw [map_zero] at hf1 hf2 ⊢
        rw [map_add, hf1, hf2, add_zero]
  -- Transport `equalizerSieve (ξ - η) 0 ∈ J` to `equalizerSieve ξ η ∈ J`.
  have hmain : Presheaf.equalizerSieve (F := (toPresheaf _).obj (F ⊗ M)) (X := X)
      (ξ - η) 0 ∈ J X.unop := hζ ▸ key ζ
  refine J.superset_covering ?_ hmain
  intro V f hf
  rw [Presheaf.equalizerSieve_apply] at hf ⊢
  rw [map_zero, map_sub, sub_eq_zero] at hf
  exact hf

end FlatWhisker
