content: I'll start by reading my role and task context files.

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi, OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	## Agent Roles
112	
113	### Plan Agent
114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
117	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
118	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
120	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
122	
123	### Prover Agent
124	
125	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
126	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
127	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
128	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
129	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
130	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
131	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
132	
133	### Subagents
134	
135	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
136	
137	### Review Agent
138	
139	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
140	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
141	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
142	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
143	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
144	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
145	
146	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
147	
148	These run automatically between phases:
149	
150	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
151	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
152	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
153

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs in your assigned `.lean` file.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas on your file.
9	3. Check the `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments (file-specific user hints).
10	4. **Read the relevant blueprint chapter before writing Lean code.** The chapter holds the mathematical proof sketch you must align with. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs. Push as far as possible.
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you can't fully close a sorry, leave your best attempt — commented-out steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` if needed), but your work must be visible for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to a bare `sorry` — that wastes the work.
13	7. Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
14	
15	**Write permissions**: only your assigned `.lean` file(s) and `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, blueprint chapters, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	`archon-protected.yaml` lists declarations with **frozen signatures**. You may fill their proof bodies but must not rename, re-type, reorder arguments, or weaken hypotheses. Only the mathematician edits protected signatures.
20	
21	## Avoid early termination
22	
23	- Don't abandon a proof prematurely. Many complex proofs run to thousands of lines.
24	- Difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave a `sorry`.
25	- Don't delegate to "the next iteration" or "another prover" if more effort could close it.
26	- Only modify the proof for your assigned task — leave unrelated proofs untouched.
27	- **Decompose**: break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure when available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each individually until the entire goal closes.
28	
29	## Task completion criteria
30	
31	Your task is complete ONLY when ALL of:
32	
33	1. Every `sorry` in scope is replaced with a complete proof.
34	2. Zero axioms introduced.
35	3. The file compiles cleanly.
36	
37	## Never weaken the type to dodge the proof
38	
39	When the substantive type is unattainable this iter, you have one honest move: leave `sorry` with the **intended type signature** so the type itself encodes the claim. You must NOT substitute a tautologically-true type to trivialize the body. Three patterns are banned:
40	
41	- **Reflexive-iso placeholder.** Replacing a non-trivial `X ≅ Y` (or `Nonempty (X ≅ Y)`) target with `Nonempty (X ≅ X) := ⟨Iso.refl _⟩`. The type now asserts nothing; the docstring's "iter-N placeholder" disclosure does not undo the structural lie, and the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference will resolve to this hollow declaration.
42	- **`Classical.choice` body around an explicit witness.** Writing `Classical.choice (α := X) ⟨witness⟩` to dissolve `Type` vs `Prop` friction — the witness is unreachable through any unfold, so the def is indistinguishable from `Classical.choice ⟨any-other-X⟩` downstream. Either use `Classical.choose` on the existential chain (preserves the spec), or `Nonempty.some`-with-witness; or ship a typed `sorry`.
43	- **Empty-content `proof_wanted`.** `proof_wanted` discards the declaration post-elaboration — the named decl never enters the environment, so the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference breaks. Use `theorem name : T := by sorry` with the intended signature instead.
44	
45	**Litmus test**: if you `unfold` your declaration, does it expose the named substantive content (Kähler differential module, the explicit iso, …) or does it stop at `Classical.choice` / `Iso.refl _` / nothing? If the latter, the body is structurally vacuous — ship the typed `sorry` instead.
46	
47	## When infrastructure is missing
48	
49	Do NOT report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up:
50	
51	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`): "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
52	2. **Try the alternative** — formalize whatever the informal agent suggests.
53	3. **If you still can't**: write the alternative sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md` and record in your task result what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found. "I couldn't prove X, but here's approach Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than "infrastructure missing".
54	
55	When stuck more generally: break into smaller subgoals, search Mathlib more thoroughly, prove missing helpers yourself, try alternative strategies, re-read the blueprint, use Web Search for published proofs.
56	
57	**Impossibility vs difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
58	
59	## Proof style
60	
61	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its body.
62	- Keep edits minimal; don't delete comments or change labels; don't add unrelated declarations.
63	- Helper lemmas you introduced may be modified if they turn out wrong.
64	- Add a concise comment above each helper lemma so reuse is easy.
65	- **`change` vs `show`** — `change` reshapes the goal up to defeq; `show` is purely display-level annotation. Using `show` where `change` is needed produces a linter warning. Default to `change` when in doubt.
66	
67	## Mathlib tags in PROGRESS.md
68	
69	The plan agent tags suggested lemmas:
70	
71	- `[verified]` — confirmed to exist.
72	- `[expected]` — guessed by naming convention. Quick `lean_local_search`; pivot if it doesn't exist.
73	- `[gap]` — verified NOT in Mathlib. Don't waste search time; formalize a workaround.
74	
75	## LSP MCP tools
76	
77	The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls** (full names like `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal` / `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`). The lean4 skill reference uses short names (`lean_goal`, `lean_local_search`, …) — same tools, NOT shell binaries.
78	
79	- Always invoke through your tool-call interface, the same way as `Read` or `Grep`.
80	- **Never** call `Bash` with `lean_goal …` etc. — there is no such shell command.
81	- First LSP action: `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your file. If it returns `success: false` the server is cold; retry once or run `lake build` once via Bash, then retry.
82	
83	## Search protocol
84	
85	Follow `references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`. Key priority:
86	
87	1. `lean_local_search` first.
88	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — describe the mathematical content, not just the name.
89	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only.
90	4. Never use shell `find` / `grep` to locate Mathlib theorems.
91	
92	## Tooling traps
93	
94	- **Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics**: in `lean_multi_attempt`, `diagnostics: []` can be misleading (line-scoping quirks attach errors to the enclosing `by` block). Check that `goals_after` is empty or has advanced.
95	- **Beware `lean_run_code` with imports**: standalone snippets can silently swallow elaboration errors when they contain imports. Rely on the actual file's `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.
96	
97	## Logging
98	
99	Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror your `.lean` path: `Algebra/WLocal.lean` → `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`):
100	
101	```markdown
102	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
103	
104	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
105	### Attempt 1
106	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
107	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance, not available
108	- **Dead end:** direct case split without IsLocalRing
109	
110	### Attempt 2
111	- **Approach:** Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
112	- **Result:** RESOLVED
113	- **Key insight:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective` bridges the gap
114	
115	## helper_bijective (line 78)
116	### Attempt 1
117	- **Approach:** Split into injectivity + surjectivity; prove injectivity
118	- **Result:** PARTIAL (branch closed) — injectivity closed; surjectivity remains
119	- **Next step:** `PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective` for the right branch
120	- **Lemmas found:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective`
121	```
122	
123	**Rules**: one section per theorem/lemma in your file. Each attempt records approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps. Log negative search results ("Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib"). The plan agent merges these into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md` next iter.
124	
125	**Read-only context**: read `task_pending.md` (prior attempts on your file) and `task_done.md` (when the problem resembles a completed one). Do not write to either.
126	
127	## End-of-session handoff
128	
129	Before stopping:
130	
131	1. Write `task_results/<your_file>.md` with: current result, lemmas discovered, concrete next step, dead-end warnings.
132	2. Save all changes; ensure the file compiles (use scoped `sorry` for stuck parts if needed).

6	import Mathlib
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.RelPicFunctor
8	
9	/-!
10	# The `Scheme.Modules.tensorObj` substrate (A.1.c.SubT)
11	
12	This file is the **A.1.c.SubT** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the
13	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It records the dedicated
14	substrate on which the abelian-group instance of the relative Picard quotient
15	`Pic^♯_{C/k}(T) := Pic(C ×_k T) / π_T^* Pic(T)` rests (the residual `sorry`
16	of `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean`).
17	
18	The mathematics is straightforward; the obstacle to formalisation is purely
19	infrastructural. The Lean construction of the abelian-group law
20	`[L] + [L'] := [L ⊗ L']` on isomorphism classes of line bundles requires three
21	ingredients on the Lean carrier:
22	
23	1. a binary tensor-product operation
24	   `⊗ : Scheme.Modules X × Scheme.Modules X → Scheme.Modules X`;
25	2. the structure sheaf `O_X` as a designated unit object for `⊗`;
26	3. an inverse operation on the full subcategory of invertible objects, i.e.
27	   the dual `L⁻¹ = Hom(L, O_X)` of an invertible sheaf.
28	
29	At Mathlib's pinned commit (`b80f227`), only a presheaf-level version of (1)
30	is available (`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj`); (2) and (3) are present
31	as scheme-level objects, but the binary operation in (1) that ties them
32	together at the `Scheme.Modules` level is missing, and there is no
33	`MonoidalCategory` instance on `Scheme.Modules X`. This file records the
34	project-side substrate that supplies (1) and consequently lifts (2) + (3)
35	into a monoidal-category structure on `Scheme.Modules X`.
36	
37	## Status (iter-202 Lane TS — file-skeleton scaffold)
38	
39	This file is the **iter-202 Lane TS** file-skeleton: each of the 4 pinned
40	declarations carries the *intended* substantive type signature (matching the
41	blueprint `\lean{...}` pin in `chapters/Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex`) with a
42	`sorry` body. The bodies are iter-203+ work: the `tensorObj` definition lifts
43	`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` through sheafification, and the consumer
44	`PicSharp.addCommGroup_via_tensorObj` then closes the residual `addCommGroup`
45	sorry of `RelPicFunctor.lean` L235.
46	
47	The 4 blueprint-pinned declarations are:
48	
49	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj` (def) — the substrate binary
50	   operation `⊗ : Scheme.Modules X × Scheme.Modules X → Scheme.Modules X`,
51	   lifting `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` on underlying presheaves
52	   composed with sheafification on the small Zariski site.
53	   Per blueprint `def:scheme_modules_tensorobj`.
54	
55	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_functoriality` (def) — the
56	   functorial action of `⊗` on morphisms: a pair `f : M ⟶ M'`, `g : N ⟶ N'`
57	   determines `f ⊗ g : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N'`.
58	   Per blueprint `lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality`.
59	
60	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.monoidalCategory` (instance) — the
61	   monoidal-category structure on `Scheme.Modules X` with tensor `⊗`, unit
62	   `O_X`, associator, unitors, and braiding inherited from
63	   `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal` under sheafification.
64	   Per blueprint `thm:scheme_modules_monoidal`.
65	
66	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp.addCommGroup_via_tensorObj` (def) — the
67	   `AddCommGroup` structure on the relative Picard quotient
68	   `Quotient (RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup πC πT)`, built via the
69	   `tensorObj` substrate. This is the iter-204+ closure target for the Lane RPF
70	   L235/L266-269 `addCommGroup` residual sorry.
71	   Per blueprint `thm:rel_pic_addcommgroup_via_tensorobj`.
72	
73	Plus (PUSH-BEYOND) the supporting helper lemmas of the lift section
74	(`lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial`,
75	`lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible`, `lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct`,
76	`lem:pullback_compatible_with_tensorobj`).
77	
78	## References
79	
80	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex` (740 LOC,
81	4 pins). Source: [Kleiman], "The Picard scheme", §2 (FGA Explained Ch.9 §9.2),
82	Defs. `df:aPf` + `df:Pfs`; Stacks tags 01CR (Picard group), 03DM (relative
83	tensor product of `O_X`-modules). Mathlib module
84	`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.PresheafOfModules`
85	(`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj`).
86	-/
87	
88	set_option autoImplicit false
89	
90	universe u
91	
92	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory
93	
94	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — `restrictScalars` is lax monoidal
95	
96	The presheaf-of-modules restriction-of-scalars functor along a morphism of
97	presheaves of *commutative* rings is lax monoidal. Mathlib ships the sectionwise
98	fact `ModuleCat.restrictScalars f` is `LaxMonoidal`
99	(`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Monoidal.Adjunction`); here we lift it to
100	the presheaf level through the sectionwise presheaf monoidal structure
101	(`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal`). This is the sole project-side ingredient feeding
102	the oplax comparison `δ` of `pullback φ` (the mate of `pushforward φ`) used to
103	close `tensorObj_restrict_iso`. Per blueprint `lem:restrictscalars_laxmonoidal`. -/
104	
105	namespace PresheafOfModules
106	
107	universe v'
108	
109	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v'} C] {R S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
110	
111	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
112	/-- The lax-monoidal unit `ε` of `restrictScalars α`, assembled sectionwise from
113	`ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X)`'s lax-monoidal unit. -/
114	noncomputable def restrictScalarsLaxε
115	    (α : R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⟶ S ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :
116	    (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))) ⟶
117	      (restrictScalars α).obj (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (S ⋙ forget₂ _ _))) where
118	  app X := Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X).hom)
119	  naturality {X Y} f := by
120	    ext r
121	    dsimp
122	    erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_one, ModuleCat.restrictScalars_η,
123	      ModuleCat.restrictScalars_η]
124	    simp only [map_one]
125	    erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_one]
126	
127	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
128	/-- The lax-monoidal tensorator `μ` of `restrictScalars α`, assembled sectionwise
129	from `ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X)`'s lax-monoidal tensorator. -/
130	noncomputable def restrictScalarsLaxμ
131	    (α : R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⟶ S ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
132	    (M₁ M₂ : PresheafOfModules.{u} (S ⋙ forget₂ _ _)) :
133	    (restrictScalars α).obj M₁ ⊗ (restrictScalars α).obj M₂ ⟶
134	      (restrictScalars α).obj (M₁ ⊗ M₂) where
135	  app X := by
136	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X).hom) (M₁.obj X) (M₂.obj X)
137	  naturality {X Y} f := by
138	    refine ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (fun m₁ m₂ ↦ ?_)
139	    dsimp
140	    erw [PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul, ModuleCat.restrictScalars_μ_tmul,
141	      ModuleCat.restrictScalars_μ_tmul, PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul]
142	    rfl
143	
144	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
145	/-- **`restrictScalars α` is lax monoidal** for a morphism `α` of presheaves of
146	commutative rings. Project-local lift of `ModuleCat.instLaxMonoidalRestrictScalars`. -/
147	noncomputable instance restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal
148	    (α : R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⟶ S ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :
149	    (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).LaxMonoidal where
150	  ε := restrictScalarsLaxε α
151	  μ M₁ M₂ := restrictScalarsLaxμ α M₁ M₂
152	  μ_natural_left := by
153	    intro X Y f X'
154	    ext1 Z
155	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ_natural_left (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
156	      (f.app Z) (X'.obj Z)
157	  μ_natural_right := by
158	    intro X Y X' f
159	    ext1 Z
160	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ_natural_right (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
161	      (X'.obj Z) (f.app Z)
162	  associativity := by
163	    intro M N P
164	    ext1 Z
165	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.associativity (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
166	      (M.obj Z) (N.obj Z) (P.obj Z)
167	  left_unitality := by
168	    intro M
169	    ext1 Z
170	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.left_unitality (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
171	      (M.obj Z)
172	  right_unitality := by
173	    intro M
174	    ext1 Z
175	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.right_unitality (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
176	      (M.obj Z)
177	
178	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — flat left-whiskering preserves the
179	sheafification localizer
180	
181	The single non-formal ingredient of the `⊗`-invertibility associator
182	(`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_assoc_iso`, blueprint
183	`lem:flat_whisker_localizer`): for a sectionwise-*flat* presheaf of modules `F`
184	and a morphism `g` that is locally injective / locally surjective for the
185	Grothendieck topology `J` (i.e. lies in the sheafification localizer `J.W`), the
186	left-whiskered morphism `F ◁ g` is again locally injective / surjective. Local
187	surjectivity is pure right-exactness of `⊗` (no flatness); local injectivity is
188	where sectionwise flatness enters, via `Module.Flat.lTensor_exact`. All
189	ingredients are present in Mathlib — the route uses **no** `MonoidalClosed`
190	structure. -/
191	
192	section FlatWhisker
193	
194	open MonoidalCategory CategoryTheory.Presheaf
195	
196	variable {J : CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology C}
197	
198	/-- Sectionwise computation: the underlying additive map of `(F ◁ g).app X` is
199	`LinearMap.lTensor (F.obj X) (g.app X).hom`, acting on a simple tensor by
200	`a ⊗ₜ b ↦ a ⊗ₜ g(b)`. -/
201	lemma toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_tmul
202	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
203	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (X : Cᵒᵖ)
204	    (a : F.obj X) (b : M.obj X) :
205	    (((toPresheaf _).map (F ◁ g)).app X).hom (a ⊗ₜ[(R.obj X)] b)
206	      = a ⊗ₜ[(R.obj X)] (g.app X).hom b := by
207	  erw [toPresheaf_map_app_apply]
208	  exact ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.whiskerLeft_apply _ _ a b
209	
210	/-- The underlying additive map of `(F ◁ g).app X` is `LinearMap.lTensor`. -/
211	lemma toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_apply
212	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
213	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (X : Cᵒᵖ)
214	    (z : (F ⊗ M).obj X) :
215	    (((toPresheaf _).map (F ◁ g)).app X).hom z
216	      = LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) (g.app X).hom z := by
217	  erw [toPresheaf_map_app_apply, PresheafOfModules.whiskerLeft_app]
218	
219	/-- **Local surjectivity is preserved by left-whiskering.** Right-exactness of
220	`⊗`: no flatness needed. Blueprint `lem:flat_whisker_localizer`, surjectivity
221	half. -/
222	lemma isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft
223	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
224	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
225	    (hg : IsLocallySurjective J g) :
226	    IsLocallySurjective J (F ◁ g) := by
227	  constructor
228	  intro U s
229	  induction s using TensorProduct.induction_on with
230	  | zero =>
231	      refine J.superset_covering ?_ (J.top_mem U)
232	      intro V i _
233	      exact ⟨0, by rw [map_zero]; exact (map_zero _).symm⟩
234	  | tmul a b =>
235	      refine J.superset_covering ?_ (hg.imageSieve_mem b)
236	      intro V i hi
237	      obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := hi
238	      refine ⟨(F.map i.op).hom a ⊗ₜ[(R.obj (Opposite.op V))] c, ?_⟩
239	      rw [toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_tmul]
240	      erw [presheaf_map_apply_coe, PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul]
241	      congr 1
242	  | add s t hs ht =>
243	      refine J.superset_covering ?_ (J.intersection_covering hs ht)
244	      intro V i hi
245	      obtain ⟨⟨ds, hds⟩, ⟨dt, hdt⟩⟩ := hi
246	      refine ⟨ds + dt, ?_⟩
247	      rw [map_add, hds, hdt]; exact (map_add _ s t).symm
248	
249	/-- **Local injectivity is preserved by flat left-whiskering.** This is where
250	sectionwise flatness of `F` enters: via `Module.Flat.lTensor_exact` on the
251	kernel exact sequence `ker(gₓ) ↪ M(X) →gₓ N(X)`, an element of `ker(F ◁ g)` is
252	a sum of simple tensors with kernel entries, each of which restricts to `0` on a
253	covering sieve (local injectivity of `g`). Blueprint `lem:flat_whisker_localizer`,
254	injectivity half. -/
255	lemma isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_flat
256	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
257	    [∀ X, Module.Flat (R.obj X) (F.obj X)]
258	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
259	    (hg : IsLocallyInjective J g) :
260	    IsLocallyInjective J (F ◁ g) := by
261	  constructor
262	  intro X ξ η h
263	  -- View the sectionwise map of `g` as `R.obj X`-linear (the ring is commutative).
264	  let gl : ((M.obj X : ModuleCat _) : Type _) →ₗ[(R.obj X : CommRingCat)]
265	      ((N.obj X : ModuleCat _) : Type _) := (g.app X).hom
266	  -- `h` says `F ◁ g` agrees on `ξ, η`, i.e. `lTensor` kills `ξ - η`.
267	  have hδ : LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl (ξ - η) = 0 := by
268	    have heq : LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl ξ
269	        = LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl η := by
270	      rw [← toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_apply, ← toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_apply]; exact h
271	    exact (map_sub (LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl) ξ η).trans
272	      (sub_eq_zero.mpr heq)
273	  -- Flatness: `ker(F ⊗ gl) = range(F ⊗ ker.subtype)`, so `ξ - η` is a sum of simple
274	  -- tensors with kernel entries.
275	  have hex : Function.Exact
276	      (LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) (LinearMap.ker gl).subtype)
277	      (LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl) :=
278	    Module.Flat.lTensor_exact (F.obj X) (LinearMap.exact_subtype_ker_map gl)
279	  obtain ⟨ζ, hζ⟩ := (hex (ξ - η)).mp hδ
280	  -- Each simple tensor `a ⊗ k` with `gl k = 0` restricts to `0` on a covering sieve
281	  -- (local injectivity of `g`); induct on the witness `ζ`.
282	  have key : ∀ ζ : TensorProduct (R.obj X) (F.obj X) (LinearMap.ker gl),
283	      Presheaf.equalizerSieve (F := (toPresheaf _).obj (F ⊗ M)) (X := X)
284	        (LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X)
285	          (LinearMap.ker gl).subtype ζ) 0 ∈ J X.unop := by
286	    intro ζ
287	    induction ζ using TensorProduct.induction_on with
288	    | zero =>
289	        rw [map_zero]
290	        exact J.superset_covering (Presheaf.equalizerSieve_self_eq_top _).ge (J.top_mem _)
291	    | tmul a kk =>
292	        rw [LinearMap.lTensor_tmul]
293	        have hk : ((toPresheaf _).map g).app X kk.1
294	            = ((toPresheaf _).map g).app X (0 : ((toPresheaf _).obj M).obj X) := by
295	          rw [map_zero]
296	          erw [toPresheaf_map_app_apply]
297	          exact kk.2
298	        refine J.superset_covering ?_ (hg.equalizerSieve_mem kk.1 0 hk)
299	        intro V f hf
300	        rw [Presheaf.equalizerSieve_apply] at hf ⊢
301	        rw [map_zero] at hf ⊢
302	        erw [presheaf_map_apply_coe, PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul]
303	        erw [presheaf_map_apply_coe] at hf
304	        rw [Submodule.subtype_apply, hf]
305	        simp
306	    | add ζ₁ ζ₂ h₁ h₂ =>
307	        rw [map_add]
308	        refine J.superset_covering ?_ (J.intersection_covering h₁ h₂)
309	        intro V f hf
310	        obtain ⟨hf1, hf2⟩ := hf
311	        rw [Presheaf.equalizerSieve_apply] at hf1 hf2 ⊢
312	        rw [map_zero] at hf1 hf2 ⊢
313	        exact (map_add _ _ _).trans (by rw [hf1, hf2, add_zero])
314	  -- Transport `equalizerSieve (ξ - η) 0 ∈ J` to `equalizerSieve ξ η ∈ J`.
315	  have hmain : Presheaf.equalizerSieve (F := (toPresheaf _).obj (F ⊗ M)) (X := X)
316	      (ξ - η) 0 ∈ J X.unop := hζ ▸ key ζ
317	  refine J.superset_covering ?_ hmain
318	  intro V f hf
319	  rw [Presheaf.equalizerSieve_apply] at hf ⊢
320	  rw [map_zero, map_sub, sub_eq_zero] at hf
321	  exact hf
322	
323	end FlatWhisker
324	
325	end PresheafOfModules
326	
327	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
328	
329	namespace Scheme
330	
331	namespace Modules
332	
333	/-! ## §1. The substrate tensor-product operation -/
334	
335	/-- **The substrate operation `⊗` on `Scheme.Modules X`.**
336	
337	For a scheme `X` and `M, N : X.Modules`, the tensor product
338	`M ⊗_X N : X.Modules` is the sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules tensor
339	product `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` of the underlying presheaves of
340	`M` and `N` (affine-locally `(M ⊗_X N)(Spec A) = M(Spec A) ⊗_A N(Spec A)`).
341	
342	Per blueprint `def:scheme_modules_tensorobj`. iter-202 Lane TS scaffold: the
343	body is a typed `sorry`; the iter-203+ body lifts
344	`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` through the sheafification functor on
345	the small Zariski site of `X`. -/
346	noncomputable def tensorObj {X : Scheme.{u}} (M N : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
347	  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
348	      (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) M.val N.val) :
349	    SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf)
350	
351	/-- **Functoriality of `⊗_X`.**
352	
353	A pair of morphisms `f : M ⟶ M'` and `g : N ⟶ N'` in `X.Modules` determines a
354	morphism `f ⊗ g : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N'`, compatible with identities
355	and composition; the assignment `(M, N) ↦ tensorObj M N` thereby extends to a
356	bifunctor `X.Modules × X.Modules ⥤ X.Modules` natural in both arguments.
357	
358	Per blueprint `lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality`. iter-202 Lane TS
359	scaffold: the body is a typed `sorry`; the iter-203+ body inherits the
360	morphism action from `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` under
361	sheafification. -/
362	noncomputable def tensorObj_functoriality {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
363	    (f : M ⟶ M') (g : N ⟶ N') : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N' :=
364	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
365	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorHom
366	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) f.val g.val)
367	
368	/-! ## §2. (Off the critical path) the full monoidal-category structure
369	
370	iter-206 PIVOT: the full `MonoidalCategory (X.Modules)` instance is
371	**deliberately not built**. Per blueprint `rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path`,
372	the relative Picard group law is a structure on *isomorphism classes* of line
373	bundles — every group axiom is a `Nonempty (… ≅ …)` proposition, so no monoidal
374	coherence (pentagon/triangle/hexagon) and no `MonoidalClosed` structure is ever
375	consumed. The earlier `monoidalCategory := sorry` instance (and the two
376	localization-transport supplements `isMonoidal_W_of_whiskerLeft`,
377	`monoidalCategoryOfIsMonoidalW`) routed through the verified-absent
378	`MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)` wall; they are removed here. The group
379	law is built directly on the line-bundle subcategory from the four
380	existence-of-iso lemmas below, mirroring Mathlib's `CommRing.Pic`. -/
381	
382	/-! ## §3. The lift through `LineBundle.OnProduct` (PUSH-BEYOND supporting lemmas)
383	
384	The following helper lemmas record the lift of the substrate to the
385	locally-trivial subcategory used by the relative Picard consumer. They are not
386	`\lean{...}`-pinned in the blueprint (their statements are descriptive); the
387	typed signatures here scaffold the iter-203+ bodies. -/
388	
389	/-- **The substrate operation respects isomorphisms in both arguments.**
390	
391	A pair of isomorphisms `e : M ≅ M'` and `e' : N ≅ N'` in `X.Modules` induces an
392	isomorphism `tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj M' N'`, obtained by sheafifying the
393	tensor product (in the presheaf-of-modules monoidal category) of the underlying
394	presheaf isomorphisms `e.val`, `e'.val`. Its `hom` is
395	`tensorObj_functoriality e.hom e'.hom`. This is the reusable functor-of-isos
396	ingredient feeding `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial`. -/
397	noncomputable def tensorObjIsoOfIso {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
398	    (e : M ≅ M') (e' : N ≅ N') : tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj M' N' :=
399	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
400	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorIso
401	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
402	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e)
403	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e'))
404	
405	/-- **The substrate tensor of the structure sheaf with itself is the structure
406	sheaf.**
407	
408	`tensorObj 𝒪_X 𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_X`, where `𝒪_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf` is the
409	designated unit object. Built from the presheaf-level left unitor
410	`λ_ (𝟙_)` (the unit of the `PresheafOfModules` monoidal category is exactly
411	`SheafOfModules.unit.val`) under sheafification, composed with the
412	sheafification-adjunction counit isomorphism on the (already-sheaf) unit. -/
413	noncomputable def tensorObj_unit_iso {X : Scheme.{u}} :
414	    tensorObj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
415	      ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf :=
416	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
417	      (λ_ (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))) ≪≫
418	    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app
419	      (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
420	
421	/-- **Refining a trivialisation to a smaller open.** If `M` is trivialised on an
422	open `U` (`M.restrict U.ι ≅ 𝒪_U`), it is trivialised on every open `W ≤ U`.
423	
424	The chart-chase is identical in spirit to `LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial.pullback`:
425	factor `W.ι = (X.homOfLE hWU) ≫ U.ι`, transport through `restrictFunctorCongr`
426	and `restrictFunctorComp` to identify `M.restrict W.ι` with
427	`(M.restrict U.ι).restrict (X.homOfLE hWU)`, restrict the given trivialisation
428	`e` along that open immersion, and identify the restricted unit with the unit
429	via `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` + `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` (an isomorphism
430	because the open immersion's `Opens.map` is `Final`). -/
431	noncomputable def restrictIsoUnitOfLE {X : Scheme.{u}} {M : X.Modules} {U W : X.Opens}
432	    (hWU : W ≤ U)
433	    (e : M.restrict U.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (U : Scheme).ringCatSheaf) :
434	    M.restrict W.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (W : Scheme).ringCatSheaf := by
435	  have hWU' : W ≤ (𝟙 X) ⁻¹ᵁ U := hWU
436	  set j : (W : Scheme) ⟶ (U : Scheme) := Scheme.Hom.resLE (𝟙 X) U W hWU' with hj
437	  have hjι : j ≫ U.ι = W.ι := by rw [hj, Scheme.Hom.resLE_comp_ι, Category.comp_id]
438	  haveI : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map j.base).Final :=
439	    CategoryTheory.final_of_representablyFlat _
440	  -- M.restrict W.ι ≅ (pullback W.ι).obj M
441	  refine (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback W.ι).app M ≪≫ ?_
442	  -- ≅ (pullback (j ≫ U.ι)).obj M
443	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr hjι.symm).app M ≪≫ ?_
444	  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj ((pullback U.ι).obj M)
445	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp j U.ι).symm.app M ≪≫ ?_
446	  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj (M.restrict U.ι)
447	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullback j).mapIso
448	    ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback U.ι).symm.app M) ≪≫ ?_
449	  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj 𝒪_U
450	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullback j).mapIso e ≪≫ ?_
451	  -- ≅ 𝒪_W
452	  haveI hI : IsIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit j.toRingCatSheafHom) := inferInstance
453	  exact @asIso _ _ _ _ _ hI
454	
455	/-- **Substrate tensor commutes with restriction along an open immersion.**
456	
457	For an open immersion `f : Y ⟶ X` and `M N : X.Modules`,
458	`(tensorObj M N).restrict f ≅ tensorObj (M.restrict f) (N.restrict f)`.
459	
460	This is the single missing-infrastructure ingredient of `A.1.c.SubT`. It says
461	the substrate `⊗` (sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules tensor) commutes
462	with the restriction functor `restrictFunctor f ≅ pullback f` along an open
463	immersion. Mathematically it factors as: (i) the presheaf-of-modules tensor
464	commutes with the pullback of presheaves (sectionwise this is the
465	extension-of-scalars identity
466	`B ⊗_A (P ⊗_A Q) ≅ (B ⊗_A P) ⊗_B (B ⊗_A Q)` for `A → B`, Stacks 03DM); and
467	(ii) sheafification commutes with pullback along an open immersion (the
468	restriction is exact and the small-Zariski sheafification is local). Neither is
469	in Mathlib at the `SheafOfModules` level (there is no monoidal structure on
470	`SheafOfModules`, hence no strong-monoidal pullback). A genuine construction
471	requires either the `Localization.Monoidal` transport of the monoidal structure
472	through `PresheafOfModules.sheafification.IsLocalization` together with
473	`W.IsMonoidal` for `W := (J.W).inverseImage (toPresheaf _)` (an instance NOT
474	present in Mathlib and the real residual obstacle), or a direct sectionwise
475	construction. Left as a named typed `sorry` feeding `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial`;
476	see the task result for the precise missing instance statement. -/
477	noncomputable def tensorObj_restrict_iso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X)
478	    [IsOpenImmersion f] (M N : X.Modules) :
479	    (tensorObj M N).restrict f ≅ tensorObj (M.restrict f) (N.restrict f) := by
480	  -- Step 1. Reduce `restrict` to `pullback` along the open immersion `f`
481	  -- (`restrictFunctorIsoPullback`, Mathlib).
482	  refine (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback f).app (tensorObj M N) ≪≫ ?_
483	  -- Step 2. **Sheafification commutes with pullback.** `tensorObj M N` is, by
484	  -- definition, `sheafification.obj (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj
485	  -- M.val N.val)`, so the genuine Mathlib lemma
486	  -- `SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback`
487	  -- (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.PullbackContinuous`,
488	  -- `sheafification ⋙ pullback φ ≅ PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom ⋙
489	  -- sheafification`) moves the pullback *inside* the sheafification. This
490	  -- discharges half (ii) of the original obstruction (sheafification commuting
491	  -- with pullback). After it the goal is the purely **presheaf-level** residual
492	  -- `sheafify ((PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom).obj (M.val ⊗ N.val))
493	  --    ≅ (M.restrict f).tensorObj (N.restrict f)`.
494	  refine (SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback f.toRingCatSheafHom).app
495	      (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M.val N.val) ≪≫ ?_
496	  -- Step 3. **Strip the outer sheafification.** Both sides are
497	  -- `PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.obj)` applied to a
498	  -- presheaf-of-modules: the LHS to `(pullback φ.hom).obj (M.val ⊗ₚ N.val)`, and
499	  -- the RHS `(M.restrict f).tensorObj (N.restrict f)` *by definition* to
500	  -- `(M.restrict f).val ⊗ₚ (N.restrict f).val`. So it suffices to give the
501	  -- comparison at the PRESHEAF level and sheafify it. This is a genuine reduction
502	  -- step (verified: the goal below has no sheafification).
503	  refine (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.obj)).mapIso ?_
504	  -- Step 4 (RESIDUAL — CORRECTED iter-208 analysis). The remaining presheaf goal is
505	  --   `(PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom).obj (M.val ⊗ₚ N.val)
506	  --      ≅ (M.restrict f).val ⊗ₚ (N.restrict f).val`
507	  -- where `φ.hom = (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom` and `⊗ₚ =

570	  exact tensorObj_restrict_iso W.ι M N ≪≫
571	    tensorObjIsoOfIso (restrictIsoUnitOfLE hWU eM) (restrictIsoUnitOfLE hWU' eN) ≪≫

586	    (hL : LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial L) :

13	Scope the `⊗_X` associator `(M⊗N)⊗P ≅ M⊗(N⊗P)` to INVERTIBLE (locally-free rank-1,
14	hence flat) objects only — all the relative-Picard group law consumes. Under that

16	`MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)`? (2) which of three realizations —
17	(1) local-trivialization gluing, (2) flat-exactness whiskerLeft, (3) `J.W.IsMonoidal`
18	on the flat subcategory — should we formalize? (3) does realization (1) genuinely

23	  sheafification(PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M.val N.val)`.

37	## Decisions identified
38	
39	### Decision 1: which realization of the invertible-scoped associator
40	
41	The associator nests an inner sheafification inside the presheaf tensor on both sides:
42	`(M⊗N)⊗P = a( a(M.val ⊗ᵖ N.val).val ⊗ᵖ P.val )` where `a = PresheafOfModules.sheafification`.
43	Transporting the presheaf associator `α` needs, besides `a.mapIso(α)`, the
44	**absorption iso** `a(a(A).val ⊗ᵖ B) ≅ a(A ⊗ᵖ B)` (and its mirror) = whiskering-stability
45	of the sheafification localizer `J.W`: `J.W g → J.W (B ◁ g)`.
46	
47	- **Mathlib idiom**. Mathlib's general whiskering-stability of a sheafification localizer
48	  is `CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.W.whiskerLeft`
49	  (`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Monoidal`), whose signature **requires
50	  `[MonoidalClosed A]`** plus enriched-hom instances on `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A`. For invertibility/Picard,
51	  Mathlib instead scopes to a *predicate on objects* — `Module.Invertible R M`
52	  (`Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup`) — and derives `Module.Invertible.instFinite`,
53	  `Module.Invertible.instProjective`, hence `Module.Flat.of_projective` (all present), so the
54	  invertible/line-bundle objects are FLAT, and flat-exactness replaces the closed structure.
55	- **Realization (1) — local-trivialization gluing**. To build `(M⊗N)⊗P ≅ M⊗(N⊗P)` by gluing
56	  structure-sheaf associators on a trivialising cover, you must first identify
57	  `((M⊗N)⊗P).restrict W` with the affine triple tensor — i.e. invoke
58	  `tensorObj_restrict_iso`. That lemma is a `sorry`; its residual (H1/H2, file L357–398) is
59	  the strong-monoidal-pushforward / `restrictScalars`-along-ring-iso infrastructure,
60	  **absent from Mathlib and flatness-INDEPENDENT** (H2 is about the open-immersion ring iso
61	  `f.appIso`, not about M,N,P). So scoping M,N,P to invertible does **not** lighten it.
62	  The cited precedent `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial` proves only EXISTENCE of local
63	  trivialisations yet still routes through the same `sorry` — `lean_verify` confirms
64	  `sorryAx` in its axioms. **Gap: divergent-and-wrong** (renamed wall).
65	- **Realization (3) — `J.W.IsMonoidal` on the flat subcategory**. Gating Mathlib's
66	  `CategoryTheory.Sheaf.monoidalCategory` needs a `MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal J.W` instance,
67	  whose `whiskerLeft` field is exactly `GrothendieckTopology.W.whiskerLeft` — **`MonoidalClosed`-
68	  gated**. Even restricted to flat objects, instantiating Mathlib's bundled `W.IsMonoidal`
69	  pulls in the closed structure for ALL objects. **Gap: divergent-with-cost** (the wall, packaged).
70	- **Realization (2) — flat-exactness whiskerLeft (RECOMMENDED)**. Do NOT instantiate the
71	  bundled `W.IsMonoidal`. Prove the two specific absorption-iso *instances* directly:
72	  `a(η_{M⊗N} ◁ P)` is iso (P invertible⇒flat) and the mirror `a(M ▷ η_{N⊗P})` is iso
73	  (M invertible⇒flat), where `η = toSheafify` is the sheafification unit (in `J.W` by
74	  `instIsLocallyInjectiveToSheafify`). The bridge `J.W g → J.W (P ◁ g)` for FLAT P is
75	  elementary: via the `J.WEqualsLocallyBijective` characterisation (`Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective`
76	  + `IsLocallySurjective`), `P ◁ g` stays locally injective by
77	  `Module.Flat.lTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap` (sectionwise) and locally surjective by
78	  right-exactness of `⊗`. **All named ingredients present in Mathlib.** Assemble:
79	  `(M⊗N)⊗P ≅[a(η◁P)] a((M⊗ᵖN)⊗ᵖP) ≅[a.mapIso α] a(M⊗ᵖ(N⊗ᵖP)) ≅[a(M▷η)] M⊗(N⊗P)`.
80	  **Gap: divergent-equivalent → ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB** (this is the `Module.Invertible`+flat idiom).
81	- **Verdict**: realization (2) — **ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB**; realizations (1) and (3) — reject
82	  (both bottom out in absent Mathlib: (1) in `tensorObj_restrict_iso`'s H1/H2, (3) in
83	  `MonoidalClosed`).
84	
85	### Decision 2: re-scope `lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso` from arbitrary `M,N,P` to invertible/flat
86	
87	- **Mathlib idiom**. `Module.Invertible` is a `Prop` object-predicate; the Picard arithmetic
88	  (`CommRing.Pic.mk_tensor`, `mk_dual`, …) are existence-of-iso facts scoped to invertibles,
89	  never coherence on all modules. The flatness the bridge "needs" is FREE on invertibles
90	  (`Module.Invertible.instProjective` → `Module.Flat.of_projective`).
91	- **Project's path**. Blueprint L536 states the associator for *arbitrary* `M,N,P` with "no
92	  line-bundle, local-freeness or flatness hypothesis required" — which is precisely what forces
93	  the arbitrary-`F` whiskering-stability = `MonoidalClosed` wall (its own `% NOTE` L549–562).
94	- **Gap**: divergent-with-cost. The arbitrary statement is unbuildable at the pinned commit; the
95	  consumer `lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup`/`thm:rel_pic_addcommgroup_via_tensorobj` only ever
96	  associates INVERTIBLE objects.
97	- **Verdict**: **ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB** — re-scope the hypothesis to `IsInvertible M/N/P` (or a
98	  `Module.Flat`-style predicate); drop the "no flatness required" clause.
99	
100	## Recommendation
101	
102	Re-scope `lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso` to invertible `M,N,P` and formalize it via **realization (2)**,
103	the flat-exactness whiskerLeft. The associator is built as a 3-step composite —
104	`a(η ◁ P)` absorption (P flat), `a.mapIso` of the presheaf associator `α`, mirror `a(M ▷ η)`
105	absorption (M flat) — whose only non-`mapIso` content is the bridge lemma
106	`J.W g → J.W (P ◁ g)` for flat `P`, assembled from `Module.Flat.lTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap`
107	+ right-exactness through the `J.WEqualsLocallyBijective` / `IsLocallyInjective`+`IsLocallySurjective`
108	API (all present Mathlib). This genuinely avoids `MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)`.
109	
110	Do **NOT** take realization (1): it reduces to the sorry'd `tensorObj_restrict_iso`, whose
111	residual (H1 presheaf pushforward-adjunction, H2 strong-monoidal `restrictScalars`-along-ring-iso)
112	is absent from Mathlib AND flatness-independent, so the invertible scope does not unlock it — the
113	"already proven" precedent `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial` is itself `sorryAx`-tainted via the same
114	hole. Do **NOT** take realization (3): instantiating Mathlib's bundled `W.IsMonoidal` is gated on
115	`GrothendieckTopology.W.whiskerLeft`'s `[MonoidalClosed A]` requirement — the wall, renamed.
116	
117	**Reversal signal: NOT triggered globally.** Realization (2) is a genuine present-Mathlib escape,
